title: 4005: creation
content: The created order, established as a sovereign decision on the part of God. The creation is dependent upon and under the authority of its Creator. Scripture affirms the role of both Jesus Christ and the Holy Spirit in the work of creation.

title: 4006: creation, origin of
content:
The free act of God based on his own wisdom and power, forming the whole natural order by his word.

God is Creator of the universe and everything in it

NEH 9:6 See also GEN 1:1, GEN 2:1; PSA 24:1–24:2; PRO 8:22–8:31; ECC 3:11, ECC 11:5; ISA 66:1–66:2; JER 10:16, JER 51:19; JHN 1:3; ACT 4:24, ACT 7:50, ACT 14:15, ACT 17:24

Light and darkness

GEN 1:3–1:5 See also ISA 45:7

The heavens

PSA 136:5 See also GEN 1:6–1:8; PSA 33:6, PSA 96:5; AMO 5:8; REV 14:7

The land

GEN 1:9–1:10 See also PSA 95:5, PSA 102:25, PSA 104:5–104:6; GEN 1:14; EXO 20:11; PSA 74:17, PSA 118:24

The sea and sea creatures

PSA 95:5 See also GEN 1:20–1:22

Birds

GEN 1:20–1:22

Land animals

GEN 1:24–1:25 See also JOB 40:15

The human race

GEN 1:26–1:27 Genesis witnesses to order and progression in God's creative activity. The creation of Adam and Eve is not merely the end point, but the climax to the sequence. See also GEN 2:7, GEN 2:21–2:22, GEN 5:1–5:2; DEU 32:6; PSA 100:3, PSA 139:14; PRO 14:31, PRO 17:5, PRO 22:2; ECC 12:1, ECC 12:7

The role of Jesus Christ in creation

JHN 1:3 See also JHN 1:10; 1CO 8:6; COL 1:16; HEB 1:2

The role of the Spirit in creation

JOB 33:4 See also GEN 1:2; PSA 104:30; ISA 40:12–40:13

The creation of the world

GEN 1:1; HEB 11:3 See also PSA 33:9, PSA 148:5; ROM 4:17

The original creation was complete and perfect

Its completion

GEN 2:1 See also GEN 2:2–2:3; EXO 20:11; ISA 48:12–48:13; HEB 4:3

Its perfection

GEN 1:31 See also GEN 1:10, GEN 1:12, GEN 1:18, GEN 1:21, GEN 1:25; ECC 7:29; ISA 45:18; 1TI 4:4

The witness of creation

Creation should cause people to worship God

REV 4:11 See also NEH 9:6; PSA 19:1–19:4, PSA 95:6, PSA 148:5, PSA 148:13; ROM 1:20 God's power and deity is witnessed through the created order.

Sinners refuse the witness of creation

ROM 1:18–1:20

Believers receive the witness of creation by faith

HEB 11:3 See also GEN 6:6

title: 4007: creation, and God
content:
The natural world is sustained by God and speaks of God.

God sustains the creation

He upholds the natural order

HEB 1:3 See also JOB 38:33–38:37; PSA 104:1–104:35, PSA 135:6–135:7, PSA 145:16–145:17; MAT 10:29–10:30; COL 1:17

He sustains humanity

ACT 17:28 See also JOB 33:4; PSA 36:6; DAN 4:34–4:35; 1CO 8:6

Creation is upheld for the good of humanity

GEN 8:22 See also GEN 9:12–9:16; MAT 6:11

God's sustaining power reserves the world for judgment

2PE 3:7 See also 2PE 3:9–3:12 Time is being given for sinners to repent.

God has given humanity responsibility to preserve creation

GEN 1:28 See also GEN 2:15, GEN 9:1–9:3; PSA 8:6–8:8, PSA 115:16; HEB 2:8; JAS 3:7

Creation is spoilt by sin

GEN 3:17–3:19 See also ROM 8:20–8:22; HEB 6:8

Creation speaks of God's nature and character

His revelation of himself

JOB 12:7–12:10 This is not to suggest that animals can talk to human beings, rather it is a poetic device to stress what can be learnt from created things.

His eternal power and divine nature

ROM 1:20 See also JER 32:17

His authority

JOB 38:4–39:30; ; JER 33:2

His glory and majesty

PSA 19:1–19:2 See also PSA 8:1–8:9

His love and faithfulness

PSA 36:5 See also MAT 6:30

His power

ISA 40:25–40:28

His wisdom

PSA 136:5 See also PRO 8:27–8:29

His unchangeableness and eternity

PSA 102:25–102:27 See also PSA 90:1–90:2; HEB 1:11

His spiritual work in believers' lives

MAT 13:3–13:43

resource.label: 3296: Holy Spirit, work in the world

title: 4008: creation, and Jesus Christ
content: See 2303: Jesus Christ, as creator

title: 4009: creation, and Holy Spirit
content: See 3266: Holy Spirit, in creation

title: 4010: creation, renewal of
content:
God's creation, spoilt by sin, will one day be completely renewed. The old order will pass away and a new everlasting order established.

The renewal of creation

The passing away of the old order

2PE 3:10–3:12 See also PSA 102:25–102:26; ISA 34:4, ISA 51:6; MAT 5:18, MAT 24:35; REV 6:14, REV 21:4

The expectancy of nature

ROM 8:19–8:22 See also GEN 3:17–3:19, GEN 5:29

Jesus Christ's destruction of all his enemies

1CO 15:24–15:28 See also PSA 110:1; ACT 2:34–2:35; HEB 1:13, HEB 10:13

The new heavens and new earth

REV 21:1–21:2 See also ISA 65:17, ISA 66:22; ACT 3:21; HEB 11:10, HEB 11:16, HEB 12:22; 2PE 3:13; REV 3:12, REV 21:3–21:5, REV 21:10

The purity of the new creation

REV 21:27 See also PSA 37:20; ISA 52:1; JOL 3:17; REV 20:10, REV 22:14–22:15

The creation of the new humanity

The new birth

2CO 5:17 See also JHN 3:3, JHN 3:5–3:8; ROM 6:4; GAL 6:15; EPH 2:15; TIT 3:5

Renewal in God's image

COL 3:10 See also EPH 4:24

A new heart and a new spirit

EZK 36:26 “new” refers to the work of the Holy Spirit in renewing Christians as they believe in Jesus Christ. This renewing will be complete in the new creation. See also PSA 51:10; JER 31:33; EZK 11:19, EZK 18:31; 2CO 4:16; COL 3:1

A new mind

EPH 4:23 See also ROM 12:1–12:2

A new song

REV 5:9–5:10 See also PSA 33:3, PSA 40:3, PSA 144:9, PSA 149:1; ISA 42:10; REV 14:3, REV 15:3–15:4

The future glorification of humanity

The desire for a new body

ROM 8:23–8:25 See also ROM 8:17–8:18; 2CO 5:1–5:5

The resurrection of a new body

ROM 8:11 See also JOB 19:25–19:27; DAN 12:2–12:3; JHN 5:28–5:29; ACT 24:15

The nature of the resurrection body

1CO 15:42–15:54 See also PHP 3:21

The absence of sorrow and pain

REV 21:4 See also ISA 25:7–25:8, ISA 35:10, ISA 51:11, ISA 60:20, ISA 65:19; REV 7:17

A new name

ISA 56:5 See also ISA 62:2, ISA 65:15; REV 2:17, REV 3:12

A new home

JHN 14:2 See also 2CO 5:1

God will dwell with his people

REV 21:3 See also EZK 48:35; ZEC 2:10

title: 4015: life
content: The state of being alive, characterised by vitality, growth and development.

title: 4016: life, human
content:
Seen by Scripture as the climax of the work of creation, life is a gift of God and is to be treated with reverence and respect.

Life is from God

GEN 2:7; ACT 3:15, ACT 17:25 See also 1SA 2:6; JOB 33:4; PSA 139:13; DAN 5:23; ACT 17:28; JAS 4:14–4:15

The ultimate duration of life is unknown to people

PSA 39:4 See also GEN 27:2; JAS 4:13–4:14

Life is precious

MAT 10:31 See also GEN 1:26–1:27 Since all people are made in the image of God they are of value to him and should be so to each other; GEN 9:5–9:6; PSA 49:7–49:9, PSA 139:14; MAT 16:26 parallel LUK 9:25

God's presence in life

DEU 30:16; PSA 23:6 See also PSA 71:5–71:6, PSA 71:9

Life is to be lived for God

ECC 12:13; ; JER 10:23; ; MIC 6:8; ; MAT 10:39; ; PHP 1:21

The span of human life

ECC 11:8–12:8 See also PSA 90:10

Life is temporary

2CO 5:1 See also PSA 49:12, PSA 103:15–103:16; ISA 38:12; 2PE 1:13–1:14

Life is short

1CH 29:15; JOB 14:1; JAS 4:14 See also JOB 7:6–7:7, JOB 8:9, JOB 9:25, JOB 14:2; PSA 39:4–39:6, PSA 89:47, PSA 90:12, PSA 102:11, PSA 144:4; ECC 6:12; ISA 40:6–40:8

The termination of life by death is due to sin

ROM 5:12 See also GEN 2:17; 1CO 15:22

Examples of long life

GEN 5:3–5:32, GEN 9:29, GEN 11:10–11:32

A full span of years

PRO 3:16 See also EXO 20:12 parallel DEU 5:16; EXO 23:25–23:26; 1KI 3:14; JOB 5:26; PRO 3:2, PRO 9:11, PRO 10:27

Attitudes to life

Life loved

1PE 3:10 See also PSA 34:12–34:13; JOB 8:21; PSA 91:16

Life despised

ECC 2:22–2:23 See also JOB 7:16, JOB 10:1; ECC 2:17; JON 4:8

title: 4017: life, animal and plant
content:
All living creatures and plants were made by God and are sustained by him.

Animal life

Animals were created by God

GEN 2:19; PSA 104:24 See also GEN 1:20–1:25

Animals are cared for by God

PSA 145:15–145:16; MAT 6:26 parallel LUK 12:24 See also DEU 22:7; PSA 104:10–104:12, PSA 104:14, PSA 104:17–104:18, PSA 104:21, PSA 104:27, PSA 136:25, PSA 147:9; JON 4:11; MAT 10:29

Animal life is controlled by God

DAN 6:22 See also NUM 22:28; JON 1:17; HEB 11:33

Animal life has been placed under human care

GEN 1:26 See also GEN 1:28, GEN 2:19, GEN 9:2; PSA 8:6–8:8; DAN 2:38; HEB 2:8; JAS 3:7

Animals serve human needs

GEN 3:21; ; MAT 21:1–21:3 parallels MRK 11:1–11:3, LUK 19:29–19:31

Animal life saved by Noah from the flood

GEN 6:19–6:22

Animals subsequently given to humanity for food

GEN 9:3–9:4; ; DEU 12:20–12:25

Clean and unclean animals

GEN 7:2–7:3; ; LEV 11:47

Animals used in sacrifice

GEN 4:4 See also GEN 8:20; LEV 17:11; HEB 9:22

Animal life glorifies God

PSA 148:7, PSA 150:6

Plant life

Plants were created by God

GEN 1:11–1:12

Plant life is often short-lived

MAT 6:30 parallel LUK 12:28 See also PSA 90:5–90:6, PSA 103:15–103:16; ISA 37:27, ISA 51:12; 1PE 1:24; ISA 40:6–40:8

Plants are provided for food

GEN 1:29–1:30 See also GEN 2:16–2:17, GEN 3:2, GEN 9:3; 1KI 18:5; JOB 28:5; PSA 104:14, PSA 147:8–147:9; DAN 4:32; JOL 2:22

Plant life glorifies God

PSA 96:12–96:13

title: 4018: life, spiritual
content:
Life embraces more than physical existence; it includes humanity's relationship with God. Human beings come to life spiritually only through faith in the redeeming work of God in Jesus Christ. This spiritual life is a foretaste of the life which believers will finally enjoy to the full in the new heaven and earth. Life in the Spirit means keeping in step with the promptings and guidance of the Holy Spirit, and always being open to his gifts and empowerment.

The nature of spiritual life

It is new life

ACT 5:20 See also ACT 11:18; 2PE 1:3; 1JN 3:14

It is true life

1TI 6:19

It is eternal life

ROM 5:21 See also DAN 12:2; MAT 19:29; JHN 6:27; 1JN 5:11, 1JN 5:20

It is abundant life

PSA 16:11; JER 17:8 See also PSA 1:3; JHN 10:10

The origins and nature of spiritual life

Spiritual life is the work of the Holy Spirit

JHN 3:6, JHN 3:8 See also EZK 36:26; JHN 3:3, JHN 3:5–3:7; ROM 8:11; TIT 3:5–3:7

Spiritual life unites believers to Jesus Christ

EPH 2:4–2:5 See also ROM 6:3–6:5, ROM 8:10; 1CO 12:13; COL 2:13; 1JN 5:12

Spiritual life makes believers the children of God

JHN 1:12–1:13 See also DEU 30:20; MAT 6:9; ROM 8:15; JAS 1:18; 1JN 4:7, 1JN 5:1

Spiritual life brings people to know God

JHN 17:3 See also MAT 11:27

Spiritual life brings about faith

JHN 3:15, JHN 20:31 See also JHN 3:16, JHN 3:36, JHN 5:24, JHN 6:40, JHN 11:25

Keeping in step with the Spirit

A new way of life is made possible

GAL 5:25 See also ROM 8:5–8:6, ROM 8:9–8:16; GAL 5:16–5:18, GAL 5:22–5:24

Bondage to the written law is ended

ROM 2:29 See also ROM 7:6, ROM 8:2; 2CO 3:6; GAL 5:17–5:18

Obedience to God is made possible

ROM 8:4 See also EZK 36:27; ROM 8:13; GAL 5:16; 1TH 4:7–4:8

Deepening unity is encouraged

EPH 4:3 See also COL 2:13; PHP 2:1–2:4

Strength and encouragement are received

ACT 9:31

Gifts for those living in the Spirit

Gifts are given for building up the church

1CO 12:4–12:11 See also ROM 12:6–12:8; 1CO 12:27–12:30

Visions are given

ACT 2:17; ; JOL 2:28; ; REV 1:10, REV 1:12–1:13, REV 4:2, REV 17:3, REV 21:10

Miracles are worked

MAT 12:28 See also ACT 10:38; ROM 15:19; GAL 3:5

Ministry is enhanced

2CO 3:6 See also 2CO 3:7–3:9

Those living in the Spirit receive revelation and guidance

God is revealed as Father

GAL 4:6 See also ROM 8:14–8:16

God's purposes are revealed

1CO 2:9–2:10 See also ROM 15:13; 2CO 5:2–5:5; GAL 5:5; EPH 1:17–1:18

Guidance is given to believers

ACT 8:29 See also ACT 10:19, ACT 11:12, ACT 13:2, ACT 16:6–16:7, ACT 20:22–20:23

Help is given to pray

ROM 8:26–8:27; ; EPH 6:18; ; JUD 1:20

The Holy Spirit sanctifies those in whom he lives

Through the Spirit, Jesus Christ lives in believers

EPH 3:16–3:17

The Spirit transforms believers

2CO 3:18 See also ROM 15:16; 2TH 2:13; 1PE 1:2

The fruit of the Spirit is seen in believers' lives

ACT 13:52; ; ROM 5:5, ROM 8:6, ROM 14:17, ROM 15:30; ; GAL 5:22–5:23; ; COL 1:8; ; 1TH 1:6

Examples of life in the Holy Spirit

Jesus Christ

MAT 4:1 parallels MRK 1:12, LUK 4:1; ; MAT 12:18, MAT 12:28; ; LUK 4:14, LUK 4:18, LUK 10:21; ; ACT 10:38

Simeon

LUK 2:25–2:27

Peter

ACT 4:8, ACT 10:19, ACT 10:44

Stephen

ACT 6:5, ACT 6:10, ACT 7:55

The first Christians

ACT 4:31, ACT 6:3–6:5, ACT 11:24, ACT 11:27–11:29, ACT 13:1–13:3, ACT 15:28

resource.label: 3269: Holy Spirit, in life of Jesus Christ

title: 4019: life, believers' experience of
content:
God is at work in all that happens to believers, whether to warn them, to draw them to himself or to do them good.

God has a purpose in all the experiences that believers have in life

ROM 8:28; EPH 1:11 See also GEN 21:22, GEN 28:16, GEN 39:20–39:21, GEN 45:5–45:8; 1SA 2:6–2:9; 1CH 29:11–29:12; JOB 42:10–42:13; PSA 75:6–75:7; ACT 17:28; 1PE 4:12

Rejection of this conviction leads to despair

ECC 1:1–1:2, ECC 1:16–1:17

God uses every experience in the lives of believers for good

To warn and correct

PSA 119:67 See also GEN 12:17; 2CH 7:13–7:14; JOB 5:17; ISA 38:17, ISA 48:9–48:10; AMO 4:10–4:11; ROM 2:4; HEB 12:5–12:11; REV 9:20–9:21

To test and exercise believers' trust in God

DEU 8:15–8:16 See also EXO 15:22–15:25; JDG 2:21–2:22; PSA 23:1–23:6, PSA 81:7; ISA 43:1–43:2; NAM 1:7; ROM 8:35–8:39; PHP 4:12; HEB 11:17–11:19, HEB 13:6

To purify and prepare believers for glory

2CO 4:16–4:17 See also JOB 23:10; PSA 66:10; ISA 48:10; JER 9:7; ZEC 13:8–13:9; ROM 5:3–5:5, ROM 8:28–8:30; 1PE 1:6–1:7

To benefit others

EST 4:14; ; 2CO 1:3–1:6, 2CO 4:15; ; PHP 1:12–1:14; ; 2TI 2:10

In all of life's experiences believers should be thankful and trusting

PHP 4:6 See also GEN 8:20 Noah's sacrifice would, among other things, express his thankfulness; DEU 8:18; JOB 1:20–1:21; PSA 103:1–103:2; PRO 3:5–3:6; ACT 16:25; EPH 5:20; 1TI 4:4–4:5

Believers do not merit the blessings they receive

PSA 103:10 See also GEN 32:10, GEN 50:19–50:21; EZR 9:13; LAM 3:22; LUK 7:6; ROM 6:23

title: 4020: life, of faith
content:
The way by which believers journey through this world and into the life to come. Jesus Christ himself is the way to life.

Life seen as travelling with God

Walking with God

GEN 17:1 See also GEN 5:22, GEN 5:24, GEN 6:9, GEN 48:15; PSA 56:13, PSA 89:15; MIC 4:5; ZEC 10:12

Journeying from the old to the new

ISA 43:19 See also EXO 18:8; ISA 40:3–40:5; MIC 2:13

God's guidance along the way

EXO 13:21 See also EXO 23:20; DEU 1:32–1:33, DEU 8:2; NEH 9:12, NEH 9:19; PSA 25:9; JER 2:17; GAL 5:25

God's ways

Walking in God's way

ISA 35:8; 1JN 2:6 See also GEN 18:19; EXO 18:20; DEU 10:12–10:13, DEU 13:5, DEU 28:9; JOS 22:5; JOB 23:10–23:12; PSA 1:1–1:2, PSA 18:30; 2TI 3:10

God teaches believers his way

ISA 48:17 See also 1SA 12:23; 1KI 8:35–8:36 parallel 2CH 6:26–6:27; PSA 25:8–25:9, PSA 25:12, PSA 86:11, PSA 119:30; PRO 6:23; ISA 2:3, ISA 30:20–30:21PSA 16:11, PSA 23:2; PRO 8:20; JER 6:16; MIC 6:8; GAL 5:22–5:23

Sinners refuse to follow God's way

ISA 53:6 See also ISA 56:11; ACT 14:16; 2PE 2:15

All other routes end in death

PRO 14:12 See also DEU 11:28, DEU 31:29; JDG 2:17; 2KI 21:22; PSA 1:6; PRO 15:10, PRO 16:25

Those who travel God's way are blessed

PRO 4:18 See also DEU 5:33; 1KI 8:23; PRO 11:5; ISA 26:7–26:8; MAT 5:3–5:12

Jesus Christ is the way to life

JHN 14:6; COL 2:3 See also JHN 8:12; HEB 12:2ACT 9:2, ACT 19:9, ACT 19:23, ACT 22:4, ACT 24:14, ACT 24:22

Entrance to the way to life

Entrance is restricted

MAT 7:13–7:14 See also JHN 10:9, JHN 14:6

Entrance is by faith

HEB 11:8–11:10 See also JHN 3:15–3:16; 2CO 5:7; HEB 11:6, HEB 11:13–11:16

resource.label: 2363: Jesus Christ, preaching and teaching of

title: 4025: world, the
content: Scripture understands “the world” in a number of senses. It initially refers to the world as God's good creation. However, that same world has now fallen into sin, with the result that it can be a threat to believers. Believers are called to live in the world, maintaining contact with it, while remaining distinct from it, and avoiding being contaminated by it.

title: 4026: world, as God's creation
content:
The heavens and the earth were created by God, and ordered by him and for him.

The world was created out of nothing

By the will of God

GEN 1:1 See also PSA 96:5, PSA 102:25, PSA 148:5; ISA 40:28; HEB 11:3; REV 4:11, REV 10:6

By the word of God

JHN 1:1–1:3 Jesus Christ as the eternal Word is creator of all things. See also GEN 1:3; JHN 1:10; 1CO 8:6; HEB 1:2; 2PE 3:5

By the Spirit of God

GEN 1:2 See also JOB 26:13, JOB 33:4; PSA 33:6

By the hand of God

HEB 1:10 See also PSA 8:3

By the mind of God

PSA 136:5 See also PSA 104:24; PRO 3:19–3:20

The world God created was perfect

GEN 1:31 See also 1TI 4:4

God created the world for a purpose

For his glory

PSA 19:1–19:2 See also PSA 50:6, PSA 148:1–148:4; ROM 1:20, ROM 11:36

That people might worship and revere him

PSA 147:4–147:5 See also GEN 15:5; NEH 9:6; JOB 22:12; ISA 40:25–40:26; JER 31:35

For his possession and use

DEU 10:14; COL 1:16–1:17 See also PSA 24:1–24:2, PSA 50:12, PSA 89:11; ACT 7:49 God reigns within the world he has made.

title: 4027: world, as fallen
content:
The world has fallen into sin and rebellion against its Creator. As a result, it has come under the power of Satan.

The world is under the power of Satan

He is prince of this world

JHN 14:30 See also JHN 12:31; 2CO 4:4; 1JN 4:4

He holds the world in his grasp

1JN 5:19 See also EPH 2:1–2:4

The world is opposed to God

It is evil

JHN 7:7 See also JHN 3:19–3:20; GAL 1:4; 1JN 2:15–2:16

Evil extends to the unseen world of the heavenly realms

EPH 6:12

It is opposed to God's wisdom

1CO 1:21 See also 1CO 1:25, 1CO 2:6–2:8, 1CO 3:19

It is opposed to the life of faith

JHN 15:18–15:19 See also JHN 1:10–1:11, JHN 17:6, JHN 17:9, JHN 17:14–17:18, JHN 17:25; JAS 2:5, JAS 4:4; 1JN 3:1

It is opposed to Jesus Christ's kingdom

JHN 18:36 See also ISA 40:23; MAT 4:8–4:10 parallel LUK 4:5–4:8; MAT 24:14; LUK 12:30; JHN 8:23, JHN 14:17; 2CO 10:3; HEB 11:38; 1PE 2:11

The world is under judgment

It is condemned

1CO 11:32 See also GEN 6:5–6:7, GEN 7:4, GEN 7:21; ISA 13:11; ZEP 3:8; MAT 18:7; JHN 12:31, JHN 16:11; 1JN 2:17

It will be judged in righteousness

PSA 9:8 See also PSA 96:13; ISA 26:9; NAM 1:5–1:6; ACT 17:31; ROM 3:19

It will be judged by the saints

1CO 6:2

title: 4028: world, as redeemed
content:
Though fallen, the world is offered the hope of redemption and restoration through Jesus Christ.

This world has an end

It is temporary

1PE 1:24 See also ISA 40:6–40:7; MAT 5:18, MAT 24:35 parallels MRK 13:31, LUK 21:33; 1CO 7:31; HEB 1:10–1:12, HEB 12:26–12:27

It is under God's judgment

ISA 13:13 See also ISA 34:4, ISA 51:6; JOL 2:30–2:31; 2PE 3:10; 1JN 2:17; REV 20:11

Through Jesus Christ there is redemption and renewal for the world

For humanity

1JN 2:2 See also MAT 1:21; JHN 1:29, JHN 3:16–3:17; ROM 5:18; 2CO 5:18–5:19

For creation

ROM 8:19–8:21 See also MAT 24:7–24:8 parallels MRK 13:8, LUK 21:10–21:11; COL 1:20

The promise of a new heaven and a new earth

2PE 3:13 See also ISA 65:17; 1CO 15:24–15:28; EPH 1:10; REV 21:1–21:4

title: 4029: world, human beings in
content:
The world is intended to be the dwelling-place of humanity, as the height of God's creation. Human beings are organised within the world into groupings and nations and have a God-given authority to rule.

The responsibility of human beings to rule

Over the earth

GEN 2:15–2:16 See also GEN 1:25–1:26, GEN 1:29; PSA 8:4–8:6

Over the animal kingdom

GEN 1:28 “subdue” and “rule” do not give liberty to exploit and abuse but responsibility for caring for and bringing the best out of God's world. See also GEN 9:1–9:5; DEU 22:6–22:7; PRO 27:23–27:27; MAT 6:25–6:26; ACT 10:11–10:13

The response of human beings to God's commission to rule

Failure through abuse

HOS 4:2–4:3 See also GEN 3:12, GEN 6:11–6:12; DEU 28:15–28:21

Refusal to fill the earth

GEN 11:5–11:7 See also GEN 4:17

Obedience and blessing

DEU 28:4–28:5 See also GEN 49:25

The ordering of nations within the world

By language

GEN 11:8–11:9 See also GEN 10:5; NEH 13:24; EST 1:22; ACT 2:6–2:8; REV 14:6

By race

REV 7:9 See also REV 5:9, REV 13:7

By territory

ACT 17:26 See also GEN 13:11–13:12; NUM 13:27–13:29

By their rise and fall

JER 18:7–18:10 See also 2CH 15:6; ISA 19:2; JER 1:10; DAN 7:17–7:18; MAT 24:7 parallels MRK 13:8, LUK 21:10–21:11

By government

ROM 13:1 See also DAN 2:21, DAN 4:17; 1PE 2:13–2:14

The world as the object of the gospel proclamation

God sent Jesus Christ to this world

JHN 3:16 See also ISA 45:22; JHN 1:10, JHN 1:29; 1JN 2:2, 1JN 4:9

Believers are commanded to evangelise this world

MAT 28:18–28:19 See also MRK 16:15; LUK 24:46–24:47; ACT 1:8

resource.label: 3296: Holy Spirit, work in the world

title: 4030: world, human behaviour in
content:
Human behaviour in the world is now characterised by self-centredness as a result of sin. Believers are called upon to renounce such sinful behaviour.

Human passion in the world is expressed in various ways

In physical desires

1JN 2:15–2:16 See also EXO 20:17; PRO 27:20; LUK 12:15, LUK 21:34; JAS 4:4; 2PE 2:18–2:20

In indulgence

JAS 5:1, JAS 5:5 See also JOB 21:7–21:13; PSA 73:2–73:3; 2TI 4:10

In intellectual pursuits

COL 2:8 See also GAL 4:3–4:5; EPH 2:3

In a weak will

EXO 23:2 See also JOB 31:34; ROM 6:17–6:19

Though remaining in the world, believers are not of the world

JHN 17:6–17:19

Believers are to renounce the passions of the world

Through a renewed mind

ROM 12:2 See also GAL 1:4; EPH 4:23; COL 3:5–3:10

Through obedience

1PE 1:14–1:15 See also 2CO 10:2–10:5; GAL 5:24, GAL 6:15; EPH 5:17; TIT 2:12; JAS 1:27; 2PE 1:4

Through having spiritual priorities

LUK 12:29–12:31 parallel MAT 6:31–6:33 See also MAT 10:39; LUK 14:26–14:27, LUK 17:33; JHN 12:24–12:26; 1TI 6:6

Through growing to maturity

1CO 3:1 See also HEB 5:12, HEB 6:1–6:3

The futility of worldly pursuits

They bring no lasting material gain

1TI 6:7 See also JOB 1:21; PSA 49:17; ECC 5:15; 1TI 6:17–6:19

They bring no spiritual gain

MRK 8:36–8:37 parallel LUK 9:25 See also JOB 20:4–20:5; PSA 17:14; LUK 16:25–16:26

title: 4035: abundance
content:
A state of plenty. Since Israel was often restricted in resources, abundance was an important feature of her future hope, and became a symbol of spiritual well-being.

Examples of material abundance

Rain

DEU 32:2 See also 1KI 18:41, 1KI 18:45; JOB 36:28; PSA 68:9; JOL 2:23

Water, streams and rivers

PSA 78:20 See also NUM 24:7; 2CH 32:4; PSA 1:3; ISA 44:4; EZK 47:5

Luxuriant growth and fruitfulness

EZK 47:12 See also EZK 19:10, EZK 31:2–31:9; DAN 4:12, DAN 4:21; REV 22:2

Harvest, food, milk and wine

AMO 9:13 See also 2CH 31:10; PSA 4:7, PSA 23:5, PSA 37:19; PRO 3:10; ISA 7:22; JER 44:17; JOL 3:13; MIC 2:11

Spaciousness

PSA 31:8 See also GEN 26:22; PSA 66:12

Wealth and possessions

GEN 24:35 See also GEN 30:43, GEN 36:7; 2SA 19:32; 2CH 1:15, 2CH 9:22; JOB 1:3

Canaan

EXO 3:8 See also NUM 13:27; DEU 8:8; JER 2:7

Material abundance a reward for obedience

LEV 26:5 See also DEU 30:9; JOB 42:12; PSA 132:15

Obedience not always rewarded in this way

JOB 1:12, JOB 2:6; ; PHP 4:12

Examples of spiritual abundance

The abundance of divine power

DEU 9:26; ; PSA 93:4; ; JOL 2:29–2:32; ; EPH 1:18–1:20, EPH 3:20

The abundance of divine grace

ROM 5:20; ; 2CO 9:8–9:14; ; EPH 1:7, EPH 2:6–2:7; ; 1TI 1:14; ; 1PE 1:2; ; 2PE 1:2

The abundance of human thanksgiving

2CO 4:15 See also PSA 119:171

The abundance of God's future blessing

2CO 4:17 See also PSA 36:8; ISA 64:4; JHN 10:10; 1CO 2:9; 2PE 1:11; REV 14:3

title: 4040: beauty
content:
A physical or spiritual quality which brings pleasure to those who behold it. Scripture stresses the beauty of God himself and his creation, while noting that the beauty of the creation can lead away from God or become idolised.

Beauty in nature

JOB 38:31; ECC 3:11 See also GEN 49:21; EZK 20:6, EZK 31:7–31:9

Beautiful people

Women

GEN 29:17; EST 1:11 See also GEN 6:2, GEN 12:11, GEN 12:14, GEN 24:15–24:16, GEN 26:7; DEU 21:11; 1SA 25:3; 2SA 13:1, 2SA 14:27; 1KI 1:3–1:4EST 2:7, EST 2:17 JOB 42:15; PSA 45:11; SNG 2:14, SNG 4:7, SNG 7:1

Men

GEN 39:6 See also 1SA 16:12, 1SA 17:42; 2SA 14:25; 1KI 1:6; SNG 1:16, SNG 5:10; EZK 23:6, EZK 23:23

An enhanced quality

EST 2:2–2:3 See also EST 2:9, EST 2:12; ISA 3:24; EZK 7:20

Beauty and lack of beauty as external impressions

ISA 53:2 See also PRO 11:22, PRO 31:30; 1PE 3:3

May lead to pride and lust

PRO 6:25–6:26; EZK 28:17 See also GEN 12:11–12:12, GEN 39:7; 2SA 11:2–11:4; EZK 23:12

Beautiful artefacts

LUK 21:5 See also JOS 7:21; EZK 27:3–27:4, EZK 27:11, EZK 27:24; ACT 3:2

The beauty of Jerusalem

PSA 50:2 See also PSA 48:2; JER 6:2; LAM 2:15; EZK 16:10–16:15, EZK 16:25; DAN 11:45; REV 21:2

The beauty of God as the joy of believers

PSA 27:4 See also 1CH 16:29; PSA 29:2; ISA 4:2, ISA 28:5, ISA 33:17, ISA 52:7, ISA 61:3; JER 11:16; ROM 10:15

title: 4045: chaos
content:
A state of complete disorder or confusion. The first stage of creation where, having brought something out of nothing, God had not yet brought order to the universe. This primeval chaos, often symbolised by surging waters, threatens to disrupt God's order at any time. God sometimes allows a return to chaos as judgment.

God creates order out of chaos

God creates something out of nothing

GEN 1:1–1:2 See also PSA 33:9, PSA 148:5–148:6; ROM 4:17; HEB 11:3

God brings order to his creation

ISA 45:12 See also GEN 1:14–1:19; JOB 9:8; PSA 8:3; ISA 48:13

Chaos threatens God's order

Life and order in creation must be sustained by God

PSA 104:29; COL 1:16–1:17 See also JOB 12:10, JOB 34:14–34:15; ISA 42:5; HEB 1:2–1:3

The raging sea as a symbol of chaos

PSA 46:2–46:3 The raging sea is a frequent symbol of the chaos from which the world was created and to which it could return. Yet those whose confidence is in the God who brought order out of chaos have nothing to fear; PSA 104:7–104:9 See also EXO 15:8; PSA 69:2, PSA 69:15, PSA 77:16; PRO 8:29; ZEC 10:11; MAT 8:23–8:27 parallels MRK 4:36–4:41, LUK 8:22–8:25

Threatening nations are likened to the raging seas as bringers of chaos

ISA 17:12–17:13 See also PSA 46:6; JER 46:7

Spiritual and political chaos

JDG 17:6 A period of anarchy, when no spiritual or political authority was recognised in Israel; EPH 4:14 See also GAL 1:7

God uses chaos as a judgment

ISA 34:11 See also ISA 24:1, ISA 24:3–24:4, ISA 24:10–24:12, ISA 24:17–24:21, ISA 27:10, ISA 32:14; ZEP 1:14–1:15; REV 6:15–6:17

Floodwaters as a symbol of the chaos of judgment

JON 2:3 See also GEN 6:17; JOB 20:28, JOB 22:16; ISA 8:7

Panic and destruction characterise the chaos that follows judgment

ZEC 14:13 See also JDG 7:22; 1SA 14:20; 2CH 20:22–20:23; ISA 19:2; EZK 38:21; HAG 2:22

resource.label: 3296: Holy Spirit, work in the world

title: 4050: dust
content:
Human beings were created from the dust by God and will return to it at death. Dust is therefore taken as a symbol of poverty, lowliness and humility. In Scripture dust is shaken off the feet to warn of future judgment, and is also used as a means of expressing grief.

Human beings were created from the dust by God

GEN 2:7 See also GEN 18:27; PSA 103:14; 1CO 15:47

We will all return to dust when we die

GEN 3:19 God's curse upon Adam affected all humanity; PSA 90:3 See also JOB 10:9, JOB 34:15; ECC 12:7

Dust as a symbol of poverty, lowliness and humility

1SA 2:8 See also GEN 18:27; 1KI 16:2; JOB 30:19

Dust as a simile for a multitude

GEN 13:16 See also GEN 28:14; PSA 78:27

Dust is shaken off the feet as a warning of judgment

MRK 6:11 parallels MAT 10:14, LUK 9:5 See also LUK 10:10–10:11; ACT 13:51

Dust is used in expressing grief and repentance

JOB 42:5–42:6; LAM 2:10 See also JOS 7:6; JOB 2:12; EZK 27:30; REV 18:19

title: 4055: heaven and earth
content:
An expression which in the original Hebrew refers to the whole of creation. It is frequently used in relation to God both as Creator and Lord in order to emphasise his power and authority.

Heaven and earth in relation to God

He is the Creator of heaven and earth

ISA 37:16 parallel 2KI 19:15 See also GEN 14:18–14:23; 2CH 2:12; PSA 115:15, PSA 121:2, PSA 124:8, PSA 134:3, PSA 146:5–146:6; ACT 14:15, ACT 17:24

He is Lord of heaven and earth

MAT 11:25 parallel LUK 10:21 See also 1CH 29:11–29:12; EZR 5:11; JER 23:23–23:24, JER 33:25–33:26

The personification of heaven and earth

DEU 31:28 See also DEU 4:26, DEU 30:19; PSA 69:34; JER 51:48

Allusions to the end of heaven and earth

MAT 24:35 parallels MRK 13:31, LUK 21:33 See also MAT 5:18; LUK 16:17

The expression ``between heaven and earth''

1CH 21:16 See also ZEC 5:9

title: 4060: nature
content:
God's creation, often with special reference to plant and animal life.

Nature is God's creation

PSA 89:11 See also ISA 45:12; JER 27:5; ACT 4:24, ACT 14:15, ACT 17:24; REV 4:11, REV 10:6

Plants

GEN 1:11–1:12 See also GEN 2:4–2:9; PSA 104:14–104:16

Land and sea

PSA 24:1 See also GEN 1:9–1:10; JOB 38:4–38:11; ISA 40:12; AMO 5:8; JON 1:9

The sun, moon and stars

JOB 9:9 See also GEN 1:14–1:18; PSA 8:3, PSA 148:3–148:5; JER 31:35

Animals

GEN 1:20–1:22 See also JOB 12:7–12:10; PSA 104:25

Human beings

EXO 4:11; ISA 45:12 GEN 1:26–1:27, GEN 2:7; PSA 95:6, PSA 100:3; ; PRO 22:2; ; JER 27:5

The distinction between humanity and the rest of nature

God created human beings alone in his image

GEN 1:26–1:27; COL 3:9–3:10 See also GEN 5:1, GEN 9:6; JAS 3:9

Humanity is the steward of nature

PSA 115:16 See also GEN 1:26–1:30, GEN 2:15; PSA 8:5–8:8

God is active in nature

In sustaining nature

MAT 10:29 parallel LUK 12:6 See also PSA 147:1–147:17; ACT 17:24–17:28; COL 1:17; HEB 2:10

In providing for plant life

LEV 26:4; MAT 6:28–6:29 parallel LUK 12:27 See also JOB 38:25; PSA 104:14–104:16; JON 4:6–4:10

In the elements and seasons

PSA 135:7 See also GEN 8:22; JOB 5:10, JOB 36:27, JOB 37:11–37:13; JER 10:13

God makes himself known through the natural order

JOB 36:24–36:26 See also PSA 8:1–8:4, PSA 19:1–19:3; ACT 14:15–14:17, ACT 17:24–17:28; ROM 1:18–1:21

title: 4065: orderliness
content:
In his creating, sustaining and saving work, God reveals himself as a God of order. Likewise, the life of his people and of society as a whole should be orderly.

Orderliness in creation

ISA 45:12 See also GEN 1:31; JOB 26:7–26:10, JOB 38:4–38:11; PSA 8:3, PSA 19:1, PSA 104:5–104:9; ISA 40:26; ACT 17:26

Orderliness in providence

GEN 8:22 See also PSA 65:9, PSA 104:14–104:27, PSA 145:15; ACT 14:17

Orderliness in salvation

Salvation was planned by God

ROM 8:29 See also 1CO 2:7; EPH 1:4–1:5; 2TI 1:9; TIT 1:2; 1PE 1:2; REV 13:8

Jesus Christ's coming was prepared in advance

LUK 24:26–24:27 See also JHN 5:46; ACT 2:23, ACT 3:18; GAL 4:4

Orderliness in the life of God's people

Corporate worship must be orderly

EXO 25:9; 1CO 14:40 See also 1CH 28:12; ACT 7:44; 1CO 14:32–14:33

Personal devotion includes orderliness

1TH 5:6 See also PRO 25:28; ROM 13:13; 1CO 7:17; EPH 5:16; 1TI 3:2–3:5

Respect for leaders as part of God's order

EXO 22:28; HEB 13:17 See also 1SA 24:6; 1TI 5:17; HEB 13:7

Order in pastoral care

1CO 12:28; TIT 1:5 See also ACT 6:3, ACT 14:23; ROM 12:4–12:8; EPH 4:11; 1PE 4:10, 1PE 5:1–5:3

Orderliness in society

God gives responsibility in his administration to men and women

PSA 82:6; ; DAN 6:2–6:7; ; ROM 13:1; ; TIT 3:1; ; 1PE 2:13–2:14

Governing authorities are ordained by God

ROM 13:1 See also JHN 19:11; ROM 13:6

Those in authority are to be honoured

TIT 3:1 See also MAT 22:21 parallels MRK 12:17, LUK 20:25; 1TI 2:1–2:2; 1PE 2:13

Order in the home and workplace

1TI 3:4 See also EXO 6:14, EXO 20:12; 1SA 3:13; JOB 1:5; EPH 6:1, EPH 6:5; 1TI 3:4, 1TI 3:12, 1TI 6:1; TIT 1:6, TIT 2:4–2:5, TIT 2:9–2:10; 1PE 2:18

title: 4100: Supernatural beings
content:
• 4110: angels
  • 4120: Satan
  • 4130: demons
  • 4140: angel of the Lord
  • 4145: archangels
  • 4150: cherubim
  • 4155: divination

title: 4110: angels
content: Spiritual beings who assist God, especially in his work of salvation, conveying his word to human beings and attending to the needs of God's people.

resource.label: 4113: angels, as agents of God's judgment

title: 4111: angels, as God's servants
content:
Angels have specific yet limited roles to play in declaring God's will and purposes for humanity.

Angels are God's servants

PSA 103:20 See also HEB 1:7; PSA 104:4

Named angels

The archangel Michael: DAN 10:13, DAN 12:1; ; JUD 1:9; ; REV 12:7; DAN 8:16, DAN 9:21; ; LUK 1:19, LUK 1:26

Angels as agents of God's salvation

HEB 1:14 See also EXO 23:23, EXO 32:34, EXO 33:2; NUM 20:16; ISA 63:8–63:9

Angels do not fully understand God's salvation

1PE 1:10–1:12

Angels deliver God's people from their enemies

2KI 19:35 parallels 2CH 32:21, ISA 37:36; ; ACT 5:19, ACT 12:6–12:11

Angels as mediators

ACT 7:53 The law was mediated by angels to Moses and Israel See also JOB 33:22–33:26; ACT 7:38; GAL 3:19; HEB 2:2

Angels as revealers of God's will

ZEC 1:8–1:10 See also DAN 7:15–7:16; ZEC 4:11–4:14; REV 17:1, REV 21:9

Angels convey and fulfil God's instructions

ZEC 3:4 See also NUM 22:21–22:35; ZEC 3:6–3:7; MAT 2:13; GAL 1:8; REV 7:2

Angels attend to the needs of God's people

Angels provide food at special times

1KI 19:5–19:7 See also PSA 78:23–78:25

Angels provide protection

PSA 91:11–91:12 See also GEN 19:15, GEN 48:16; PSA 34:7; DAN 3:28, DAN 6:22; MAT 18:10; ACT 27:23–27:24

Angels give guidance

EXO 23:20 See also GEN 24:7, GEN 24:40; ACT 8:26

Angels serve Jesus Christ

Angels care for Jesus Christ's needs

MAT 4:11 parallel MRK 1:13 See also LUK 22:43

Angels are ready to protect Jesus Christ

MAT 26:53 See also MAT 2:13, MAT 2:19–2:20, MAT 4:6 parallel LUK 4:10; PSA 91:11–91:12

Angels are inferior to Jesus Christ

HEB 1:5–1:13

title: 4112: angels, as God's messengers
content:
Spiritual messengers with the special role of making known God's work of salvation.

Angels give hope through the words of the prophets

ISA 40:3–40:5 The voice in this passage may be that of an angel. ISA 40:1–40:2, ISA 40:6–40:8; DAN 8:15–8:16, DAN 10:12, DAN 10:14–10:21, DAN 12:8–12:13; ZEC 1:12–1:21, ZEC 2:3–2:5, ZEC 4:1–4:7

Angels and the coming of the Messiah

An angel foretells the birth of John the Baptist

LUK 1:11–1:13 See also LUK 1:14–1:19

Angels foretell Jesus Christ's birth

MAT 1:20–1:21 See also LUK 1:26–1:38

Angels announce Jesus Christ's birth

LUK 2:8–2:11 See also LUK 2:12–2:20

Angels announce Jesus Christ's resurrection

MAT 28:5–28:7 parallel MRK 16:5–16:7 See also LUK 24:4–24:7, LUK 24:23; JHN 20:10–20:14

Angels foretell Jesus Christ's second coming

ACT 1:10–1:11

Angels reveal the gospel for the Gentiles

ACT 11:13 See also ACT 10:1–10:5, ACT 10:30–10:33; REV 14:6–14:7

Angels foretell God's final triumph

REV 1:1 See also REV 19:9, REV 22:1, REV 22:6, REV 22:16

title: 4113: angels, as agents of God's judgment
content:
Spiritual beings who assist God in carrying out his judgments. Angels will be especially active when Jesus Christ returns for the last judgment.

Angels as agents of earthly judgments

PSA 78:49

Against Sodom and Gomorrah

See also GEN 19:13, GEN 19:24–19:25

Against opponents of God

EXO 12:23; ; 2KI 19:35 parallels 2CH 32:21, ISA 37:36; ; PSA 35:4–35:6

Against Israel

EXO 32:35; ; 2SA 24:16–24:17 parallel 1CH 21:15–21:16; ; 1CO 10:10

Against Herod Antipas

ACT 12:18–12:23

Angels restrained by God's mercy

1CH 21:15–21:16 parallel 2SA 24:16–24:17 See also GEN 18:20–18:32

Angels and final judgment

Angels proclaim God's sovereignty

REV 12:10–12:12 See also REV 10:1–10:4, REV 11:15

Angels announce God's final invitation

REV 14:6–14:7 See also REV 14:9–14:13, REV 19:9

Angels hold back the final judgment

REV 7:1–7:3

Angels carry out preliminary warning judgments

REV 8:1–8:13, REV 9:1–9:16, REV 10:5–10:7

Angels accompany Jesus Christ when he returns to judge

2TH 1:7 See also MAT 16:27, MAT 25:31; MRK 8:38 parallel LUK 9:26; 1TH 3:13

Angels gather everyone for the final judgment

MAT 13:37–13:41 See also MAT 13:49–13:50, MAT 24:31; REV 14:15–14:19

Angels announce the final judgment

REV 14:15 See also REV 10:8–10:11, REV 17:1–17:3, REV 17:7, REV 17:15, REV 18:1–18:2, REV 18:4

Angels enact the final judgment

REV 15:1 See also REV 14:16–14:19, REV 15:6–15:8, REV 16:1–16:21, REV 18:21, REV 19:17–19:18, REV 20:1–20:3

Angels are subject to judgment

1CO 6:3 See also 2PE 2:4; JUD 1:6

title: 4114: angels, and praise of God
content:
Spiritual beings who praise God in a manner that is beyond human capacity.

Angels worship God in his presence

REV 7:11 See also REV 3:5, REV 8:2

Angels praise God for his works

Angels praise God's work of creation

JOB 38:4–38:7 See also PSA 148:1–148:5; NEH 9:6

Angels praise God's work of redemption

LUK 2:13–2:14 The angels are praising God for Jesus Christ's birth. See also ISA 44:23, ISA 49:13; LUK 15:10; HEB 1:6; DEU 32:43

Angels praise God's kingdom

PSA 103:19–103:21 See also PSA 96:10–96:11 parallel 1CH 16:30–16:31; PSA 145:10–145:11; REV 11:15

Angelic beings praise God

ISA 6:2–6:4 See also REV 4:8, REV 5:8–5:10, REV 7:11–7:12

Thousands of angels worship God

HEB 12:22 See also DEU 33:2; PSA 68:17; DAN 7:10; REV 5:11–5:12

Images of angelic beings decorate Israel's sanctuaries

1KI 6:23–6:29 parallels 2CH 3:7, 2CH 3:10–3:13 See also EXO 26:1; EZK 41:18–41:20, EZK 41:25 The images of the cherubim represent the angels who worship God in heaven.

Angels are not to be worshipped

REV 22:8–22:9 See also MAT 4:9–4:10 parallel LUK 4:7–4:8; ROM 1:25; COL 2:18

title: 4115: angel, of the Lord
content: See 4140: angel of the Lord

title: 4116: angels, opposed to God
content:
Spiritual beings who fell with Satan. They are under God's judgment and are subject to the authority of believers.

Spiritual authorities led by Satan

2CO 11:14 See also JHN 12:31, JHN 16:11; EPH 6:12; REV 9:11

Angels opposed to God will be judged

JUD 1:6 See also JOB 4:18, JOB 15:15; MAT 25:41; 2PE 2:4; REV 20:10

Jesus Christ has defeated Satan and his angels

COL 2:15 See also GEN 3:15; EPH 1:19–1:21

Satan's angels are resisted by God's faithful angels

REV 12:7–12:9 See also DAN 10:13, DAN 10:20 The princes of Persia and Greece appear to be spiritual powers who resist God's rule over these regions.

Jesus Christ gives authority over Satan and his angels to believers

LUK 10:18–10:19 See also ROM 8:38–8:39, ROM 16:20; 1CO 6:3; EPH 6:10–6:13; JAS 4:7

resource.label: 6157: fall, of Satan and his angels

title: 4120: Satan
content: The one who opposes the person and purposes of God. Satan is especially associated with deceit, temptation and testing, through which he attempts to deflect believers from obeying God.

resource.label: 4121: Satan, as the enemy of God

title: 4121: Satan, as the enemy of God
content:
Scripture provides numerous examples of ways in which Satan opposes the presence and purposes of God in his world.

Satan and God's people

Satan opposes believers

1CH 21:1 See also JOB 2:1–2:7; ZEC 3:1–3:2; 1TH 2:18

Satan accuses believers

REV 12:10 See also JOB 1:8–1:11; ZEC 3:1–3:2

Satan slanders believers

1TI 5:14

Satan tests believers and causes their suffering

REV 2:10 See also EPH 6:11–6:13, EPH 6:16

Satan opposes the work of the archangel Michael

JUD 1:9 DAN 10:13, DAN 10:21, DAN 12:1; ; REV 12:7

Satan opposes God

Satan opposes God's purposes

MAT 16:23 parallel MRK 8:33

Satan opposes God's word

See also MAT 13:3–13:19 parallels MRK 4:3–4:16, LUK 8:4–8:12 the parable of the sower; MAT 13:24–13:39 the parable of the weeds and its explanation; ACT 13:8–13:10 Elymas, the sorcerer, acts on Satan's behalf to oppose Barnabas and Paul.

Satan opposes God's righteousness

1JN 3:7–3:10

Satan blasphemes God

REV 13:6

Satan's opposition to God's work will not succeed

ZEC 3:2 God rebukes him; ; LUK 10:19 His reign is temporary.

Satan's role as adversary earns him varied titles

The devil

He is the adversary because he deceives: REV 12:9, REV 20:2–20:3

Belial

He is the adversary because he is the troublemaker or scoundrel (the same Hebrew word is used in the OT passages as in 2Co 6:15 to describe Satan's work): DEU 13:13; ; 1KI 21:10; ; PRO 6:12; ; 2CO 6:15

The dragon

He is the adversary because he is the enemy: REV 12:4, REV 12:13–12:14

The serpent

He is the adversary because he thwarts spiritual insight: GEN 3:1–3:5, GEN 3:13; ; 2CO 11:3

The prince of this world

He is the adversary because his rule is temporary, not absolute: JHN 12:31, JHN 14:30, JHN 16:11

The angel of the Abyss

REV 9:11 He is the adversary because he is the destroyer.

resource.label: 6157: fall, of Satan and his angels
8787: opposition, to God's person and work

title: 4122: Satan, as tempter
content:
God's people are incited to evil by Satan. He seeks to exploit their weaknesses in order to deflect them from obedience to God.

Satan incites God's people to evil

Satan exploits human weaknesses

GEN 3:1–3:5 Rev 12:9 identifies the serpent with Satan. See also JOB 2:4–2:5; EPH 4:26–4:27

He tempts Christians to fall away

1TH 3:5 See also LUK 22:31; 1TI 3:7; 2TI 2:26

He inspires people to put God to the test

ACT 5:3–5:9 See also PSA 78:18, PSA 78:41, PSA 106:14; ACT 15:10; 1CO 10:9

He makes sin attractive

Lack of integrity: MAT 5:37; ; ACT 5:3; 1CO 7:5; ; 1TI 5:14–5:15; 2CO 2:10–2:11 unforgiveness; ; JAS 3:14–3:16 envy and selfish ambition

Jesus Christ is tempted by Satan

In the wilderness

MAT 4:1–4:11 parallels MRK 1:13, LUK 4:1–4:13

Through Simon Peter

MAT 16:23 parallel MRK 8:33

Jesus Christ identifies with believers

HEB 4:15 See also HEB 2:8

Satan tests Christians

REV 2:10 See also LUK 22:31; REV 3:10

Satan's temptations can be resisted

By being alert

1PE 5:8

By prayer

MAT 26:41 parallels MRK 14:38, LUK 22:40 See also MAT 6:13 parallel LUK 11:4; LUK 22:32, LUK 22:46

By relying on God's faithfulness

1CO 10:13 See also 2PE 2:9; REV 3:10

By Jesus Christ's service as high priest

HEB 2:18, HEB 4:15

title: 4123: Satan, as deceiver
content:
Satan's character is deceitful, devious and cunning as in a variety of guises he seeks to influence people for his own ends.

Satan's deceitful character

He is evil

MAT 6:13 See also MAT 5:37, MAT 13:19; JHN 17:15; 2TH 3:3; 1JN 2:13–2:14, 1JN 3:8, 1JN 3:12, 1JN 5:18

He is a liar

JHN 8:44 See also GEN 3:4–3:5; JOB 1:11, JOB 2:5; REV 3:9

He is devious

2CO 11:14 See also 2CO 11:3; EPH 6:11; 2TH 2:9; 1TI 3:7; 2TI 2:26

He is scheming

EPH 6:11 See also 2CO 2:10–2:11

He is a slanderer

JOB 1:9–1:11 See also 1TI 5:14–5:15; REV 2:9

Satan's deceitful work

He deceives individuals

1TI 2:14 Eve was deceived by Satan the serpent. See also DAN 8:25; 2TI 3:13; REV 12:9, REV 20:3, REV 20:10

He works counterfeit miracles

2TH 2:9–2:10 See also EXO 7:11–7:12, EXO 7:22; DEU 13:1–13:2; MAT 24:24 parallel MRK 13:22; REV 13:13–13:14, REV 19:20

He appoints false prophets

MAT 7:15 See also DEU 13:5; JER 23:26, JER 28:15, JER 29:21; EZK 13:6–13:10; 2PE 2:1–2:3, 2PE 2:18–2:19

He misuses Scripture

MAT 4:6 parallel LUK 4:10

He blinds unbelievers

2CO 4:4

title: 4124: Satan, kingdom of
content:
Satan is depicted as the prince of this world, having power over demons and over godless people. He is, however, subject to God's sovereignty and therefore his power is limited and his rule temporary.

Satan's power

He has power in the world

1JN 5:19 See also JHN 7:7, JHN 8:44, JHN 14:17, JHN 15:18–15:19, JHN 16:20, JHN 17:14; ACT 26:18; 2CO 4:4; EPH 2:2; COL 1:13; 1JN 2:15–2:16, 1JN 4:4–4:5; REV 13:12

He claims power over this world

MAT 4:8–4:9 parallel LUK 4:6

He is called the prince'' or thegod'' of this world

JHN 12:31 JHN 14:30, JHN 16:11; ; 2CO 4:4

He is responsible for some illnesses

LUK 13:16 See also JOB 2:7; ACT 10:38; 2CO 12:7 Paul's “thorn in the flesh” may or may not have been a physical illness.

He is responsible for death

HEB 2:14

Satan's kingdom

He has a kingdom

MAT 12:26 parallel LUK 11:18COL 1:13 See also ACT 26:18; EPH 2:2

He has a throne

REV 2:13

He is the prince of demons

MAT 9:34 The name Beelzebub is used elsewhere to name the prince of the demons. This name was used also to identify Satan. See also MAT 12:24 parallels MRK 3:22, LUK 11:15

Satan's authority is limited

He cannot do more than God allows

JOB 1:12 See also JOB 2:6; 1CO 10:13; REV 12:12, REV 20:3

His rule is temporary

ROM 16:20; ; REV 20:10

title: 4125: Satan, agents of
content:
Satan works in this world through people and through those spiritual beings who acknowledge his authority. His agents oppose God's will and purposes.

Satan works through people

Satan's human agents pursue his evil desires

JHN 8:44 Jesus Christ speaking of the relationship of some unbelieving Jews to Satan. See also ACT 13:10; 1JN 3:8–3:10, 1JN 3:12

Satan works through people to oppose God's purposes

ACT 5:1–5:9 Ananias and Sapphira; ; MAT 16:23 parallel MRK 8:33 Simon Peter; LUK 22:3; ; JHN 6:70, JHN 13:2, JHN 13:27; ACT 13:8–13:10 Elymas, the sorcerer

Examples of people doing Satan's work

False prophets

DEU 13:5; ; JER 23:26–23:27, JER 28:15, JER 29:21; ; EZK 13:6–13:9; ; MAT 7:15, MAT 24:24; ; 2PE 2:1–2:3

Others who lead people away from God

DEU 13:13; ; 2KI 21:9–21:26 Manasseh and his son, Amon; ; PRO 7:21 the adulteress; ; ISA 3:12; ; JER 50:6; ; 2PE 2:18–2:19

Satan works through his spiritual agents

Satan's angels

MAT 25:41 See also 2PE 2:4; REV 12:7 “angel” and “messenger” are the same word in Greek

Satan's demons

LUK 13:11–13:16 See also MAT 12:22; LUK 11:14–11:15

Satan's agents are identified by various images and titles

The little horn

DAN 8:9–8:12 This probably represents Antiochus Epiphanes.

The prince of Persia

DAN 10:13 probably representing a demon with influence over the Persian kingdom

The king of the North

DAN 11:28 probably representing Antiochus Epiphanes

The antichrist

1JN 4:1–4:4; ; 2JN 1:7

The man of lawlessness

2TH 2:3–2:10

The beast

REV 11:7, REV 13:1–13:8, REV 13:11–13:18, REV 14:9–14:11, REV 16:13, REV 17:8, REV 19:20, REV 20:10

The false prophet

REV 16:13, REV 19:20

The prostitute or Babylon the Great

REV 14:8, REV 16:19, REV 17:1–17:18, REV 18:1–18:10, REV 18:18, REV 19:2

title: 4126: Satan, resistance to
content:
Jesus Christ resisted Satan in his ministry, and believers are commanded to follow his example in this respect. God assists believers in this struggle.

Satan can be resisted

His power is limited

JOB 1:12, JOB 2:6; ; 1CO 10:13; ; REV 12:12, REV 20:3

Jesus Christ resists Satan

MAT 4:1–4:11 parallel LUK 4:1–4:13; ; JHN 1:5; ; HEB 2:18, HEB 4:15

Christians can resist Satan

JAS 4:7 See also LUK 10:19; ROM 6:13; EPH 4:26–4:27, EPH 6:11–6:13; 1PE 5:8–5:9; 2PE 3:17; 1JN 2:13–2:14

God protects his people from Satan

2TH 3:3 See also 1JN 5:18

God's protection is to be sought in prayer

JHN 17:15 See also MAT 6:13; LUK 22:31–22:32; EPH 6:11, EPH 6:18

Jesus Christ has the victory over Satan

Jesus Christ's defeat of Satan on the cross

COL 2:15 See also JHN 12:31; REV 12:11

Jesus Christ's name is powerful against Satan

PHP 2:10 See also ISA 45:23; PSA 118:10–118:12; MRK 16:17–16:18; LUK 10:17; ACT 16:18

God's love cannot be thwarted

ROM 8:38–8:39

Resisting Satan brings reward

2TI 4:7–4:8 See also JAS 1:12; REV 2:7, REV 2:17, REV 2:26, REV 3:5, REV 3:12, REV 3:21

title: 4127: Satan, defeat of
content:
Though defeated on Jesus Christ's cross, Satan is still active in this world, but his final overthrow by God is certain.

Satan is defeated by Jesus Christ

Jesus Christ is stronger than Satan

JHN 14:30 See also MAT 12:28; LUK 13:16; ACT 10:38; GAL 1:4

Jesus Christ came to destroy Satan's work

1JN 3:8 See also HEB 2:14–2:15

Satan is defeated on the cross

COL 1:13; ; TIT 2:14; ; REV 3:21

Satan's defeat is foreseen by Jesus Christ

JHN 12:31 See also MAT 25:41; LUK 10:18; JHN 16:11

Satan's agents are also defeated

COL 2:15 See also ROM 8:38–8:39

Satan is defeated by Christians

REV 12:11 See also ACT 5:40–5:42; 1JN 2:13–2:14

Satan's final overthrow

Satan and his agents will be destroyed

ROM 16:20 See also REV 17:14

Satan's final destiny

1TI 3:6 judgment; REV 20:1–20:3, REV 20:10

Death, the result of Satan's work, will be defeated

1CO 15:26; ; REV 20:13–20:14

resource.label: 9511: hell, as a place of punishment

title: 4130: demons
content: Evil spiritual forces; Scripture stresses the reality of demonic forces and recognises a number of categories to which they may be assigned. Jesus Christ's authority over such demonic forces is seen at work in his ministry and in that of his disciples.

title: 4131: demons, kinds of
content:
Scripture uses a number of terms to refer to demonic forces, stressing both their reality and hostility to God.

Demons

DEU 32:17; 1CO 10:20–10:21 See also PSA 106:37; MAT 8:16; MRK 7:26; ROM 8:38; JAS 2:19; REV 9:20

Evil spirits

MAT 10:1; ACT 5:16, ACT 8:7 See also MAT 12:43–12:45; MRK 1:23–1:26, MRK 3:11; REV 18:2

Other terms for demons

Spirits

ACT 16:16 See also MRK 9:17

Spirits of demons

REV 16:14

Deceiving spirits

1TI 4:1

Authorities, powers and forces

EPH 6:12; COL 2:15; 1PE 3:22

title: 4132: demons, malevolence of
content:
Demons are opposed to the person and work of God and seek to lead human beings, including believers, away from him. For this reason, believers are forbidden to be involved with them.

Demons harm individuals

Demons cause torment and pain

Saul's torment: 1SA 16:14–16:16, 1SA 16:23, 1SA 18:10–18:11, 1SA 19:9–19:10; MAT 15:22, MAT 17:15 parallels MRK 9:18–9:20, LUK 9:39–9:42 the boy with an evil spirit; ; MRK 5:5; ; LUK 6:18, LUK 13:11; ; ACT 5:16, ACT 19:16 the seven sons of Sceva

Demons cause dissension

JDG 9:23–9:25

Demons cause illness and handicaps

MRK 9:17–9:27 parallels MAT 17:14–17:18, LUK 9:37–9:42 See also MAT 9:32–9:33; LUK 11:14, LUK 13:11

Demons are deceitful

Demons inspire false prophets

1KI 22:21–22:23 parallel 2CH 18:21–18:22

Demons incite war

REV 16:13–16:14

Demons lead people away from God

1JN 4:1–4:6 See also 1TI 4:1–4:2; REV 16:13–16:14

The inspiration of pagan religions by demons

Pagan gods are identified as demons

1CO 10:20–10:21 See also DEU 32:16–32:17; PSA 106:36–106:39; REV 9:20

Demons inhabit ruins of pagan cities

REV 18:2

All involvement with spirits and mediums is forbidden

LEV 19:31 See also EXO 22:18; LEV 20:6, LEV 20:27; DEU 18:10–18:14; JER 27:9

Witchcraft is identified as a work of the sinful nature

GAL 5:19–5:20

Involvement with sorcery or witchcraft is futile

ISA 47:9 See also 1SA 28:7, 1SA 28:14–28:20; ISA 8:19–8:22, ISA 19:3–19:4, ISA 47:12–47:15; NAM 3:3–3:4

Examples of renunciation of the work of demons

ACT 19:19 See also 1SA 28:9; 2KI 23:24

title: 4133: demons, possession by
content:
A recognised condition in Jesus Christ's lifetime in which an individual is dominated by the influence of a demon. In his ministry, Jesus Christ encountered and delivered many people in such a condition. Many of Christ's critics unsuccessfully sought to ascribe his authority and powers to demonic possession.

Unfounded accusations of demon-possession

Against John the Baptist

MAT 11:18 parallel LUK 7:33

Against Jesus Christ

JHN 10:20–10:21 See also MAT 9:34, MAT 12:24 parallels MRK 3:22, LUK 11:15; JHN 7:20, JHN 8:48–8:52

Unfounded accusations of demon-possession against Jesus Christ are refuted

MAT 12:25–12:28 parallels MRK 3:23–3:30, LUK 11:17–11:20; ; JHN 10:21

Demon-possession arising from sin

1SA 18:9–18:10

The consequences of demon-possession

Irrational behaviour

1SA 18:10–18:11 See also 1SA 19:9–19:10

Unnatural physical strength

MRK 5:4 parallels MAT 8:28, LUK 8:29 See also ACT 19:16

Self-harm

MRK 5:5

Violent behaviour

MRK 9:18 parallel LUK 9:39 See also MAT 8:28 parallels MRK 5:3–5:5, LUK 8:27–8:29

The impairment of faculties

MAT 12:22 parallel LUK 11:14 See also MAT 9:32; MRK 9:17; LUK 13:11

Suffering

MAT 15:22 parallel MRK 7:25–7:26 See also 1SA 16:14–16:16; MAT 17:15 parallels MRK 9:18, LUK 9:39; LUK 6:18

Jesus Christ delivers from demon-possession

MAT 8:16 parallels MRK 1:32, LUK 4:40 See also MAT 4:24, MAT 9:32, MAT 12:22, MAT 15:22 parallel MRK 7:25–7:26

Multiple demon-possession

Jesus Christ encounters cases of multiple demon-possession and is able to deliver

MRK 16:9 See also MRK 5:1–5:17 parallel LUK 8:26–8:35; LUK 8:2

Further evidence of the possibility of multiple demon-possession

MAT 12:43–12:45 parallel LUK 11:24–11:26

Demons recognise divine authority and their own limited powers

JAS 2:19

Demons know Jesus Christ's identity

MRK 1:24 parallel LUK 4:34 See also MAT 8:29 parallels MRK 5:7, LUK 8:28; MRK 1:34 parallel LUK 4:41; MRK 3:11; ACT 19:15

Demons recognise Jesus Christ's disciples

ACT 16:16–16:17

Jesus Christ's disciples have power to release from demon-possession by calling on Jesus' name

See also ACT 5:16, ACT 19:13–19:16

title: 4134: demons, exorcism of
content:
Jesus Christ drove out many demons during his earthly ministry and he delegated this authority to his disciples.

Jesus Christ understood exorcism as part of his ministry

LUK 13:32 See also LUK 4:18

He drove out demons from individuals brought to him

MAT 8:16 parallels MRK 1:32–1:34, LUK 4:40–4:41 See also MAT 4:24

He drove out demons from individuals whom he encountered

MRK 1:23–1:26 parallel LUK 4:33–4:35 See also MRK 1:39

Jesus Christ had power over demons

He was able to drive demons out at a distance

MAT 15:22–15:28 parallel MRK 7:25–7:30

He used words of rebuke to drive out demons

MAT 17:18 parallels MRK 9:25, LUK 9:42 See also MRK 1:25 parallel LUK 4:35; LUK 4:41

Jesus Christ identifies the spiritual resources needed for exorcising demons

The Spirit of God

MAT 12:28 parallel LUK 11:20

Faith

MAT 17:19–17:20

Prayer (and fasting)

MRK 9:28–9:29

The ministry of exorcism was an indication of the kingdom's presence

MAT 12:28 parallel LUK 11:20

The results of being delivered from demon-possession

Visible transformation and healing

MRK 5:15 parallel LUK 8:35 See also MAT 9:33; LUK 11:14, LUK 13:13

Devotion to Jesus Christ

LUK 8:1–8:2 See also MRK 5:18 parallel LUK 8:38

Loss of supernatural faculties

ACT 16:16–16:19

Demons seek to return

MAT 12:43–12:45 parallel LUK 11:24–11:26

The impact of exorcism on those who observe it

Amazement

MAT 9:33, MAT 12:23; ; MRK 1:27 parallel LUK 4:36; ; LUK 11:14

Fear

MAT 8:34 parallels MRK 5:17, LUK 8:37

title: 4135: demons, Jesus Christ's authority over
content:
Demons are subject to the authority of Jesus Christ during his ministry and subsequently in the ministry of the church.

All spiritual powers are subject to Jesus Christ who has supreme authority

EPH 1:19–1:22 See also ROM 8:38; EPH 3:10; COL 1:16, COL 2:10, COL 2:15

Demons are powerless before Jesus Christ

Demons can only do what he permits

MAT 8:31–8:32 parallels MRK 5:12–5:13, LUK 8:32–8:33

Demons obey his command

MAT 17:18 parallels MRK 9:25–9:26, LUK 9:42 See also MAT 9:33–9:34; MRK 1:34, MRK 16:9; LUK 11:14

Demons disperse at his word

MRK 9:25 See also MRK 1:25 parallel LUK 4:35

The name of Jesus Christ has power against demons

Jesus Christ's name is used by his disciples

MRK 16:17 See also MRK 9:38–9:39 parallel LUK 9:49–9:50; LUK 10:17; ACT 16:18

Jesus Christ anticipates the misuse of his own name

MAT 7:22–7:23 See also ACT 19:13–19:16 an example where Jesus' name is used in cases of demon-possession--but not by Jesus Christ's disciples, and not successfully

Jesus Christ's disciples have delegated power over demons

In Jesus Christ's own lifetime

MAT 10:1 See also MAT 10:7; MRK 3:14–3:15, MRK 6:7 parallel LUK 9:1; MRK 6:13; LUK 10:17, LUK 10:19

In the ongoing ministry of the church

MRK 16:17 See also ACT 5:16, ACT 8:7, ACT 16:18, ACT 19:12

Others will attempt to drive out demons

MAT 12:27 parallel LUK 11:19; ; MRK 9:38 parallel LUK 9:49; ; ACT 19:13–19:15

The final punishment of demons

2PE 2:4; ; JUD 1:6

title: 4140: angel of the Lord
content:
A title given to those angelic servants of God who are given specific tasks in furthering God's purposes among humanity. These tasks include the giving of revelations, guidance, encouragement, warnings and the execution of judgment on those who oppose God's purposes.

The status of the angel of the Lord

He is identified with the Lord

EXO 3:2–3:6 The angel of the LORD here speaks for God and is recognised as God and as a messenger from God. This may indicate that the angel speaks on God's behalf (compare with the role of the prophets) or, as some believe, this may be a manifestation of the pre-incarnate Jesus Christ. See also GEN 16:7, GEN 16:13, GEN 31:11–31:13; JDG 2:1–2:2, JDG 6:11–6:12, JDG 6:14, JDG 13:19–13:22; ZEC 3:1–3:2

He is distinguished from the Lord

2SA 24:16 parallel 1CH 21:15 See also ZEC 1:12–1:13; LUK 1:19; HEB 1:4

The angel of the Lord is active in various ways

He calls and speaks

GEN 16:7–16:8, GEN 21:17, GEN 22:11, GEN 22:15; ; 2KI 1:3, 2KI 1:15

He has physical contact with people

1KI 19:5, 1KI 19:7; ; ACT 12:7

He appears in human likeness

GEN 18:2, GEN 18:16, GEN 18:22, GEN 19:1; ; JDG 6:11–6:12; ; ACT 27:23

He appears in supernatural form, often evoking fear

EXO 3:2; ; NUM 22:22–22:24, NUM 22:31; ; 1CH 21:16; ; LUK 1:11–1:12, LUK 2:9

He appears in dreams and visions

GEN 31:11; ; ZEC 1:8, ZEC 1:11, ZEC 3:1, ZEC 3:5; ; MAT 2:13, MAT 2:19; ; ACT 10:3

Functions of the angel of the Lord

He reveals information, often about forthcoming births

JDG 13:3 See also MAT 1:20; LUK 1:11, LUK 1:13

He gives guidance and instruction

ACT 8:26 See also GEN 16:9, GEN 22:11–22:12, GEN 24:7, GEN 24:40; EXO 23:23; NUM 22:35

He comforts those in need

GEN 21:17 See also 1KI 19:5–19:7; ACT 27:23–27:25

He affirms promises and gives blessing

GEN 22:15–22:18 See also LUK 1:26–1:28, LUK 1:30–1:33, LUK 2:10–2:11

He leads, delivers and protects Israel

PSA 34:7 See also EXO 14:19; ISA 63:9

He rebukes sinful Israel

JDG 2:1–2:4

He rolls back the tombstone

MAT 28:2

He rescues those in prison

ACT 5:19, ACT 12:8–12:9

He executes judgment on sin

ACT 12:23 See also 2SA 24:15–24:16 parallel 1CH 21:14–21:15; 2KI 19:35 parallel ISA 37:36; PSA 35:4–35:6

title: 4145: archangels
content:
The archangel associated with the second coming

1TH 4:16

The archangel Gabriel

Gabriel as an interpreter of visions

DAN 8:15–8:26 See also DAN 9:20–9:27

Gabriel as a messenger of God

LUK 1:11–1:20 See also LUK 1:26–1:38

The archangel Michael

Michael described as a prince

DAN 10:12–10:13 See also DAN 10:21

Michael depicted as a warrior and protector

DAN 12:1 See also REV 12:7

Associated with the death of Moses

JUD 1:9

title: 4150: cherubim
content:
Winged, heavenly creatures represented in the tabernacle and the temple.

Description of the cherubim

EZK 10:12–10:14 See also EZK 1:4–1:14, EZK 1:22–1:24, EZK 10:5, EZK 10:8, EZK 10:21–10:22, EZK 41:18–41:19; REV 4:6–4:8

Cherubim represented in the tabernacle and the temple

Cherubim as decorations

EXO 26:1 See also EXO 36:8; 1KI 6:29 parallel 2CH 3:7; 1KI 7:29, 1KI 7:36

Cherubim at the entrance to the inner sanctuary

EXO 26:31–26:33, EXO 36:35; ; 1KI 6:32, 1KI 6:35; ; 2CH 3:14

Cherubim over the ark of the covenant

HEB 9:5 See also EXO 25:17–25:22, EXO 37:7–37:9; NUM 7:89; 1KI 6:23–6:28 parallel 2CH 3:10–3:13; 1KI 8:6–8:7 parallel 2CH 5:7–5:8; 1CH 28:18

Cherubim represented in Ezekiel's vision of the temple

EZK 41:18, EZK 41:20, EZK 41:25

Cherubim represent the place of God's throne

1SA 4:4 See also 2SA 6:2 parallel 1CH 13:6; 2KI 19:15 parallel ISA 37:16; PSA 80:1, PSA 99:1; EZK 10:1; REV 4:6, REV 5:6

Cherubim as guardians

GEN 3:24 See also EZK 28:14, EZK 28:16

Cherubim as God's chariot

2SA 22:11 parallel PSA 18:10 See also 1CH 28:18

Cherubim as agents of judgment

Cherubim remove God's glory

EZK 9:3, EZK 10:3–10:4, EZK 10:15–10:19, EZK 11:22–11:23

God's judgment poured out

EZK 10:2, EZK 10:6–10:7; ; REV 6:1–6:8 the living creatures release the four horsemen of God's judgment on earth; ; REV 15:7

title: 4155: divination
content:
Attempts to discover the unknown or the future by supernatural means, involving communication with the spirit world. It is expressly forbidden by God and has disastrous consequences.

Divination was part of early beliefs and practices

GEN 41:24; ; EXO 7:11; ; ISA 31:1–31:2, ISA 47:9; ; JER 27:9; ; EZK 21:21; ; DAN 2:2, DAN 5:7

Examples of divination in NT times

ACT 8:9–8:13, ACT 8:18–8:24, ACT 13:6–13:12, ACT 16:16–16:18

The reality of a supernatural spirit world

The spirit world is real and alluring

ACT 8:11 See also GEN 44:5, GEN 44:15; 1SA 6:2; ACT 8:9–8:10

The spirit world is powerful and dangerous

2KI 9:22 See also NUM 22:6–22:7, NUM 22:12; 2KI 17:17; ZEC 10:2; EPH 6:12; 2TH 2:9–2:10; REV 18:23

Deliberate involvement with divination forbidden

LEV 19:31 See also EXO 22:18; LEV 19:26; DEU 18:10–18:14; 2KI 21:6; ISA 8:19–8:20; EZK 13:23; MIC 5:12; MAL 3:5; GAL 5:20

It is God's divine privilege to reveal or conceal things

DEU 29:29 See also PRO 25:2

Examples of the evil of divination

1SA 28:7 See also 1SA 28:3–28:15

Casting lots to make decisions

Divination is clearly forbidden but some practices such as casting lots seem acceptable ways of discovering God's will in particular circumstances: 1CH 26:13–26:16; ; NEH 11:1; ; ACT 1:26

title: 4160: driving out
content:
Driving out demons

In Jesus Christ's ministry

MRK 1:34 See also MAT 8:16, MAT 9:32–9:33 parallel LUK 11:14; MRK 1:39; LUK 13:32

Jesus Christ accused of using satanic power to drive out demons

MAT 12:24–12:28 parallels MRK 3:22–3:26, LUK 11:15–11:20 See also MAT 9:34

Jesus Christ commissioned his disciples to drive out demons

MAT 10:1 parallel LUK 9:1 See also MAT 10:8; MRK 3:15, MRK 6:13

The failure of the disciples to drive out demons

MRK 9:18

Others attempted to use Jesus Christ's authority

LUK 9:49; ; ACT 19:13

Driving out demons is no substitute for obedience to the will of God

MAT 7:22

Driving out demons and Satan as an aspect of Jesus Christ's overall ministry

JHN 12:31

Driving out people

NUM 33:51–33:52 See also EXO 10:11; PSA 44:2; PRO 19:26, PRO 22:10; JER 29:2; MAT 21:12

title: 4165: exorcism
content:
The expulsion of an evil spirit or spirits from a person. Jesus Christ has unique authority as an exorcist. He delegated this authority to the disciples.

Exorcism was practised with varying degrees of success by Jews and pagans

MAT 7:22, MAT 12:27 parallel LUK 11:19; ; ACT 19:13–19:16

Jesus Christ had unique authority as an exorcist

MRK 1:27 parallel LUK 4:36MAT 12:25–12:28 parallel LUK 11:17–11:18 The authority Jesus Christ exercised over evil spirits demonstrated that God's kingly authority was present in him. See also MAT 12:29 parallels MRK 3:27, LUK 11:21–11:22 Jesus Christ is the “strong man”. His power over evil spirits shows that Satan is defeated.

Jesus Christ had a general ministry of exorcism

MAT 8:16 parallels MRK 1:34, LUK 4:41; ; MRK 1:39; ; LUK 6:17–6:18, LUK 7:21

Jesus Christ gave the apostles power to cast out evil spirits

MAT 10:1 parallels MRK 6:7, LUK 9:1; ; MRK 3:14–3:15, MRK 16:17; ; LUK 10:17

After the ascension the apostles cast out evil spirits

ACT 5:16, ACT 19:12

Various symptoms associated with the need for exorcism

Violent or self-destructive behaviour

MRK 5:1–5:5 parallel LUK 8:27–8:29

Deafness and muteness

MAT 9:32; ; MRK 9:17; ; LUK 11:14

Seizures

MAT 17:14–17:15 parallels MRK 9:17–9:18, LUK 9:38–9:39

Various components of exorcism

A word of command

MRK 1:25 parallel LUK 4:35 See also MAT 8:32; MRK 9:25; ACT 16:18

The demons recognise the source of the authority addressing them

MRK 1:24 parallel LUK 4:34 See also MAT 8:29 parallels MRK 5:7, LUK 8:28; ACT 16:17

Exorcism was often accompanied by an element of violence or trauma

MRK 1:26 See also MRK 9:26; MAT 8:32 parallels MRK 5:13, LUK 8:32–8:33; ACT 19:16

Exorcism changes the behaviour or condition of the person delivered

MRK 5:15 parallel LUK 8:35 See also MAT 9:33 parallel LUK 11:14

An exorcised person may be ``repossessed''

MAT 12:43–12:45 parallel LUK 11:24–11:26

Methods of exorcism other than the word of command

Exorcism may be achieved at a distance

MAT 15:21–15:28 parallel MRK 7:24–7:30; ; ACT 19:12

On rare occasions an object is used

ACT 19:12 This was an unusual demonstration of power in a city noted for magic.

David's music brought relief to Saul

1SA 16:14–16:16, 1SA 16:23

Intensive prayer is sometimes necessary

MRK 9:29

title: 4170: host of heaven
content:
God's angelic attendants

PSA 148:2 See also 1KI 22:19–22:22 parallel 2CH 18:18–18:21; PSA 103:21; LUK 2:8–2:15

The stars in the sky

Created by God

PSA 33:6 See also NEH 9:6; ISA 40:26, ISA 45:12

Objects of worship

2KI 23:4–23:5 See also 2KI 17:16, 2KI 21:3–21:5 parallel 2CH 33:3–33:5; JER 19:13; ZEP 1:4–1:5

Portrayed symbolically

DAN 8:10 See also ISA 34:4

title: 4175: mediums
content:
People who claim to be channels through whom the spirits of the dead speak to the living.

God's people must not be mediums

DEU 18:10–18:12

God's people must not consult mediums

ISA 8:19–8:20 See also LEV 19:31, LEV 20:6; 2KI 23:24; JER 27:9

Examples of those who turned to mediums

King Saul

1SA 28:3–28:16; ; 1CH 10:13

King Manasseh

2KI 21:6 parallel 2CH 33:6

The Egyptians

ISA 19:3

title: 4180: seraphim
content:
An order of angels, mentioned only in one OT passage. Seraphim (the plural is also rendered seraphs) have a physical appearance and a moral and spiritual nature; they understand and use speech. Their functions include the worship of God and the communication of the assurance of forgiveness.

Their appearance

ISA 6:2 The covering of faces and feet expresses reverence, humility and awe before God.

Their functions

Worship of God

ISA 6:3 parallel REV 4:8

Communication with the prophet

ISA 6:6–6:7

title: 4185: sorcery and magic
content:
The attempt to discover or influence the future by means forbidden by God, including the use of divination, astrology or witchcraft. Such practices are strongly condemned in Scripture.

Examples of magic and sorcery

Magic practices

REV 18:23 Babylon, here, represents the world as the centre of seduction.

Divination

ZEC 10:2

Spiritism

ISA 8:19–8:20 See also 2CH 33:6

Astrology

2CH 33:3–33:5 parallel 2KI 21:3–21:5

Examples of those who practised sorcery

Egyptian magicians: EXO 7:11, EXO 8:18; NUM 22:6, NUM 23:23; 2KI 21:6 Manasseh, king of Judah; ; ISA 47:9–47:13 the Babylonians; ; ACT 8:9–8:11 Simon of Samaria; ; ACT 13:6–13:8 Bar-Jesus of Cyprus; ; ACT 19:19 people in Ephesus

All sorcery and magic is strongly forbidden

DEU 18:9–18:12 See also LEV 19:26, LEV 19:31; JER 27:9–27:10; EZK 13:18, EZK 13:20

God is stronger than sorcerers and magicians

EXO 7:11–7:12, EXO 8:16–8:19; ; ISA 44:24–44:25

God's judgment comes upon sorcerers and magicians

LEV 20:6; ; MIC 5:12; ; ACT 13:6–13:11; ; REV 21:8; ; GAL 5:19–5:21

God made participation in occult practices punishable by death

EXO 22:18; ; LEV 20:27

King Saul was judged for occult practices

1CH 10:13–10:14

Jesus Christ can deliver from occultism

ACT 16:16–16:18, ACT 19:18–19:19; ; ROM 8:38–8:39

title: 4190: spiritism
content:
The use of mediums or other spiritual intermediaries to communicate with the dead. Scripture forbids such practices, which are inconsistent with obedience to a living God.

Spiritism forbidden by God

LEV 19:31 See also DEU 18:10–18:12

Punishment for spiritism

LEV 20:27 See also LEV 20:6

Spiritism practised in Israel

2KI 21:6 parallel 2CH 33:6 See also 1SA 28:4–28:20

Spiritists expelled from Israel

2KI 23:24 See also 1SA 28:3

The futility of spiritism

ISA 8:19 See also ISA 19:2–19:3

title: 4195: spirits
content:
Intelligent beings which exist separately from bodies. God is spirit and he has given human beings a spiritual entity. He has also created a realm of spiritual beings--some have remained good, others have become evil. Evil spirits seek to control and destroy people, but Jesus Christ has the victory over them.

God is spirit

JHN 4:24 See also GEN 1:2; JOB 33:4; REV 1:4, REV 3:1, REV 4:5, REV 5:6

Other spirits

The spirit of human beings

PRO 20:27 See also JOB 32:8; ECC 12:7; LUK 8:55, LUK 23:46; JHN 3:6; ACT 7:59; JAS 2:26

The spirits of the dead

ISA 26:14 See also PRO 2:18; ISA 14:9; HEB 12:23

Angels

HEB 1:14 See also JDG 6:21–6:22, JDG 13:20–13:21; ZEC 6:5; ACT 23:8

Satan

JOB 1:6 See also JOB 2:1; 2CO 11:14; EPH 6:11–6:12

Demons

REV 16:13–16:14 See also LUK 6:18, LUK 7:21; ACT 23:8; 1PE 3:19; REV 18:2

The role of spirits

As instruments of punishment

1SA 16:14 See also JDG 9:23; 1SA 18:10, 1SA 19:9; 1KI 22:20–22:22 parallel 2CH 18:19–18:21

As instruments of testing to inspire faith in believers

1PE 5:8–5:9 See also JOB 1:8–1:12, JOB 1:22, JOB 2:3–2:6, JOB 2:10; MAT 4:1 parallels MRK 1:13, LUK 4:2; JAS 4:7–4:8

Evil spirits and humanity

Possession by spirits

MRK 9:17–9:18 parallel LUK 9:39 See also MAT 8:28 parallel LUK 8:27; MAT 15:22 parallel MRK 7:25; MRK 1:23 parallel LUK 4:33

Casting out spirits

MAT 8:16 parallels MRK 1:34, LUK 4:40 See also MAT 12:28 parallel LUK 11:20; MAT 17:18 parallels MRK 9:25–9:26, LUK 9:42; ACT 5:16, ACT 8:7

Consultation with spirits forbidden

DEU 18:10–18:11 See also LEV 19:31, LEV 20:6; 1SA 28:8–28:9; 2KI 21:6 parallel 2CH 33:6; ISA 8:19, ISA 19:3

Discernment and testing of spirits

1JN 4:1 See also 1CO 12:10; 1JN 4:2–4:6

Jesus Christ has supremacy over all created spirits

COL 2:10 See also MRK 1:24–1:27, MRK 1:34 parallel LUK 4:41; LUK 4:33–4:36, LUK 10:17–10:19; COL 2:15
