title: 4203: earth, the
content:
Created and sustained by God, the world is both his possession and the dwelling-place of his creatures. As such, it should bring him glory, and will one day be judged by him.

God created the earth from nothing

EXO 20:11; NEH 9:6; ACT 14:15 See also GEN 1:1–1:2; EXO 31:17; 2KI 19:15; JOB 26:7; PSA 74:17, PSA 115:15, PSA 119:90; ISA 44:24, ISA 45:12, ISA 48:13; JER 10:12, JER 27:5, JER 32:17, JER 33:2; AMO 4:13; JHN 1:3; ACT 4:24; HEB 11:3; 2PE 3:5; REV 10:6, REV 14:7

The earth belongs to God

DEU 10:14; PSA 24:1, PSA 95:4–95:5 See also EXO 9:29; JOS 3:13; 1SA 2:8; 1CH 29:11; PSA 47:9, PSA 89:11; 1CO 10:26

God controls and sustains the earth

PSA 135:6–135:7 See also GEN 9:16–9:17; JOB 5:10, JOB 28:5; PSA 104:13–104:14, PSA 147:8; JER 10:13, JER 33:25; HOS 6:3; COL 1:17; HEB 1:3

God's character seen on earth

PSA 33:5 See also PSA 85:11, PSA 119:64; ISA 6:3, ISA 11:9; JER 9:24

The earth should resound to God's glory and praise

PSA 96:1; ISA 44:23 See also PSA 47:2, PSA 66:4, PSA 72:9, PSA 108:5, PSA 148:7; ISA 49:13; HAB 3:3; REV 5:13

The earth is the habitation of human beings and the animals

PSA 115:16 See also GEN 1:22, GEN 1:26, GEN 1:28–1:30, GEN 8:17, GEN 9:1; ACT 17:26

God judges the earth

1CH 16:14; NAM 1:5 See also GEN 18:25; DEU 32:22; 1SA 2:10; 2SA 22:16; JOB 9:6; PSA 46:6, PSA 77:18; ISA 2:21, ISA 26:9; JER 10:10

God will destroy the earth one day

PSA 102:25–102:26; 2PE 3:10–3:12 See also ISA 24:1–24:6, ISA 51:6; JER 4:23–4:26; MAT 24:35 parallel MRK 13:31; 2PE 3:7; REV 20:11

God will create a new earth

2PE 3:13 See also ISA 65:17, ISA 66:22; REV 21:1

title: 4206: land
content: Areas of territory, the possession of which was regarded as a sign of security and prosperity. The land of Israel was regarded as the land promised by God to his people and a sign of his covenant faithfulness.

title: 4207: land, as a divine gift
content:
God's gift of the land of Israel is fundamental to the covenant promises made to Abraham and his descendants.

Land as part of God's creation

GEN 1:9–1:10

The land (Canaan) promised to Abraham

GEN 12:7 See also GEN 12:1, GEN 13:15–13:17, GEN 17:8, GEN 24:7

The boundaries of the promised land

GEN 15:18–15:21 See also EXO 23:31; NUM 34:1–34:12

God's promise and its gradual fulfilment

The promise made to the patriarchs

GEN 26:3–26:4 See also GEN 23:17–23:20, GEN 28:13, GEN 35:12, GEN 48:3–48:4, GEN 50:24–50:25 The patriarchs maintained only a very small part of the land. But Genesis closes with the prospect of much more.

The promise confirmed during the wilderness wanderings

EXO 3:7–3:8 See also EXO 6:4, EXO 6:8, EXO 13:5, EXO 13:11, EXO 32:11–32:14, EXO 33:1–33:3; NUM 11:10–11:12

Exploration of the promised land

NUM 13:1–13:2 See also NUM 13:21–13:25

Entry delayed because of unbelief and rebellion

NUM 13:27–13:33 See also NUM 14:1–14:4, NUM 14:26–14:35, NUM 14:40–14:45

Criticial importance of obedience in the promised land

DEU 5:32–5:33 Moses, at the end of his life, urges all the Israelites always to remember the LORD and his commands; enjoyment of the land is dependent upon the people's obedience. See also DEU 6:10–6:12

Invasion of the land

JOS 1:2 The theme of the book of Joshua is the land: chapters 1–12 its invasion and conquest, chapters 13–21 its division among the Israelite tribes. See also JOS 2:1

Gradual conquest of the land

JOS 13:1 See also JOS 7:2, JOS 8:1, JOS 9:1–9:2, JOS 10:29–10:30, JOS 13:2–13:6, JOS 14:12, JOS 17:12–17:18

The promise fulfilled

JOS 21:43–21:45

Division of the land

JOS 23:4–23:5

Conflict with the Philistines over the land

1SA 7:7–7:14 Philistine aggression had imperilled the prospect of a full possession of the land until David overpowered them. See also JDG 2:21–2:22, JDG 13:1, JDG 15:11; 1SA 4:10–4:11, 1SA 13:17–13:19, 1SA 17:1–17:2, 1SA 31:1, 1SA 31:7

Jerusalem captured by David

2SA 5:6–5:7

The land at rest and prosperous

Under David

2SA 7:1 See also 2SA 8:1–8:6, 2SA 8:14, 2SA 10:15–10:19

Under Solomon

1KI 5:4 See also 1KI 4:20–4:21

The exile and subsequent return to the land

Losing the land

LEV 26:14, LEV 26:32–26:33 See also DEU 28:63–28:65; JER 16:13, JER 17:4, JER 25:11, JER 44:22; LAM 5:2

Return to the land

JER 23:7–23:8 See also ISA 14:1, ISA 35:10, ISA 43:5–43:6; EZK 20:41–20:42, EZK 34:11–34:13; AMO 9:14–9:15

resource.label: 7135: Israel, as the people of God

title: 4208: land, as a divine responsibility
content:
Israel's land is a gift from God, though ultimately it remains under divine ownership. Life in the land is therefore characterised by both privilege and responsibility.

The land is a gift from God

DEU 26:5–26:9

It is Israel's inheritance

DEU 26:1 See also EXO 32:13; DEU 4:21, DEU 4:38, DEU 15:4, DEU 19:10

Eleven full tribes share the inheritance

NUM 26:52–26:55

Inherited land east of Jordan allotted by Moses

NUM 32:33 See also JOS 12:6, JOS 13:8, JOS 13:15, JOS 13:24, JOS 13:29

Inherited land west of Jordan allotted by Joshua and others

NUM 34:17–34:29 See also JOS 14:1, JOS 14:13, JOS 15:1, JOS 15:20, JOS 16:4, JOS 18:11, JOS 19:1, JOS 19:10, JOS 19:17, JOS 19:24, JOS 19:32, JOS 19:40, JOS 19:51

Levi receives no inheritance of land

NUM 26:62; ; DEU 18:1; ; JOS 13:14, JOS 13:33

Israelite households to hold their inheritance in perpetuity

NUM 36:7–36:9 See also 1KI 21:3; EZK 46:18

The land remains under divine ownership

LEV 25:23

Responsibility to God for the land

See also MAT 21:33–21:46

The tithe

LEV 27:30 The tithe of the land's produce given to the LORD, is a kind of rent, a way of acknowledging his ownership.

Harvest festivals

EXO 23:16 See also LEV 23:15–23:21, LEV 23:33–23:43

Sabbath rest

LEV 25:1–25:7

The Year of Jubilee

LEV 25:8–25:13

Responsibility to fellow Israelites in the land

Individual property rights to be respected

DEU 19:14 See also DEU 27:17; PRO 15:25, PRO 22:28, PRO 23:10; HOS 5:10

Violation of these rights severely condemned

ISA 5:8 See also 1KI 21:17–21:19; JOB 20:19; EZK 45:8–45:9; MIC 2:2, MIC 2:9

Responsibility to the poor in the land

The fallow (sabbath) year

EXO 23:10–23:11 See also LEV 25:6–25:7; DEU 15:1–15:3 Debts probably include the labour of a debtor's dependants, working to pay off his debt.

The Year of Jubilee

LEV 25:8–25:13

Harvesting conventions

LEV 19:9–19:10 See also LEV 23:22; DEU 24:19–24:22; RUT 2:2–2:3

The land reflects God's blessing and curse

Blessing

DEU 28:1–28:6, DEU 28:11–28:12 See also LEV 25:18–25:19; DEU 11:13–11:15; PSA 65:9–65:13, PSA 67:5–67:6

Curse

DEU 28:15, DEU 28:18, DEU 28:21 See also LEV 18:24–18:28, LEV 20:22–20:24, LEV 26:42–26:45; 1KI 17:1

title: 4209: land, spiritual aspects of
content:
Scripture presents land in a spiritual perspective. The whole earth, once perfect but now contaminated by the fall, will one day be renewed. There will be a new heaven and earth where life is abundant and God's presence is experienced for ever.

Eden: the original good land

GEN 2:8–2:9

Paradise lost: expulsion from the garden

GEN 3:17–3:19 See also ROM 8:19–8:20

The promised land: an echo of Eden

DEU 8:7–8:10 See also EXO 3:8, EXO 3:17, EXO 13:5; LEV 20:24; NUM 13:27; DEU 6:3, DEU 11:8–11:12, DEU 26:9, DEU 27:3

The land of Israel after the exile

Promise of a return to the land

AMO 9:14–9:15 See also ISA 14:1, ISA 35:1–35:10; EZK 20:41–20:42, EZK 34:11–34:13; AMO 9:11–9:12; ACT 15:16–15:17

Promise of a restored environment

ISA 51:3 See also ZEC 8:12–8:13

The final restoration of the land

ISA 65:17 See also ISA 66:22; ROM 8:21

Jesus Christ will reign eternally over new heavens and a new earth

ZEC 9:9–9:10 See also MAT 21:5; JHN 12:15; 2PE 3:13; REV 21:1–21:4, REV 22:1–22:5

Vision of life on the new earth

ISA 11:6–11:9 See also HOS 2:18–2:23; MIC 4:2–4:4

The new Eden: the Christian's inheritance

HEB 11:13–11:16 See also PSA 37:11; MAT 5:5; ROM 8:17; 2PE 3:13; REV 2:7, REV 21:7LUK 23:43; 2CO 12:1–12:4

title: 4212: astronomy
content:
The study of the stars and planets, with a view to understanding their movements and their use in fixing the time of significant events.

Astronomy as a study

It demonstrates the greatness of God and his creation

JOB 9:7–9:9; ISA 40:26 See also GEN 2:1; DEU 10:14; 1CH 16:26; NEH 9:6 Nehemiah's prayer reviews God's grace and power in creation; JOB 22:12, JOB 25:5, JOB 26:9; PSA 19:1–19:6, PSA 33:6, PSA 74:16, PSA 89:5, PSA 89:11, PSA 96:5, PSA 104:2, PSA 136:5, PSA 136:7–136:9, PSA 148:3; PRO 3:19 Divine wisdom permeates the whole creation; ISA 38:8 the LORD's sign to Hezekiah; ISA 45:12; JER 31:35; MAT 5:45; HEB 1:10 credited to Jesus Christ

It demonstrates the humility of the human race

PSA 8:3–8:4

The use of astronomy to determine dates

GEN 1:3–1:5; PSA 81:3, PSA 104:19

The mysteries of astronomy are beyond human understanding

JOB 38:31–38:33

God's judgment and astronomical disturbances

JOL 2:31; MAT 24:29 parallels MRK 13:25, LUK 21:25 See also ISA 13:10, ISA 24:23, ISA 30:26, ISA 34:4, ISA 51:6, ISA 60:9–60:20 the glory of Zion; EZK 32:7–32:8; JOL 2:10, JOL 3:15; AMO 8:9; ACT 2:20; 2PE 3:10; REV 8:12

Lessons from astronomy

The stars as symbols of plenty

GEN 15:5

The regularity of the heavens as a symbol of the bleakness of life

ECC 1:5

The differing splendour within the creation

1CO 15:41

Wandering stars as a symbol of lostness

JUD 1:13

title: 4215: Babylon
content:
In OT times, the commercial, religious and political capital of Babylonia, which was the dominant power in the Near East in the sixth century B.C. In the NT, “Babylon” signifies the world and its forces in opposition to God. It is often contrasted with “the new Jerusalem”, in which God will finally reign supreme.

The founding of Babylon

GEN 10:8–10:10 Shinar was an alternative name for Babylon.

Biblical events prior to the exile in Babylon

Inhabitants of Babylon exiled to Samaria

See also 2KI 17:24–17:33

Envoys from Babylon visit Hezekiah

2KI 20:12–20:19 parallel ISA 39:1–39:8 Hezekiah's reception of the envoys was designed to lead to a coalition against Assyria, but in the end it brought about the very opposite of what he had hoped for.

Manasseh, king of Judah, exiled to Babylon

2CH 33:10–33:11

Jehoiakim, king of Judah, becomes a vassal of Nebuchadnezzar, king of Babylon

2KI 24:1–24:2 parallel 2CH 36:6–36:7

Nebuchadnezzar lays siege to Jerusalem

2KI 24:10–24:17 parallel 2CH 36:9–36:10

Nebuchadnezzar conquers Jerusalem and deports the population

2KI 25:1–25:21 parallels 2CH 36:17–36:20, JER 39:1–39:10, JER 52:4–52:27

Exile in Babylon

The people of Judah exiled in Babylon

1CH 9:1 See also 2CH 36:20; PSA 137:1–137:9; DAN 1:1–1:7

Advice for those exiled in Babylon

JER 29:4–29:14

Cyrus king of Babylon decrees that the Jerusalem temple should be rebuilt

EZR 5:13–5:16 See also 2CH 36:22–36:23 parallel EZR 1:1–1:4

Exiles return from Babylon

EZR 2:1–2:2 parallel NEH 7:6–7:7

Babylon, an instrument of divine judgment

2KI 24:2–24:4 See also JER 20:4–20:6, JER 21:10

The fall of Babylon

Predictions of Babylon's fall

JER 51:59–51:64 See also ISA 14:3–14:23, ISA 47:1–47:15; JER 50:1–50:10

Babylon conquered by Darius

DAN 5:30–5:31

Babylon used figuratively

By Peter

See also 1PE 5:13 Babylon is traditionally interpreted as Rome, but may also mean Mesopotamian Babylon, Egyptian Babylon, or Jerusalem.

Of the world opposed to God

REV 14:8 See also REV 17:3–17:6, REV 18:1–18:24

title: 4218: cave
content:
A natural cavity in a rock face, often used as a place of refuge.

Caves as places of refuge

JDG 6:2; 1SA 22:1–22:2 See also GEN 19:30; JOS 10:16–10:18, JOS 10:22–10:23, JOS 10:27; JDG 15:8, JDG 15:11; 1SA 13:6, 1SA 24:2–24:22 Saul comes to David's cave, but David spares his life; 2SA 17:9, 2SA 23:13 parallel 1CH 11:15; 1KI 18:4, 1KI 18:13, 1KI 19:9; PSA 57:0, PSA 142:0; ISA 2:19–2:21 a place of refuge in the face of divine judgment; JER 48:28, JER 49:8; EZK 33:27; HEB 11:38 a place of refuge for those being persecuted for their faith; REV 6:15

Caves used as graves

JHN 11:38 See also GEN 23:8–23:20, GEN 25:9, GEN 49:29–49:32, GEN 50:13

title: 4221: cistern
content:
A reservoir for storing water collected from rainfall or from a spring. When empty, it was used for a variety of purposes. The word is also employed in a figurative sense.

Cisterns were used to collect water

2CH 26:10 See also 2KI 18:31 parallel ISA 36:16; JER 14:3

Other uses for cisterns

JER 38:6–38:13 See also GEN 37:19–37:24, GEN 37:28–37:30; JER 41:4–41:7, JER 41:9; ZEC 9:11

Cisterns used as symbols

Cisterns as symbols of spiritual well-being

JER 2:13

Cisterns as symbols of physical well-being

See also PRO 5:15–5:19

title: 4224: cities of the plain
content:
The cities of the plain of the River Jordan, chosen by Lot as a place of residence

GEN 13:10–13:12

Abraham rescues their inhabitants

See also GEN 14:1–14:24

Abraham prays for their inhabitants

GEN 18:17–18:33

Judgment on their sin

GEN 19:1–19:29

The cities of the plain as symbols of sin and judgment

DEU 29:23; LUK 17:28–17:29

title: 4227: deep, the
content:
Unfathomed waters, sometimes seen as existing under the earth, held back by God's power. The term is also used of deep seas, and in metaphorical contexts.

Unfathomed waters

GEN 1:2; PSA 104:6–104:9 See also GEN 7:11, GEN 49:25; DEU 33:13; JOB 38:16; PSA 95:4, PSA 139:15; PRO 3:20, PRO 8:27–8:28; ISA 7:11; ROM 10:7

Deep seas

EXO 15:5; JON 2:3 See also JOB 7:12, JOB 38:30; PSA 42:7; ISA 44:27, ISA 51:10; HAB 3:10; MAT 18:6; LUK 5:4

The deep used metaphorically of a desperate situation

PSA 18:16 See also 2SA 22:17; PSA 30:1, PSA 69:2, PSA 69:15, PSA 130:1

title: 4230: desert
content:
The desert as a harsh place

JER 2:6 See also ISA 25:4–25:5, ISA 49:10; JER 4:26, JER 22:6; HOS 13:5

The desert as a place of human activity

Living in the desert

GEN 21:21 See also JDG 1:16; PRO 21:19; JER 25:24; EZK 34:25; LUK 1:80

Grazing livestock in the desert

EXO 3:1 See also GEN 36:24; 1CH 5:9; JOB 24:5

Hiding in the desert

1SA 23:14–23:15 See also 1SA 23:25

EXO 14:11–14:12; JHN 6:49 See also EXO 16:3; NUM 3:4, NUM 14:29, NUM 32:13; 1CO 10:5; HEB 3:17

God leading and caring for his people in the desert

DEU 2:7; NEH 9:19–9:21; PSA 78:52 See also EXO 13:18, EXO 17:1, EXO 17:5–17:6; DEU 1:19, DEU 8:2, DEU 29:5, DEU 32:10; JOS 24:7; PSA 136:16; AMO 2:10; ACT 7:36, ACT 13:18

John the Baptist in the desert

MRK 1:4 See also MAT 3:3 parallels MRK 1:2–1:3, LUK 3:4; ISA 40:3

Jesus Christ in the desert

MAT 4:1 parallels MRK 1:12–1:13, LUK 4:1

Specific deserts

See also GEN 21:14 Beersheba; EXO 15:22 Shur; EXO 16:1 sin; EXO 19:1–19:2 Sinai; NUM 12:16 Paran; NUM 13:21 Zin; NUM 33:8 Etham; DEU 2:26 Kedemoth; JOS 18:12 Beth Aven; 1SA 23:24 Maon; 1SA 24:1 En Gedi; 1SA 26:2 Ziph; 1KI 19:15 Damascus; 2KI 3:8 Edom; 2CH 20:16 Jeruel; 2CH 20:20 Tekoa; PSA 29:8 Kadesh; PSA 63:0 Judah; MAT 3:1 Judea; ACT 8:26 the desert road from Jerusalem to Gaza

Deserts used figuratively

JER 2:31 See also ISA 35:6, ISA 40:3; JER 50:12; LAM 4:3; HOS 2:3

title: 4233: field of blood
content:
The field purchased by Judas Iscariot in which he subsequently died

ACT 1:18–1:19 See also MAT 27:3–27:10

title: 4236: fountain
content:
A source of natural spring water. Figuratively, it symbolises a place of cleansing, tears or sexual fulfilment. Significantly, God is pictured as a fountain of life.

The fountain as a source of spring water

PRO 18:4

Figurative use of fountains

A place of cleansing

ZEC 13:1

Tears of inconsolable grief

JER 9:1

Sexual fulfilment

SNG 4:12 See also PRO 5:15–5:18; SNG 4:15

A source of life

PRO 14:27 See also PSA 36:8–36:9, PSA 87:7; PRO 10:11, PRO 13:14, PRO 16:22; JOL 3:18

title: 4239: garden
content: A piece of land where flowers, fruit and vegetables may be grown. The Garden of Eden was the garden entrusted to the care of Adam and Eve.

title: 4240: garden, natural
content:
A place of leisure for growing flowers, fruit and vegetables.

General references to gardens

1KI 21:2; ECC 2:5 See also SNG 4:12, SNG 5:1, SNG 6:2; ISA 5:7; JER 29:5; LAM 2:6; AMO 4:9, AMO 9:14MAT 13:32; LUK 11:42, LUK 13:19

Gardens require irrigation

DEU 11:10; ISA 1:30 See also NUM 24:6; JOB 8:16; ISA 58:11; JER 31:12

Gardens attached to palaces

EST 1:5–1:6 See also 2KI 21:18, 2KI 21:26; NEH 3:15; EST 7:7; JER 39:4

Idolatrous worship in gardens

ISA 65:3 See also ISA 1:29, ISA 66:17

Jesus Christ betrayed in a garden

MAT 26:36 parallel MRK 14:32 Gethsemane was a garden.

Jesus Christ buried in a garden tomb

JHN 19:41–19:42

title: 4241: Garden of Eden, the
content:
The garden created by God and entrusted to the care of Adam and Eve.

The ``garden of the LORD''

GEN 2:8, GEN 13:10 See also GEN 2:9–2:14; ISA 51:3; EZK 31:9, EZK 31:18 Ancient Egypt is likened to Eden; EZK 36:35; JOL 2:3

The place of fellowship with God

GEN 3:8

Where God spoke to Adam

See also GEN 2:16–2:18

Where Adam was to work

GEN 2:15, GEN 2:19

Where God made Eve and gave her to Adam

GEN 2:18 See also GEN 2:20–2:24

Where Adam and Eve lived in innocence

GEN 2:25, GEN 3:7, GEN 3:11

Where God abundantly provided for Adam and Eve

GEN 2:9–2:17 The trees of Eden are important. They are both the evidence of God's love and one is the focus of Adam's test.

The paradise that humanity has lost

Where Adam and Eve disobeyed God

GEN 3:6 See also GEN 2:17, GEN 3:1–3:7, GEN 3:11

Where humanity came under God's curse

GEN 3:16–3:19

The place from which Adam and Eve were banished

GEN 3:23 See also GEN 3:22, GEN 3:24

The picture of God's blessing

ISA 51:3 See also ISA 58:11; JER 31:12

Where God first promised Satan's destruction

GEN 3:14–3:15

Where God first provided a covering for sinners

See also GEN 3:21

Eden as an anticipation of heaven

LUK 23:43 The word “paradise” (which means “park” or “walled garden”) clearly refers back to the idea of Eden. See also 2CO 12:3, 2CO 12:4; REV 2:7, REV 22:1–22:3; EZK 28:12–28:13 the former glory of the king of Tyre likened to that of Adam in the Garden of Eden

title: 4245: hills
content:
The promised land was a place of hills

The high ground to the east of Jordan

DEU 3:12 See also GEN 31:21

The high ground of Judah in the south

JOS 11:21 See also JOS 12:8; LUK 1:39

The high ground of Judah includes Jerusalem

JOL 3:17 See also PSA 2:6; ISA 10:32; DAN 9:16

The high ground to the north

JDG 3:27 See also JOS 20:7; 2KI 5:22

Topics associated with hills

Plenty

JOL 3:18 See also DEU 8:7–8:9; PSA 50:10, PSA 72:3, PSA 72:16; ISA 7:25; AMO 9:13

Age

JOB 15:7 See also GEN 49:26; DEU 33:15; PRO 8:25; HAB 3:6

Hiding

JOS 2:16; ; 1SA 23:14

Grazing

1KI 22:17; ; JER 33:13; ; EZK 34:6; ; MAT 18:12

Idolatrous worship

DEU 12:2 See also 1KI 11:7, 1KI 14:23; 2KI 17:10, 2KI 23:13; JER 13:27; HOS 4:13

Hills used metaphorically

MAT 5:14 See also PSA 114:4; ISA 40:12, ISA 55:12

title: 4248: islands
content:
Used of the islands and coastlands of the eastern Mediterranean and, especially by Isaiah, as a more general term indicating distant nations that are called upon to respond to God. Several specific islands are mentioned in the NT.

The islands and coastlands of the Mediterranean

ISA 11:11 See also ISA 42:10, ISA 60:9; JER 2:10–2:11, JER 31:10; EZK 26:15–26:18, EZK 27:3–27:7; DAN 11:18, DAN 11:29–11:30

Islands as a symbol of human insignificance before God

ISA 40:15–40:17

Islands as a symbol of distant nations

Nations who do not know God

ISA 66:19 See also ISA 41:1, ISA 49:1

Nations who are not beyond God's law and judgment

ISA 59:18 See also EZK 25:16–25:17, EZK 39:6; REV 6:12–6:14, REV 16:17–16:20

Nations called to respond to God

ISA 42:10–42:12 See also ISA 24:15, ISA 42:4, ISA 51:4–51:5

Islands mentioned in the NT

Cos

ACT 21:1

Crete

ACT 27:7–27:26; ; TIT 1:5, TIT 1:12

Cyprus

ACT 4:36, ACT 11:19–11:21, ACT 13:4–13:12, ACT 15:39, ACT 21:16

Kios

ACT 20:15

Malta

ACT 27:39–28:11

Patmos

REV 1:9

Rhodes

ACT 21:1

Samos

ACT 20:15

Samothrace

ACT 16:11

Sicily

ACT 28:12 Syracuse was the leading city of Sicily.

title: 4251: moon
content:
The moon created by God

PSA 8:3 See also PSA 74:16

The moon listed among the heavenly bodies

GEN 37:9; ; PSA 148:3; ; ECC 12:2; ; 1CO 15:41

The moon as a basis for the calendar

The moon governs the night

GEN 1:16–1:18 See also PSA 136:7–136:9; JER 31:35

Seasons marked by the moon

PSA 104:19 See also GEN 1:14

Months marked by the new moon

NUM 28:14; ; 1CH 23:31; ; ISA 66:23

The moon as a symbol of permanence

PSA 89:37 See also PSA 72:5, PSA 72:7ISA 60:19–60:20; REV 21:23

Idolatrous worship of the moon

DEU 4:19 See also DEU 17:3; 2KI 23:5; JOB 31:26; JER 8:2

Signs in the moon indicating divine intervention

At the close of history

ISA 13:10, ISA 30:26; ; JOL 2:10, JOL 2:31; ; ACT 2:20; ; MAT 24:29 parallel MRK 13:24–13:25; ; REV 6:12, REV 12:1

Other examples

JOS 10:12–10:13; ; EZK 32:7

The moon thought to affect the mind

PSA 121:6 The moon is indicated as a source of danger; ; MAT 4:24 The Greek expression translated “having seizures” originally meant “moonstruck”.

title: 4254: mountains
content:
These natural features of Palestine are often used as poetic symbols when describing God or his dealings with his people. Specific mountains are remembered because of their association with important events in the history of Israel. Some of the key events in the life and ministry of Jesus Christ take place on mountain sites.

Mountains and the character of God

Mountains and the steadfastness of God

PSA 125:2 See also PSA 11:1, PSA 90:2; ISA 54:10

Mountains tremble at God's awesome presence

JDG 5:5 The violent physical response to the presence of the holy God evokes a sense of his might and majesty. See also EXO 19:16–19:19; PSA 97:5; ISA 64:1; MIC 1:3–1:4; HAB 3:3–3:6

Mountains cannot stand before the anger of God

PSA 18:7 See also JER 4:23–4:26; NAM 1:5–1:6; REV 6:12–6:14, REV 16:19–16:20

Mountains as places of encounter with God

EXO 34:1–34:3 See also 1KI 19:11–19:13; 2PE 1:16–1:18

Specific mountains as the sites of important events in Israel's history

The mountains of Abarim: site of Israel's camp

NUM 33:47–33:48

Mount Ararat: resting place of Noah's ark

GEN 8:1–8:5

Mount Carmel: scene of Elijah's defeat of the prophets of Baal

1KI 18:16–18:40

Mount Ebal: site of cursing; Mount Gerizim: site of blessing

See also DEU 11:29, DEU 27:12–27:13; JOS 8:30–8:35

Mount Gilboa: place of Saul's suicide

1CH 10:1–10:12 parallels 1SA 31:1–31:13, 2SA 1:4–1:12

Mount Hermon: a boundary of Israel

See also DEU 3:8; JOS 11:16–11:17; PSA 42:6–42:7, PSA 89:12

Mount Hor: place of Aaron's death

NUM 33:37–33:39 See also NUM 20:22–20:29

Mount Moriah: testing place of Abraham

GEN 22:1–22:3

Mount Moriah: site of Solomon's temple

2CH 3:1

Mount Nebo: place of Moses' death

DEU 32:48–32:52 See also DEU 34:1–34:5

Mount Sinai: God gives Moses the law

EXO 24:12 See also EXO 31:18; NEH 9:13–9:14

Mount Tabor: scene of Sisera's defeat

JDG 4:12–4:16

Mountains as the sites of significant events in the NT

On the Mount of Temptation the devil offers Jesus Christ the kingdoms of the world

MAT 4:8–4:9 parallel LUK 4:5–4:7

In the Sermon on the Mount Jesus Christ teaches about life in the kingdom of God

MAT 5:1–5:2

On the Mount of Transfiguration Jesus Christ's glory is revealed

MAT 17:1–17:8 parallels MRK 9:2–9:8, LUK 9:28–9:36

On the Mount of Olives Jesus Christ teaches about the destruction of the temple and the last things

MAT 24:3 parallel MRK 13:3–13:4

title: 4257: pit
content:
A hole in the ground

MAT 15:14 See also EXO 21:33–21:34; 2SA 18:17, 2SA 23:20; MAT 12:11; MRK 12:1; LUK 6:39

A pit used as a trap

PSA 35:7–35:8 See also PSA 7:15, PSA 9:15, PSA 57:6, PSA 94:13; PRO 26:27; ECC 10:8; ISA 24:17–24:18; JER 18:20–18:22, JER 48:43–48:44; EZK 19:4, EZK 19:8

A pit as a symbol of distress or grief

PSA 40:2 See also JOB 9:30–9:31; PSA 88:6; PRO 22:14, PRO 23:27; LAM 3:53–3:55; ZEC 9:11

The pit as a term for the grave

ISA 14:15 See also JOB 33:18, JOB 33:22, JOB 33:24, JOB 33:28, JOB 33:30; PSA 28:1, PSA 30:3, PSA 30:9, PSA 55:23, PSA 69:15, PSA 88:4, PSA 103:4, PSA 143:7; PRO 1:12; ISA 14:19, ISA 38:17–38:18; EZK 26:20, EZK 28:8, EZK 31:14, EZK 31:16, EZK 31:18, EZK 32:23–32:25, EZK 32:29–32:30; JON 2:6

The pit as a symbol of judgment

ISA 30:33

title: 4260: rivers and streams
content:
The term river is used to refer to all kinds of watercourses, including wadis (dried-up desert river beds) and permanent rivers. It is also used symbolically to refer to things which threaten or overwhelm people and nations, such as invading nations, the peace of God and the Holy Spirit.

Different kinds of rivers and streams

Wadis

1KI 17:7 Wadis were dry river beds in summer but became raging torrents in the rainy season. See also GEN 32:22 The Jabbok was a wadi, here in full flow; NUM 21:13–21:15; JOB 6:15–6:17; JER 15:18

Springs

PSA 104:10 See also GEN 16:7; EXO 15:27; JDG 1:14–1:15; 2KI 2:19–2:22

Permanent rivers

GEN 2:10–2:14 See also GEN 41:1–41:3; NUM 22:1; DEU 1:7; ISA 18:1

Canals

DAN 8:2 See also EXO 7:19; EZR 8:21; EZK 1:1 Although called a river here, the Kebar was a canal of the Euphrates.

Some significant rivers and streams in Scripture

River Arnon

The Arnon flowed through Moab to the mid point of the Dead Sea: NUM 21:13–21:15; ; JDG 11:18; ; ISA 16:2; ; JER 48:20

Rivers of Damascus

2KI 5:12 The Abana flowed through the city of Damascus and the Pharpar just to the south of it.

River Euphrates

The Euphrates flowed through Mesopotamia for some 1,700 miles (2,700 km) from its source in eastern Turkey to the Persian Gulf: DEU 1:7; ; 2KI 23:29 parallel 2CH 35:20; ; ISA 11:15; ; JER 51:63–51:64; ; REV 16:12

River Habor

2KI 17:6 parallel 2KI 18:11 The Habor was a tributary of the Euphrates.

River Jabbok

The Jabbok flowed westward through Gilead into the Jordan about 20 miles (32 km) north of the Dead Sea: GEN 32:22; ; NUM 21:23–21:24; ; DEU 3:16; ; JDG 11:13

River Jordan

The Jordan flowed from the slopes of Mount Hermon through Lake Huleh and the Sea of Galilee, and on into the Dead Sea, providing a fertile basin on either side of the river: GEN 13:10–13:11, GEN 32:9–32:10; ; NUM 22:1; ; JOS 3:14–4:9; ; 2KI 5:8–5:14; ; MRK 1:4–1:5 parallel MAT 3:5–3:6

River Kebar

The Kebar was a canal of the Euphrates in Babylon where the exiles gathered to pray: EZK 1:1, EZK 3:15, EZK 43:1–43:3

River Kerith

1KI 17:2–17:7 The exact location of this wadi is uncertain, but it ran from the east into the Jordan or one of its tributaries.

River Kishon

The Kishon flowed north-west across the Plain of Megiddo and could quickly flood as a result of storms: JDG 4:6–4:7, JDG 4:13, JDG 5:21; ; 1KI 18:40; ; PSA 83:9

River Nile

The Nile flowed north some 3,500 miles (5,600 km) from Lake Victoria to the Mediterranean, providing a natural irrigation system in the desert through its annual floodings: EXO 1:22–2:10, EXO 7:17–7:21; ; ISA 19:5–19:8; ; EZK 29:3–29:5

River Tigris

The Tigris flowed from the Armenian mountains through Mesopotamia to join the Euphrates some 40 miles (65 km) north of the Persian Gulf: GEN 2:14; ; DAN 10:4–10:5; ; NAM 2:6–2:8

The crossing of rivers and streams

Rivers crossed by fords

JOS 2:7; ; JDG 3:28, JDG 12:5–12:6; ; 2SA 17:16

Rivers crossed by miraculous intervention from God

JOS 3:14–3:17; ; 2KI 2:7–2:8, 2KI 2:13–2:14

Human uses of rivers and streams

As boundaries

GEN 15:18 See also JOS 1:2, JOS 12:2; 1KI 4:21

For irrigation

DEU 11:10 The reference may be to a machine operated by foot, or to the foot breaking through the mud walls of the irrigation channel to let the water flow into the fields. See also EZK 17:5–17:8, EZK 19:10

As places of prayer

ACT 16:13 See also PSA 137:1–137:3; EZK 3:15

For ritual washing and baptism

2KI 5:10 See also MRK 1:5 parallel MAT 3:6; ACT 8:36–8:38

God as sovereign Creator is ruler and judge over all rivers and streams

PSA 74:15 See also PSA 78:13–78:16, PSA 107:33–107:35; ISA 19:5–19:8; REV 8:10–8:11, REV 16:12

The symbolic use of rivers and streams

A symbol of threatening circumstances

PSA 124:1–124:5 See also PSA 32:6, PSA 88:16–88:17

A symbol of invading nations

JER 47:2 See also ISA 8:7–8:8; JER 46:7–46:8

A symbol of God's judgment on his enemies

2SA 5:20 “Baal Perazim” means “the lord who breaks out”. The picture is of a river bursting its banks. See also JOB 20:28–20:29; ISA 59:19; NAM 1:7–1:8

A symbol of God's peace

ISA 48:18 See also ISA 66:12

A symbol of God's sustaining power

PSA 46:4–46:5 See also ISA 8:6

A symbol of God's provision and blessing

JOL 3:18 See also PSA 1:3, PSA 36:8–36:9; ISA 30:25

A symbol of renewal and life

ISA 41:18 See also EZK 47:1–47:12; REV 22:1–22:2

A symbol of the Holy Spirit

JHN 7:37–7:39 See also ISA 44:3; JHN 4:13–4:14

title: 4263: Rome
content:
The city state at the heart of the great world empire of NT times, pictured in the book of Revelation as the embodiment of corrupt earthly power. A large church had been established in Rome long before Paul arrived there, towards the end of his life and his mission. His letter to the believers in Rome includes a comprehensive explanation of the gospel.

Rome and the mission of Paul

Paul plans to visit Rome

ACT 19:21 See also ROM 1:13–1:15, ROM 15:23–15:29

Jesus Christ confirms that Paul will preach the gospel in Rome

ACT 23:11

Paul is sent for trial in Rome

ACT 25:10–25:12 See also ACT 27:1–27:2

Paul arrives in Rome

ACT 28:14–28:16

Paul preaches in Rome

ACT 28:30–28:31 See also ACT 28:23–28:24; PHP 1:12–1:13

The church in Rome existed long before Paul's arrival

ROM 15:23–15:24 See also ACT 28:15; ROM 1:8, ROM 16:3–16:16 Although Paul had never been to Rome, he seemed to be personally acquainted with 26 people associated with the church there, so its overall size was probably quite considerable.

Paul's letter to the church in Rome presents the whole gospel

ROM 1:16–1:17 The gospel is God's offer of salvation to all.

Sin and the universal need for salvation

ROM 3:23 See also ROM 3:10, ROM 6:23

God's ``rescue'' through Jesus Christ available to all

ROM 3:22 See also ROM 1:16

Peace with God through believing in Jesus Christ

ROM 5:1–5:2 See also ROM 4:23–4:25, ROM 5:8–5:10

Gentiles obtain salavation

ROM 10:11–10:13

Rome is pictured as the embodiment of corrupt earthly power

REV 17:1–17:18 Verse 9 clearly identifies the city as Rome, widely known then as the city built on seven hills. See also REV 14:8 Babylon was the great godless city of the OT and here is taken by most commentators to depict Rome, which in turn represents corrupt worldly power in general. However, some believe that only a general reference to the world system is in mind, and others that a literal reference to the actual city of Babylon is intended; REV 16:19, REV 18:1–18:24

title: 4266: sea
content:
The sea created and controlled by God

GEN 1:9–1:10; JER 5:22 See also PSA 95:5, PSA 107:29, PSA 146:6; ISA 50:2; AMO 5:8, AMO 9:6; ACT 4:24, ACT 14:15

Creatures of the sea

PSA 104:25 See also GEN 1:21; PSA 8:8; EZK 38:20; HOS 4:3; HAB 1:14

Sea monsters sometimes represented spiritual forces

PSA 74:13 See also JOB 26:12; ISA 27:1; REV 13:1

The sea represented hidden depths

AMO 9:3 See also 2SA 22:16; JOB 38:16; MIC 7:19; MAT 18:6

Named seas

The Red, or Reed, Sea

EXO 10:19 See also HEB 11:29

The Dead, or Salt, Sea

GEN 14:3 See also 2KI 14:25

The Sea of Galilee

NUM 34:11; JHN 6:1

The Great, or Mediterranean, Sea

NUM 34:6–34:7 See also EZK 47:10

The Adriatic Sea

ACT 27:27 In NT times referring to an area extending south of the present Adriatic Sea.

The Sea before the temple

See also 1KI 7:23–7:26, 1KI 7:39; 2KI 16:17, 2KI 25:13

The sea before the throne

REV 4:6, REV 15:2

Metaphorical references to the sea

ISA 17:12; JAS 1:6 See also JOB 7:12, JOB 11:9, JOB 14:11–14:12; PSA 93:4; ISA 5:30, ISA 11:9, ISA 48:18, ISA 57:20; JER 6:23, JER 49:23, JER 50:42, JER 51:42; LAM 2:13; EZK 26:3; ZEC 10:11; JUD 1:13

No more sea

REV 21:1

title: 4269: Sinai, Mount
content:
The mountain in the Sinai peninsula (probably present-day Jebel Musa), also called Horeb in the OT, where God made a covenant with Israel, establishing them as his people and giving them his laws. In the NT Sinai is seen as symbolising the old covenant.

Mount Sinai and the call of Moses

EXO 3:1–3:10 Most scholars agree that the names Horeb and Sinai are used interchangeably of the same mountain, although some see them as referring to separate peaks within the same range. See also ACT 7:30–7:34

God reveals himself to Israel at Mount Sinai

The people of Israel arrive at Mount Sinai three months after leaving Egypt

EXO 19:1–19:2

God promises to make Israel his people

EXO 19:3–19:6

God calls for holiness among his people

See also EXO 19:10–19:15, EXO 19:20–19:24

God appears in a theophany

EXO 19:16–19:19 See also DEU 4:10–4:12; HEB 12:18–12:21

God gives his laws to Israel on Mount Sinai

God gives Israel the Ten Commandments

EXO 20:1–20:17 parallel DEU 5:6–5:21

Israel responds with fear to God's revelation

See also EXO 20:18–20:21; DEU 5:23–5:27, DEU 18:16

God gives Israel the Book of the Covenant

EXO 20:22–23:19 These laws, largely an expansion and exposition of the Ten Commandments, are called “the Book of the Covenant” in Ex 24:7.

God establishes the covenant with Israel on Mount Sinai

Moses performs the ceremony establishing the covenant

EXO 24:4–24:8

Moses and other leaders ascend Mount Sinai to seal the covenant with a meal before God

EXO 24:9–24:11

Moses ascends Mount Sinai further to receive the stone tablets and the requirements of covenant worship

EXO 24:12–24:18 During Moses' 40 days on Mount Sinai, God revealed to him the requirements of covenant worship, as outlined in Ex 25:1–31:18. See also LEV 7:37–7:38, LEV 26:46

Moses descends Mount Sinai and discovers Israel's idolatry

EXO 32:19 See also EXO 32:1–32:6, EXO 32:21–32:35; DEU 9:8–9:21; PSA 106:19–106:23

God commands Israel to move on from Mount Sinai

EXO 33:1–33:6, EXO 33:15–33:16

God calls Moses up Mount Sinai again

Moses is commanded to prepare new stone tablets

EXO 34:1–34:4

God reveals his nature and name to Moses

EXO 34:5–34:7

God renews his covenant with Israel

See also EXO 34:10–34:28

Moses descends Mount Sinai, his face radiant

EXO 34:29–34:35

The people of Israel prepare to move on from Mount Sinai

God commands Moses to take a census of Israel in the Desert of Sinai

NUM 1:1–1:4, NUM 3:14–3:16

The Israelites celebrate the Passover at Mount Sinai

NUM 9:1–9:5

The Israelites leave Mount Sinai

NUM 10:11–10:13 The Israelites had been at Mount Sinai for almost a year (see also Ex 19:1); ; DEU 1:6–1:8

Later generations look back to God's revelation on Mount Sinai

JDG 5:5 Ascribing the title “the One of Sinai” to God, Deborah acknowledges the great importance of his revelation at Mount Sinai. See also 1KI 19:8–19:9 Elijah's return to Horeb, the mountain of God, shows his need to hear God clearly again; NEH 9:13–9:14; PSA 68:7–68:8, PSA 106:19–106:23; MAL 4:4; ACT 7:37–7:38

Mount Sinai seen by NT writers as a symbol of the old covenant

GAL 4:24–4:31 Hagar, Mount Sinai and the present city of Jerusalem stand for the old covenant and bondage to the law, while Sarah and the new heavenly Jerusalem stand for the new covenant and living by faith; ; HEB 12:18–12:29

resource.label: 8270: holiness, as set apart for God

title: 4272: sky
content:
The sky demonstrating God's creative power and providence

PSA 19:1; JER 14:22 See also GEN 1:8–1:9; JOB 26:7, JOB 37:15–37:18; PSA 36:5, PSA 68:34, PSA 71:19, PSA 77:16–77:18, PSA 78:23, PSA 147:8; ISA 50:3; HOS 2:21–2:22

The sky associated with heavenly bodies

GEN 1:14–1:15; DEU 4:19 See also GEN 22:17 a promise repeated many times in the OT; JOB 37:21; PSA 89:37

The sky associated with birds

GEN 1:20; ; PRO 30:19; ; JER 4:25, JER 8:7; ; LAM 4:19

The sky associated with rain and hail

GEN 8:2 See also EXO 9:22–9:23; JOS 10:11; 2SA 22:12; 1KI 18:45; PSA 147:8; LUK 4:25; REV 11:6, REV 16:21

The sky associated with unusual phenomena

ISA 34:4; MAT 24:29–24:30 See also EXO 20:21–20:22; JOS 10:13; JER 10:2; JOL 2:10, JOL 3:16; MRK 13:25; REV 6:13–6:14, REV 8:10, REV 9:1–9:2, REV 12:4, REV 20:11

The sky used figuratively

MAT 16:2–16:3 See also EXO 24:10; DEU 1:28; PRO 23:5; JER 51:53; MAT 11:23; LUK 17:24

title: 4275: Sodom and Gomorrah
content:
Two neighbouring cities, probably located at the eastern or south-eastern end of the Dead Sea, noted for their sinfulness. God's response in wrath and judgment serves as a warning to subsequent generations.

Sodom and Gomorrah's location

GEN 10:19, GEN 13:10–13:12

Sodom became Lot's home

GEN 13:10–13:12

Sodom and Gomorrah at war

Sodom and Gomorrah, with other cities of the plain, defeated by the alliance of four kings

GEN 14:1–14:12

Abram rescues Lot and is honoured by the king of Sodom and his allies

GEN 14:13–14:24

The wickedness of Sodom and Gomorrah

GEN 13:13 See also GEN 18:20–18:21, GEN 19:1–19:9

Abraham pleads for the righteous in Sodom and Gomorrah

GEN 18:16–18:32

God's judgment against Sodom and Gomorrah

GEN 19:13 See also GEN 19:23–19:29; DEU 29:23; LUK 17:29

The story of Sodom and Gomorrah becomes a picture of human sinfulness and divine judgment

JUD 1:7 See also ISA 3:9, ISA 13:19; JER 23:14; LAM 4:6; EZK 16:44–16:58; AMO 4:11; MAT 10:15 parallel LUK 10:12; MAT 11:23–11:24; LUK 17:28–17:30; 2PE 2:6–2:9

title: 4278: spring of water
content:
Springs of water were important to the life of Israel, providing fresh water for the people. Both God and the gospel are often compared to springs of living water, on account of their life-bringing and refreshing qualities.

The symbolic importance of springs of water to Israel

Living water as a sign of hope and prosperity

ZEC 14:8 See also ISA 12:3, ISA 49:10; JER 31:9

Living water as a source of fertility

JER 17:8 See also PSA 1:3

Living water as a sign of God's presence and power

EXO 17:1–17:7; ; NUM 20:1–20:11

God as a spring of water

JER 2:13 See also JER 17:13

The gospel as a spring of water

JHN 4:13–4:14 See also PSA 42:2; ISA 55:1; JHN 7:37; REV 21:6, REV 22:17

Other biblical uses of the image of a spring

PRO 25:26 spoiling what is good; ; JAS 3:9–3:11 the tongue compared to a spring; ; 2PE 2:17 a dry spring as a symbol of sterility

title: 4281: stars
content:
Heavenly luminaries other than the sun and moon, often worshipped as gods by pagan nations. Scripture stresses that the stars were created by God, and are subject to him. Stars are often treated as symbols of the generosity of God, and his faithfulness to his promises.

The relationship between God and the stars

God is the creator

PSA 33:6 See also GEN 1:16; NEH 9:6; PSA 136:7–136:9, PSA 148:3–148:6

The stars demonstrate the power of their maker

ISA 40:26 See also JOB 9:7–9:9, JOB 38:31–38:33; PSA 147:4–147:5; ISA 45:11–45:12; AMO 5:8

Worship of the stars

Prohibited by God

DEU 4:19 See also DEU 17:3; 2KI 23:4–23:5

God's judgment of star worshippers

JER 8:2 See also JER 19:12–19:13; AMO 5:25–5:27; ZEP 1:4–1:5; ACT 7:42–7:43

Examples of star worship

2KI 17:16, 2KI 21:3–21:5; ; 2CH 33:5; ; ACT 28:11

Reading the stars

ISA 47:13 See also DAN 2:2–2:5, DAN 4:7

Signs in the stars

ISA 34:4 See also EZK 32:7–32:8; REV 6:13

The star of Bethlehem

MAT 2:9–2:10 See also MAT 2:2, MAT 2:7

The stars illustrating God's promise to Abraham

GEN 15:5 See also GEN 22:17; DEU 10:22; 1CH 27:23; NEH 9:23; JER 33:22; HEB 11:12

Metaphorical references to the stars

A coming ruler

NUM 24:17

The morning star

REV 22:16 See also ISA 14:12–14:13; 2PE 1:19; REV 2:28

Wandering stars

JUD 1:13

Stars representing angels

REV 1:16, REV 1:20

God's people shining like stars

PHP 2:15 See also DAN 12:3

title: 4284: sun
content:
Created as a source of light and heat and an indicator of time and direction. The sun is also used as a metaphor for the splendour and light of God himself and the righteousness of his people.

Natural functions of the sun

A source of light

GEN 1:16–1:18 See also PSA 136:7–136:8

A marker of time

GEN 1:14 See also GEN 29:7; EXO 22:26; JDG 9:33; 1SA 11:9

An indicator of direction

NUM 21:11 See also EXO 27:13; DEU 11:30; JOS 19:12; PSA 113:3; ISA 45:6

A blessing, necessary for life

ECC 11:7 See also DEU 33:13–33:14; JOB 8:16; MAT 5:45

The scorching heat causing discomfort and harm

JON 4:8 See also PSA 121:6; ISA 49:10; MAT 13:6; JAS 1:11; REV 7:16

Worship of the sun

Forbidden to God's people

DEU 4:19 See also DEU 17:2–17:5; JOB 31:26–31:28

Practised in Israel and Egypt

EZK 8:16 See also 2KI 23:5, 2KI 23:11; JER 8:1–8:2, JER 43:13

Sunlight and God's judgment

JOL 2:31; MAT 24:29–24:30 parallels MRK 13:24, LUK 21:25–21:26 See also EXO 10:21–10:23; ISA 13:9–13:10; JER 4:28; EZK 32:7–32:8; JOL 3:14–3:15; AMO 5:20; MAT 27:45 parallels MRK 15:33, LUK 23:44 the crucifixion; ACT 2:20; REV 6:12–6:17

God and the sun

God as creator and controller

JOB 9:7; PSA 74:16 See also JOS 10:12–10:13; 2KI 20:8–20:11; PSA 19:1–19:6, PSA 148:3, PSA 148:5; ISA 38:8; JER 31:35–31:36; HAB 3:11

God likened to the sun

LUK 1:78–1:79 See also PSA 84:11; ISA 9:2; HAB 3:4; MAL 4:2; MAT 17:2; 2CO 4:6; REV 1:16

God's glory outshines the sun

REV 21:23 See also ISA 24:23, ISA 60:19–60:20; ACT 26:13; REV 22:5

Metaphorical allusions to the sun

In Paul's conversion experience

ACT 26:13

As righteousness

PSA 37:6; MAT 13:43 See also 2SA 23:3–23:4; PRO 4:18; ISA 58:8, ISA 58:10, ISA 62:1; DAN 12:3

As constancy and dependability

HOS 6:3 See also PSA 72:5, PSA 72:17, PSA 89:36

The setting sun signifying a premature end

JER 15:9; ; AMO 8:9; ; MIC 3:6

Miracles and signs concerning the sun

Darkening of the sun: ISA 5:30, ISA 24:23; ; AMO 8:9; JOS 10:12–10:13; ; 2KI 20:11; ; ISA 38:8; ; HAB 3:11

title: 4287: universe
content:
All existing things, including the earth and its creatures and all the heavenly bodies. The universe was made by God and is sustained by Jesus Christ.

God as the Maker of all things

PSA 115:15; JER 10:16; REV 4:11 See also GEN 14:19; 2KI 19:15; ECC 11:5; ISA 37:16, ISA 44:24; ACT 14:15; ROM 11:33–11:36; 1CO 8:6; EPH 3:9; HEB 11:3

God as the ruler of the universe

1CH 29:12; ACT 17:24 See also PSA 119:91; JER 33:25; MAT 11:25

Jesus Christ, the one through whom the universe was made, now sustains all things

JHN 1:3; COL 1:15–1:17 The title “firstborn” implies that Jesus Christ is the heir, that everything belongs to him; HEB 1:2–1:3 See also JHN 13:3; 1CO 8:6; EPH 1:22, EPH 4:10

The universe is temporary

MAT 24:35; 2PE 3:10–3:12 See also MAT 5:18, MAT 24:29; LUK 21:26

Future renewal of the universe

EPH 1:10; 2PE 3:13 See also ISA 65:17, ISA 66:22; MAT 19:28; ROM 8:19–8:22; EPH 1:10

Other references to the universe

DEU 4:25–4:26, DEU 30:19; ; PHP 2:15

title: 4290: valleys
content:
Valleys used as places of permanent settlement

GEN 26:17 See also NUM 14:25; JOS 17:16; JDG 18:28; 1CH 12:14–12:15

Valleys as places for camp-sites

JDG 6:33 See also NUM 21:10–21:12; DEU 3:29; JDG 7:8–7:12; 1CH 11:15

Valleys used for pasture and crops

1CH 4:39–4:40 Palestine was noted as a dry land, but valleys provided the necessary moisture for pastures and crop growth. See also 1CH 27:29; PSA 65:13

Valleys used for battles

GEN 14:8 See also JOS 11:7–11:8; 1SA 17:1–17:3; 2SA 8:13; 2CH 25:11–25:12 parallel 2KI 14:7

Valleys as places of worship and sacrifice

2CH 28:3 See also DEU 21:1–21:9; 2CH 20:25–20:26; JER 2:23–2:25, JER 32:35

Valleys on the day of the LORD

ISA 22:5; ; MIC 1:3–1:4; ; ZEC 14:3–14:5

The metaphorical use of valleys

The valley of death

PSA 23:4 See also JOB 21:32–21:33

The valley of decision

JOL 3:14 See also JOL 3:1–3:2, JOL 3:12

The valley of dry bones

EZK 37:1–37:14

The levelling of valleys as a preparation for the coming of the Lord

LUK 3:4–3:5 See also ISA 40:3–40:4

The Valley of Hinnom

The Valley of Hinnom as part of the boundary between Judah and Benjamin, lying to the south of Jerusalem

JOS 15:8, JOS 18:16

The Valley of Hinnom as a place of child sacrifice

2CH 28:3 See also JER 32:35

The Valley of Hinnom as a place of God's judgment

JER 7:30–7:32 See also JER 19:1–19:15

The Kidron Valley

The Kidron Valley as a boundary of Jerusalem

1KI 2:36–2:37 See also 2SA 15:23; JER 31:38–31:40; JHN 18:1

The Kidron Valley as a place for the destruction of pagan cult objects

1KI 15:11–15:13; 2KI 23:4–23:6 parallel 2CH 34:3–34:4 See also 2KI 23:12

The Kidron Valley in prophetic visions

EZK 47:1–47:8; JOL 3:1–3:2 See also ZEC 14:8

title: 4293: water
content:
The colourless, odourless liquid that is essential for sustenance of life in human beings and animals. God is described as the spring of living water, being the source of life and salvation to those who come to him.

Water for drinking

For human beings

2SA 23:15–23:16 parallel 1CH 11:17–11:18 See also GEN 21:14–21:19 God provides water for Hagar and Ishmael; EXO 15:22–15:27, EXO 17:1–17:3; NUM 20:1–20:11; DEU 2:28; JDG 4:19, JDG 5:25; 1SA 30:11–30:12; 1KI 17:10 Elijah asks the widow at Zarephath for a drink of water; 2KI 6:22; 2CH 18:25–18:26; NEH 9:20; JOB 6:19; ISA 33:16; DAN 1:8–1:16 Daniel's resolve to live on vegetables and water; HOS 2:5; MAT 10:42 parallel MRK 9:41; JHN 4:7; 1TI 5:23

For animals

2KI 3:9 See also GEN 24:13–24:19, GEN 29:1–29:10; EXO 2:15–2:16, EXO 17:3; 2KI 3:17; PSA 104:10–104:11; ISA 43:20; LUK 13:15

Water as a general descriptive term for liquid

EXO 23:25 See also 1SA 25:11; 1KI 13:6–13:22, 1KI 18:4; JOB 22:7; PRO 25:21

Abstention from water during fasting

DEU 9:9 See also EXO 34:28; DEU 9:18; EZR 10:6

Water for washing

For general hygiene

GEN 18:4 See also GEN 24:32, GEN 43:24; LUK 7:44; JHN 13:1–13:17

For ritual cleansing

LEV 17:15 See also EXO 30:17–30:21; LEV 1:3–1:9, LEV 16:20–16:24; NUM 19:7–19:9; JHN 2:6; HEB 10:22

Water for cooking

EXO 12:9–12:10 See also EZK 24:3–24:5

Rainwater

JDG 5:4 See also GEN 7:11–7:12; JOB 5:10, JOB 37:13, JOB 38:25–38:28, JOB 38:37; PSA 77:17; ECC 11:3; HOS 6:3

Sources and stores of water

Wells

GEN 26:19–26:20 See also GEN 21:19, GEN 24:11; ISA 37:25; JHN 4:6

Springs

JAS 3:11–3:12 See also JOS 15:15–15:19 parallel JDG 1:11–1:15; REV 8:10–8:11, REV 14:7, REV 16:4

Cisterns

JER 2:13 See also LEV 11:36; 2KI 18:31 parallel ISA 36:16; PRO 5:15–5:16; JER 14:3, JER 38:6

Abundance of water as a sign of blessing

DEU 8:7 See also NUM 24:7; DEU 10:7; PSA 1:1–1:3; JER 17:7–17:8; EZK 17:5–17:6, EZK 19:10–19:11

Baptism in water

MAT 3:11 parallels MRK 1:8, LUK 3:16 See also MAT 3:13–3:17 parallels MRK 1:9–1:11, LUK 3:21–3:22, JHN 1:31–1:34; JHN 1:26–1:27, JHN 3:23; ACT 1:5, ACT 8:36–8:39, ACT 10:47, ACT 11:16; 1PE 3:20–3:22

Living water

JER 17:13 See also ZEC 14:8–14:9; JHN 4:4–4:26, JHN 7:37–7:39; REV 7:13–7:17, REV 21:6–21:8, REV 22:1–22:2, REV 22:17

Metaphorical references to water

As a symbol of affliction

2SA 22:17; ; PSA 69:1; ; ISA 30:20, ISA 43:2

As a symbol of salvation

ISA 12:3, ISA 49:10, ISA 55:1; ; EZK 36:25; ; JHN 7:38

As a symbol of life

JHN 4:14, JHN 7:37–7:39; ; REV 21:6, REV 22:17

Other metaphorical references

SNG 4:15; ; ISA 30:20, ISA 35:6, ISA 44:12; ; JER 9:1; ; EPH 5:25–5:26; ; 2PE 2:17

title: 4296: wells
content:
Artificial openings to tap underground water supplies. They were very important for life in a settled urban society and this is reflected in their symbolical use in Scripture.

The importance of wells in urban life

NEH 9:25 See also DEU 6:10–6:11

Women drew water for domestic use

GEN 24:11–24:20 See also 1SA 9:11; JHN 4:7–4:9

Livestock were watered at wells

GEN 29:2–29:3 See also GEN 29:7–29:10; EXO 2:15–2:17

Wells were sometimes a source of contention

GEN 21:25 See also GEN 26:12–26:22; NUM 20:17, NUM 21:22

Wells as landmarks

GEN 16:7 Verse 14 refers to this spring as a well. See also GEN 16:14, GEN 21:31 “Beersheba” means either “well of seven” or “well of the oath”; GEN 26:20–26:22; JHN 4:6

Figurative use of wells

Associated with God and his goodness

ISA 12:3; JER 17:13 See also JER 2:13; JHN 4:14; REV 7:17

Associated with human love

PRO 5:15

In warnings against and judgment of sin

JER 6:7 See also PRO 23:27; JER 51:36

title: 4299: wilderness
content:
Wilderness as a wild, uncultivated area of land, frequently interchangeable with ``desert''

JER 2:6 See also JER 50:12

Good news for the wilderness

ISA 35:1–35:10 See also ISA 40:3–40:5; MAT 3:3 parallels MRK 1:3, LUK 3:4

title: 4303: metals
content:
Gold, silver, bronze, iron, copper, tin and lead are all mentioned in Scripture. Prized for their value, they were used in a variety of ways. Scripture uses as an image a metal refiner's fire for the purification of the lives of believers.

Gold

JOB 23:10 See also EXO 25:10–25:22, EXO 38:21–38:24; 1KI 6:27–6:35, 1KI 7:48–7:50, 1KI 10:14–10:21; PSA 19:7–19:11; REV 3:18, REV 17:4

Silver

PSA 12:6 See also GEN 37:26–37:28; EXO 38:25–38:28; 2CH 9:26–9:27; PSA 119:72; MAT 27:3–27:10

Bronze

GEN 4:22 See also EXO 27:1–27:7, EXO 38:29–38:31; NUM 16:31–16:40; 2CH 4:9–4:18

Iron

DEU 3:11 See also DEU 8:7–8:9; JDG 4:2–4:3; JOB 40:15–40:18; EZK 27:19

Copper

JOB 28:2 See also MRK 12:41–12:44 parallel LUK 21:1–21:4

Tin

EZK 27:12 See also EZK 22:18

Lead

It was an exceptionally heavy metal

EXO 15:10 See also ZEC 5:5–5:8

It was refined before use

JER 6:29 See also NUM 31:21–31:23; EZK 22:18–22:20

Inscriptions were sometimes made on lead

JOB 19:23–19:24

It was traded between nations

EZK 27:12

The value of metals

All metals were of value

JOS 22:8 See also 1CH 18:10, 1CH 22:14, 1CH 29:1–29:7; REV 18:11–18:12

The relative value of metals

ISA 60:17 See also 1KI 10:21 parallel 2CH 9:20; 1KI 14:25–14:28

Trade with metals

1KI 10:22 parallel 2CH 9:21 See also GEN 37:28; 1KI 10:11, 1KI 10:14–10:15 parallel 2CH 9:13–9:14; 1KI 22:48; EZK 27:13, EZK 27:22; ZEP 1:11

Idols were often made from various metals

LEV 19:4 See also 1KI 14:9; PSA 106:19; ISA 48:5; DAN 5:22–5:23, DAN 11:8

The refining of metals as an image of purification

ISA 48:10; MAL 3:2 See also PSA 12:6; ZEC 13:9

Material mined from the earth, especially metal ores and other substances of neither animal nor vegetable origin.

Bitumen

Chalk

Crystal

Flint

Lime

Pitch

Salt

LEV 2:13 See also DEU 29:23; JOB 39:5–39:6; EZK 47:6–47:11

Soda

PRO 25:20 “soda” in this instance is probably sodium carbonate, which would react violently with vinegar. See also JOB 9:30–9:31; JER 2:22

Sulphur

GEN 19:24 See also DEU 29:23; LUK 17:29; REV 19:20

Tar

GEN 14:10 See also EXO 2:3

Metals

content:
Tar

GEN 14:10 See also EXO 2:1–2:3 the infant Moses placed in a basket coated with tar

Bitumen

GEN 11:1–11:4

title: 4312: bronze
content:
A metal highly prized for its strength and durability. It had a variety of uses in biblical times and was employed extensively in both the tabernacle and temple.

Bronze as a material used by craftsmen

GEN 4:22 See also EXO 31:1–31:6 parallel EXO 35:30–35:34; 1KI 7:13–7:14; 1CH 22:15–22:16; 2CH 2:7, 2CH 2:13–2:14, 2CH 24:12

Characteristics of bronze

It was renowned for its strength

JER 15:12 See also LEV 26:18–26:19; DEU 28:23; JOB 6:12, JOB 37:18, JOB 40:18, JOB 41:27; ISA 48:4; JER 1:18, JER 15:20; DAN 7:19; MIC 4:13

It was noted for its brilliance

DAN 10:5–10:6 See also EZK 40:3; REV 1:15, REV 2:18

The monetary value of bronze

It was an expensive metal

REV 18:11–18:12 See also 1CH 18:8–18:10; EZR 8:26–8:27; EZK 27:13

It was less valuable than gold or silver

ISA 60:17 See also 1KI 14:25–14:28 parallel 2CH 12:9–12:11

Offerings of bronze were made at various times

EXO 35:24 See also EXO 25:1–25:7 parallel EXO 35:4–35:9; EXO 38:29–38:31; 1CH 18:9–18:11, 1CH 22:14, 1CH 29:6–29:7

Bronze was used in the tabernacle

For the altar and its utensils

2CH 1:5–1:6 See also EXO 27:1–27:6 parallel EXO 38:1–38:6; EXO 35:16 parallel EXO 39:39; NUM 4:13, NUM 16:39–16:40

For the laver

EXO 30:18 See also EXO 35:16, EXO 38:8

For the tent and the framework of the tabernacle

EXO 27:17–27:19 parallel EXO 38:17–38:20 See also EXO 26:10–26:11, EXO 26:36–26:37, EXO 27:9–27:11 parallel EXO 38:9–38:11; EXO 36:18, EXO 36:37–36:38

Bronze was used extensively in the temple

2CH 4:18 See also 1KI 7:23–7:33, 1KI 7:38–7:45 parallel 2CH 4:9–4:16; 1KI 8:64; 2KI 16:17, 2KI 25:13–25:17 parallel JER 52:17–52:23; 1CH 18:8, 1CH 29:2; 2CH 4:1

For armour

1SA 17:5–17:6 See also 1SA 17:38

For weapons

2SA 21:16 See also 1SA 17:6; 2SA 22:35 parallel PSA 18:34

For shackles

JDG 16:21 See also 2KI 25:7 parallels JER 39:7, JER 52:11; 2CH 33:11, 2CH 36:6; DAN 4:15, DAN 4:23

For gates

PSA 107:16 See also DEU 33:25; 1KI 4:13; 1CH 22:3; ISA 45:2

For the bronze snake

NUM 21:9 See also 2KI 18:4

For idols

DAN 5:4 See also DAN 5:23; REV 9:20

For mirrors

EXO 38:8 See also JOB 37:18

For cooking utensils

LEV 6:28

For tools

GEN 4:22

For musical instruments

1CH 15:19

For statues

See also DAN 2:32, DAN 2:35, DAN 2:45

Bronze as booty in war

JOS 22:7–22:8 See also NUM 31:21–31:23; JOS 6:24; 2SA 8:8 parallel 1CH 18:8

The metaphorical use of bronze

DAN 2:39 See also LEV 26:19; DEU 28:23; JOB 40:18; JER 1:18, JER 1:18; ZEC 6:1

title: 4315: clay
content:
Clay was a basic building material in biblical times and clay pottery was widely used.

Clay pottery

Moulded by potters

JER 18:3–18:4 See also ISA 29:16, ISA 41:25, ISA 45:9

Used to make pots

LEV 6:28 See also LEV 11:33, LEV 14:5, LEV 14:50, LEV 15:12; LAM 4:2

Used to make jars

JER 19:1–19:3 See also NUM 5:17; JER 32:14; 2CO 4:7

Other uses of clay

To make idols

DAN 2:31–2:35 See also DAN 2:41–2:45

To make moulds

1KI 7:46 parallel 2CH 4:17

To make bricks

NAM 3:14 See also JER 43:9

In plastering

LEV 14:42

To make furnaces

PSA 12:6

To seal documents

JOB 38:14

Clay tablets were used for writing on

EZK 4:1

Clay was a relatively weak material

JOB 13:12 See also JOB 4:18–4:19

Clay was a very common material

JOB 27:16 See also 2TI 2:20

God and his people compared to a potter and the clay

Carbonised vegetable material of wood, woody matter or charcoal, used for burning.

Coal used to burn incense

ISA 6:4–6:7

Coal used for baking and cooking

1KI 19:6 See also ISA 44:19; EZK 24:9–24:11; JHN 21:9

Coal used for domestic fires

content:
Impurities removed from molten metal during refining. In a spiritual sense, wicked people whom God removes in judgment, and also impurities in believers which are removed by discipline and trials.

The most valuable of all metals, gold was indicative of wealth and prosperity in biblical times. It had a wide variety of uses and was employed extensively in both the tabernacle and the temple.

content:
The process of heating metals to high temperatures in order to burn away impurities. It is often used in a metaphorical sense to depict God refining his people to get rid of impurities in their faith.

content:
A yellow, crystalline substance, also known as brimstone, which ignites and burns readily. It is invariably associated with divine punishment in Scripture.

The lake of burning sulphur

REV 21:8 See also REV 19:19–19:21, REV 20:7–20:10

title: 4402: plants
content:
Plants are an important aspect of God's creation, and a major source of food and materials. Scripture refers to many plants, with their various uses.

Plants as a source of food

GEN 1:29–1:30 See also GEN 1:11–1:12, GEN 3:17–3:19, GEN 9:3; PSA 104:14

Growth of plants dependent upon soil, sun and irrigation

DEU 32:2 See also GEN 2:4–2:5; 2KI 19:26 parallel ISA 37:27; JOB 8:16–8:19; ISA 61:11; ZEC 10:1; MAT 13:3–13:8 parallels MRK 4:3–4:8, LUK 8:5–8:8; JAS 1:11

Individual plants

Aloes

Mustard

Rose

Rush

title: 4404: food
content:
Scripture contains references to varied foodstuffs, which comprised the diet of the people of the Near East in biblical times.

God made provision for food in creation

Flour

Flour the main ingredient in bread

GEN 18:6 See also EXO 29:2; JDG 6:19; 1SA 28:24; MAT 13:33 parallel LUK 13:21

Flour figured largely in the staple diet

1KI 17:12–17:16 See also 2SA 17:27–17:29; 1KI 4:22–4:23; 2KI 4:41, 2KI 7:1, 2KI 7:16; 1CH 12:40

Fine flour more refined and more expensive than the common variety

title: 4406: agriculture
content:
The science of cultivation. The task, given to humanity, of cultivating the land is a reflection of God's own care for his creation.

A major activity for humanity

GEN 2:15 See also GEN 3:23, GEN 4:2, GEN 4:12, GEN 9:20, GEN 47:19; DEU 8:7–8:8; 1KI 19:19; 2CH 26:10; ISA 37:30

Growth is according to the sovereign will of God

He has established the seasons

GEN 8:22; ACT 14:17 See also LEV 26:4; JER 5:24

He provides for the growth of the crops

PSA 65:9 See also PSA 104:14, PSA 107:37–107:38, PSA 147:8; EZK 34:26–34:27; ZEC 8:12; MAT 6:30; 2CO 9:10

OT laws for agriculture showed how to co-operate with God

LEV 19:19 See also LEV 19:23, LEV 25:3–25:4; DEU 22:10

Harvest festivals were for thanking God for his provision

EXO 34:22 See also LEV 23:10, LEV 23:39–23:41; DEU 16:10, DEU 16:13

Cultivation was affected by the fall

GEN 3:17 See also GEN 3:18–3:19, GEN 4:12, GEN 5:29; ROM 8:20

Agricultural images of God's action among his people

ISA 5:7; MAT 9:37; JHN 12:24 See also ISA 17:5–17:6, ISA 24:13; JER 8:20, JER 50:16, JER 51:33; HOS 6:11; MAT 13:3–13:8 parallels MRK 4:3–4:8, LUK 8:5–8:8; MAT 13:18–13:23 parallels MRK 4:13–4:20, LUK 8:11–8:15; MAT 13:24–13:30; MRK 4:26–4:29; JHN 4:35–4:38

content:
Balm was a herb valued for its healing properties

title: 4410: banquets
content:
Grand, celebratory meals consisting usually of rich food and flowing wine. Banquets usually accompanied weddings, religious festivals and other celebrations. Prophecies of the Messianic age refer to a banquet to which the righteous will be invited.

Characteristics of banquets

They were usually held in a hall

SNG 2:4 See also EST 7:8; DAN 5:10

Honoured guests were given special places

LUK 14:7–14:11 See also PRO 25:6–25:7; MAT 23:6 parallels MRK 12:39, LUK 20:46

Guests reclined on couches

EST 1:6; ; MAT 26:7 parallels MRK 14:3, LUK 7:36; ; JHN 12:2

Invitations were sent out

LUK 14:17 parallel MAT 22:3

Occasions for holding banquets

To celebrate weddings

MAT 22:2 See also GEN 29:21–29:22 a feast celebrates Jacob's wedding; JDG 14:10 Samson gives a wedding feast; EST 2:18 Xerxes celebrates Esther's choice as queen with a banquet; MAT 25:10; LUK 12:36; JHN 2:8–2:9; REV 19:9 the marriage supper of the Lamb

To celebrate birthdays

GEN 40:20; ; MRK 6:21

To celebrate other events

GEN 21:8 Abraham celebrates Isaac's weaning.; EST 8:17, EST 9:17–9:19; LUK 15:23 The father celebrates the prodigal son's return.

Religious celebrations

PSA 22:29 See also 2CH 30:21–30:22, 2CH 35:13; ZEC 7:6

Activities at banquets

Good food and drink

ISA 25:6 See also JOB 1:4; ZEC 7:6; MAT 22:4; LUK 15:23

Drunkenness and revelry

1SA 25:36 See also EST 1:7–1:8, EST 1:10; ECC 10:19; JER 51:39; DAN 5:3–5:4

Music and dancing

ISA 5:12 See also MAT 14:6 parallel MRK 6:22; LUK 15:25

Further examples of banquets

GEN 26:30 Isaac holds a feast for Abimelech; ; GEN 43:34 Joseph feasts with his brothers; ; 2SA 3:20 David holds a feast for Abner; ; 1KI 3:15 Solomon gives a feast for all his court.; EST 1:3, EST 1:5; EST 1:9 Queen Vashti holds a banquet for the women of the palace.; EST 5:4–5:5, EST 5:8; DAN 5:1–5:2 Belshazzar has a banquet for 1,000 nobles; ; LUK 5:29 parallel MAT 9:10 Levi holds a banquet for Jesus Christ; ; LUK 14:1 Jesus Christ is the guest of a leading Pharisee.

An invitation to a banquet seen as a sign of God's grace

LUK 14:12–14:14 See also 2KI 6:23 The king of Israel prepares a banquet for captured Aramean troops; MAT 22:8–22:10 parallel LUK 14:21–14:23

The Messianic banquet

MAT 8:11 See also LUK 13:29, LUK 14:15

title: 4416: branch
content:
The limb of a tree. The term is used literally, symbolically and metaphorically in Scripture. Branches are personified at times and the Messiah is referred to as the Branch.

Branches of various trees

Vine branches

GEN 49:22 See also NUM 13:23; ISA 18:5; JER 5:10, JER 6:9, JER 48:32; EZK 17:5–17:8, EZK 19:10–19:14; JHN 15:5–15:6

MAT 21:7–21:9 parallels MRK 11:7–11:10, JHN 12:12–12:13

In general

JER 23:5 See also ISA 4:2, ISA 11:1–11:5; JER 33:15–33:16; ZEC 3:8, ZEC 6:12–6:13

EXO 25:31–25:32 See also EXO 25:35–25:36, EXO 37:17–37:22

title: 4418: bread
content:
The basic component of the staple diet in biblical times. Consecrated bread was continually laid out in the Holy Place of the tabernacle and temple. Jesus Christ called himself the bread of life.

Bread the basic component of the diet

MAT 6:11 parallel LUK 11:3 See also GEN 14:18, GEN 18:3–18:6 Abraham entertains the three angels; GEN 25:34, GEN 27:17, GEN 45:23; DEU 8:7–8:9, DEU 23:4; JDG 8:5–8:6 Gideon asks for bread for his troops; JDG 19:19; RUT 2:14; 1SA 2:36, 1SA 17:17, 1SA 21:2–21:3, 1SA 25:7–25:11 Nabal refuses bread to David's men; 1KI 13:7–13:22, 1KI 17:10–17:16 The widow at Zarephath feeds Elijah and is rewarded; 1KI 19:5–19:6; JOB 23:12; PSA 104:14–104:15; PRO 30:8; ISA 33:16, ISA 51:14, ISA 55:2; JER 37:21 Jeremiah's rations in prison; JER 42:14; LAM 5:6; EZK 4:9; DEU 8:3; MAT 4:3–4:4 parallel LUK 4:3–4:4 the temptation of Jesus Christ; MRK 6:8; 2CO 9:10

A diet consisting solely of bread was indicative of deprivation

1KI 22:27 parallel 2CH 18:26 See also PRO 6:26; LAM 1:11, LAM 2:12, LAM 4:4

Kinds of bread

Unleavened bread

EXO 12:39 See also EXO 12:8, EXO 12:14–12:20, EXO 13:3–13:7

Manna

EXO 16:31–16:35 See also EXO 16:4–16:8, EXO 16:11–16:12, EXO 16:14–16:19; NEH 9:15; PSA 78:23–78:25, PSA 105:40

Gifts of bread

Offerings of bread

LEV 7:11–7:14 See also EXO 29:2, EXO 29:32–29:34; LEV 2:11, LEV 8:31–8:32, LEV 23:20; NUM 6:17–6:20; JDG 6:19–6:21

The bread of the Presence

LEV 24:5–24:8 EXO 25:23–25:30, EXO 35:10–35:13, EXO 39:33–39:36, EXO 40:22–40:23; ; NUM 4:4–4:8; 1SA 21:1–21:6 David and his men eat the consecrated bread; ; 1CH 9:31–9:32, 1CH 23:29; ; 2CH 2:4, 2CH 13:11; ; NEH 10:32–10:33; ; MAT 12:1–12:8 parallels MRK 2:23–2:28, LUK 6:1–6:5 Jesus Christ and his disciples in the cornfields on the Sabbath; ; HEB 9:1–9:2

title: 4420: breakfast
content:
Breakfast is the first meal of the day

JHN 21:1–21:13 See also JDG 19:5–19:10

content:
Prickly vegetation, painful to the touch, often seen as a symbol of dereliction.

A brier as prickly vegetation

ISA 10:17 See also ISA 9:18, ISA 27:4

Briers as an instrument of punishment

JDG 8:16 See also JDG 8:4–8:7

title: 4424: cedar
content:
A tall, majestic tree, native to Lebanon. In OT times it was highly prized for its wood, which was used extensively in the construction of Solomon's temple.

Cedar trees associated with Lebanon

1KI 4:33 See also JDG 9:15; 2KI 14:9 parallel 2CH 25:18; 2KI 19:23 parallel ISA 37:24; PSA 29:5, PSA 92:12, PSA 104:16; SNG 5:15; ISA 14:8; JER 22:23; EZK 27:5; HOS 14:5–14:6; ZEC 11:1–11:2

The cedar as a tall, majestic tree

EZK 31:3 See also JOB 40:17; PSA 80:10; ISA 2:12–2:13; EZK 17:22–17:23, EZK 31:7–31:8; AMO 2:9

Cedar trees indicating a fertile, well-watered land

NUM 24:5–24:6 See also PSA 92:12–92:15; ISA 41:17–41:20

Cedar used allegorically

EZK 17:1–17:4 prophecy of a Davidic king being taken into captivity; ; EZK 17:22–17:23 prophecy of the Messiah coming from David's family

Cedar as a costly and valued wood

1KI 10:27 parallels 2CH 1:15, 2CH 9:27 See also SNG 8:8–8:9; ISA 9:8–9:10

Cedar wood used in ritual purification

LEV 14:48–14:53 See also LEV 14:1–14:7; NUM 19:1–19:6

Royal palaces and great houses

The temple

1KI 6:18 See also 2SA 7:7; 1KI 6:9–6:10, 1KI 6:20, 1KI 6:36, 1KI 7:12

title: 4426: chaff
content:
Husks of wheat or other cereal. It was separated from the grain by threshing, whereupon it usually blew away, as it was lighter than the grain. Sometimes however, it was burned as fuel. The wicked are frequently likened to chaff in Scripture.

Grain was threshed on outdoor threshing-floors to separate the chaff

DAN 2:31–2:35 See also HOS 13:3

Characteristics of chaff

It is blown by the wind

JOB 13:25

It is light

JOB 41:28

It is useless

ISA 33:11

The wicked are compared to chaff

They will disappear as chaff is blown away

PSA 1:1–1:6 See also JOB 21:17–21:18; PSA 35:4–35:6, PSA 83:13–83:15; ISA 17:13, ISA 29:5, ISA 40:24–40:25; JER 13:24

Their punishment is compared to threshing

ISA 41:15–41:16 See also ISA 41:2

Their end will be like chaff which is burned

MAT 3:11–3:12 parallel LUK 3:16–3:17

content: Scripture lays down no general prohibition of the consumption of alcohol, but warns against its abuse.

content:
Scripture indicates that the abuse of drinking, such as drunkenness, is unacceptable. It also indicates the advantages of abstention from drinking in certain situations.

content:

content:
Offerings of the produce of the land, being the first and best of the crops, given in acknowledgment of God's abundant blessings. The term is also used metaphorically to indicate the first of a much larger group that is to follow, such as those who will be raised from the dead.

content:

title: 4464: harvest
content:
The gathering of mature crops from the land. The successful culmination of the agricultural year was evidence of God's goodness and consequently marked with festivity. Harvest is also used widely in a metaphorical sense.

The harvest is evidence of God's goodness

GEN 8:22; 2CO 9:10 See also LEV 26:5, LEV 26:9–26:10; PSA 67:6, PSA 85:12, PSA 107:37; ISA 62:8–62:9; JER 5:24

EXO 23:10–23:11, EXO 34:21; ; LEV 19:9, LEV 19:23–19:25, LEV 23:22, LEV 25:4–25:5, LEV 25:11–25:12, LEV 25:18–25:27; ; NUM 18:12–18:13; ; DEU 24:19–24:21

EXO 23:16, EXO 34:22; ; LEV 23:10–23:16, LEV 23:33–23:43; ; DEU 16:13–16:15

Rejoicing

ISA 9:3 See also PSA 126:5–126:6; ISA 16:9

Hard work

title: 4466: herbs and spices
content:
Plants whose leaves or seeds are used as food or flavourings and sometimes for medicine. The rich variety of such plants in the Near East results in numerous references in Scripture.

Balm

title: 4468: horticulture
content:
The art of garden cultivation, first practised in the Garden of Eden.

Horticulture in the Garden of Eden

The man and woman tended the garden

GEN 2:15–2:23

The fall brought about a change in human priorities from horticulture to agriculture

GEN 3:17–3:19 See also GEN 4:2

Egyptian agriculture is likened to horticulture

DEU 11:10–11:11

Horticulture is totally dependent on good irrigation

ISA 58:11 See also GEN 2:4–2:14, GEN 13:10; NUM 24:5–24:7; JOB 8:16; SNG 4:12, SNG 4:15; ISA 51:3; JER 31:12

Those who practised horticulture

Kings

JER 29:28 See also JER 29:1–29:7; JHN 19:41

Gardeners

JHN 20:15 See also ISA 5:7; JHN 15:1

Vegetable gardens

Fruit gardens

LUK 11:42 See also SNG 6:2; MAT 13:31–13:32 parallels MRK 4:31–4:32, LUK 13:19

content:
A plant closely associated with cleansing in the OT, being used extensively in rituals for purification.

Hyssop associated with cleansing

PSA 51:2–51:7 See also HEB 9:19–9:22

At the Passover

EXO 12:21–12:25

At the slaughter of the red heifer

NUM 19:1–19:10

In rites of cleansing

1KI 4:33

Hyssop at the crucifixion

JHN 19:28–19:30

title: 4472: lily
content:
Lilies admired for their beauty

LUK 12:22–12:31 parallel MAT 6:25–6:34 See also SNG 2:16, SNG 4:5, SNG 6:2–6:3

The metaphorical use of lilies

SNG 2:1–2:2 See also SNG 5:13, SNG 7:2; HOS 14:5

title: 4474: manna
content:
The source of food provided miraculously for the children of Israel during their desert wanderings. The NT sees this heavenly food as a symbol of Jesus Christ as the bread of life.

Manna provided by God

EXO 16:31; JOS 5:12 See also EXO 16:32–16:35; NUM 11:6–11:9; NEH 9:15; PSA 78:24–78:25; JHN 6:31, JHN 6:49; HEB 9:4

Manna as a type

A type of the word of God

DEU 8:3 See also DEU 8:16; NEH 9:20; MAT 4:4

A type of Jesus Christ

JHN 6:50–6:51 See also JHN 6:32–6:35, JHN 6:47–6:48, JHN 6:52–6:58

A symbol of future blessing

REV 2:17

resource.label: 2203: Jesus Christ, titles and names of

title: 4476: meals
content:
Food eaten at set times, on special occasions or following significant events. Sacrificial meals formed part of Israelite worship in the OT, as does the Lord's Supper for worship by believers in the NT.

Regular daily meals

Breakfast

JHN 21:12

Lunch

LUK 14:12–14:14 See also GEN 43:16, GEN 43:31–43:34; RUT 2:14; ACT 10:9–10:10

Evening meal

LUK 17:7–17:8 See also MAT 9:10–9:13 parallels MRK 2:15–2:17, LUK 5:29–5:32; MRK 14:3 parallels LUK 7:36, JHN 12:1–12:2; LUK 14:12–14:14

Royal meals

GEN 40:20 See also 1SA 20:18–20:34; 2SA 9:9–9:13; 1KI 4:22–4:28, 1KI 18:19; EST 1:1–1:12, EST 2:18, EST 7:1–7:10; MAT 14:6–14:11 parallel MRK 6:21–6:28

Meals of the poor

PRO 15:17 See also 1KI 17:10–17:16

Sacrificial meals

GEN 31:53–31:54 See also EXO 12:1–12:11, EXO 29:31–29:34; LEV 6:14–6:29, LEV 7:1–7:6, LEV 8:31, LEV 10:12–10:15; DEU 16:1–16:8; 1SA 1:3–1:8, 1SA 9:11–9:24, 1SA 20:28–20:29

Symbolic meals

MAT 26:20–26:29 parallels MRK 14:17–14:25, LUK 22:14–22:22 See also 1CO 11:17–11:34

Wedding and funeral meals

MAT 22:1–22:14 parallel LUK 14:16–14:24 See also JER 16:5; JHN 2:1–2:10; REV 19:6–19:9

REV 3:20 See also PRO 15:17; LUK 5:29–5:32, LUK 7:36

title: 4478: meat
content:
The flesh of animals, consumed as food. In biblical times it was a luxury, eaten on special occasions, usually in the context of sacrificial worship.

Meat a luxury food

EXO 16:3 See also NUM 11:4–11:5; 1SA 25:11; ISA 22:12–22:13; DAN 10:3

Meat served to honoured guests

1SA 28:21–28:25 See also GEN 18:1–18:8; JDG 6:11–6:21, JDG 13:9–13:16; 1SA 9:22–9:24; 2SA 12:1–12:4; 1KI 19:19–19:21; PRO 9:1–9:6; ISA 25:6; LUK 15:20–15:24

Meat provided by God

1KI 17:2–17:6 See also EXO 16:1–16:14; NUM 11:4–11:34; PSA 78:17–78:31

Restrictions on eating certain types of meat

Unclean meat

LEV 11:1–11:47 parallel DEU 14:3–14:20 See also ISA 65:2–65:5

Other prohibited meat

GEN 9:4 See also EXO 21:28, EXO 22:31; LEV 19:26; DEU 12:23, DEU 14:21; 1SA 14:33–14:34; EZK 4:14, EZK 33:25; ACT 15:20, ACT 15:29, ACT 21:25

Other sacrifices

EXO 29:31–29:34 See also LEV 7:11–7:18; NUM 18:14–18:19; DEU 12:27; 1SA 1:3–1:8, 1SA 2:12–2:16; JER 7:21; HOS 8:13

Non-consecrated meat

DEU 12:4–12:25

Allegorical and metaphorical references to meat

MIC 3:1–3:3 See also EZK 11:1–11:12, EZK 24:1–24:13

Varying convictions about eating meat

ROM 14:1–14:23

title: 4480: milk
content:
A common source of food and nourishment, associated with rich and productive land. The term is also used figuratively as a form of spiritual food suitable for those who have yet to reach maturity in the Christian faith.

Milk as a source of nourishment

DEU 32:13–32:14 See also GEN 18:8; JDG 4:19; 2SA 17:29; PRO 27:27; EZK 25:4; 1CO 9:7 illustrating the support of the ministry

Milk as an indication of prosperity and well-being

DEU 6:3 A frequent description of the promised land; ISA 55:1 See also EXO 3:8; LEV 20:24; NUM 13:27; ISA 7:22, ISA 60:16; EZK 20:6; JOL 3:18

Laws concerning milk

EXO 23:19, EXO 34:26; ; DEU 14:21

Elementary Christian teaching likened to milk

HEB 5:12–5:13 See also 1CO 3:2; 1PE 2:2

Milk used figuratively

PRO 30:33 See also GEN 49:12; JOB 10:10; SNG 4:11, SNG 5:12; LAM 4:7

title: 4482: millstones
content:
Pairs of upper and lower millstones were used for grinding grain in the ancient East. Millstones were common features of the daily life of ordinary people.

Millstones as a means of earning a living

DEU 24:6 See also ISA 47:1–47:2; JER 25:10; REV 18:22

Millstones were heavy and burdensome

MAT 18:6 parallels MRK 9:42, LUK 17:2

Millstones as potential weapons

JDG 9:50–9:53 See also 2SA 11:21

Millstones in figurative use

JOB 41:24

Millstones as symbols of destructive force

REV 18:21

title: 4484: mustard seed
content:
A very small seed which grew rapidly into a substantial plant. It was used in parables by Jesus Christ to encourage the faith of the individual and to illustrate the growth of the kingdom of God.

The mustard seed and the faith of the individual

LUK 17:5–17:6 See also MAT 17:14–17:20

The mustard seed and the kingdom of God

MAT 13:31–13:32 parallels MRK 4:30–4:32, LUK 13:18–13:19

title: 4486: myrrh
content:
A resinous substance derived from certain trees and bushes native to the Near East. It was a valuable commodity and had a variety of uses.

Myrrh was traded by merchants

GEN 37:25 See also REV 18:11–18:13

Myrrh was presented as a gift

MAT 2:11 See also GEN 43:11

Uses of myrrh

In the sacred anointing oil

EXO 30:22–30:28

As a perfume

SNG 1:13 See also PSA 45:8; PRO 7:10–7:18; SNG 3:6

In beauty treatments

EST 2:12 See also SNG 5:5

As a painkiller

MRK 15:22–15:23

In embalming

JHN 19:38–19:40

The metaphorical use of myrrh

SNG 5:13 See also SNG 4:1–4:7, SNG 4:12–4:14, SNG 5:1

title: 4488: oil
content:
Normally olive oil, which was in plentiful supply in Israel and in general household use. Its ritual uses included the anointing of priests and kings and ceremonial cleansing; in the NT oil was used to anoint the sick. As a sign of God's provision for his people, oil was associated with prosperity and gladness, and was a mark of spiritual blessing.

The production of oil

JOB 24:11 Olives were pressed either with a pestle and mortar or in a stone press; ; MAT 26:36 “Gethsemane” means “oil press”, named after the stone presses associated with the Mount of Olives.

General uses of oil

As a necessary provision

1CH 12:40; ; 2CH 11:11

As a fuel for lamps

EXO 27:20 parallel LEV 24:2 See also EXO 35:14 parallel EXO 39:37; NUM 4:9; MAT 25:1–25:10

Mixed with flour in baking

1KI 17:12–17:16 See also EXO 29:23; NUM 11:8

As an ointment

ISA 1:6 See also PSA 55:21; LUK 10:34

Oil for use in the sanctuary

NEH 10:39 See also EXO 25:6 parallel EXO 35:8; NUM 4:16; 1CH 9:29; EZR 6:9

Oil used for ceremonial anointing

Instructions for making and using the anointing oil

EXO 30:23–30:33 See also EXO 31:11, EXO 37:29

Holy objects were consecrated by anointing with oil

Jacob twice anoints a pillar at Bethel: GEN 28:18, GEN 31:13, GEN 35:14; EXO 40:9–40:11; ; LEV 8:10–8:11

Priests were ordained by anointing with oil

LEV 8:12 parallel EXO 29:7 See also EXO 29:21 parallel LEV 8:30; LEV 10:7, LEV 21:10–21:12; PSA 133:2; ZEC 4:12–4:14

Kings were anointed with oil

1SA 10:1 Saul; 1SA 16:13; ; PSA 89:20; 1KI 1:39 Solomon; ; 2KI 9:1–9:6 Jehu

In ceremonial cleansing

LEV 14:15–14:18, LEV 14:24–14:29

In offerings

At the consecration of priests: EXO 29:2; ; NUM 8:8; EXO 29:40; ; NUM 28:5; LEV 2:1–2:7, LEV 6:20–6:21; ; NUM 7:13; LEV 7:12 with the thank offering; ; NUM 6:15 at the completion of the Nazirite's vow; NUM 28:9, NUM 28:28; ; EZK 46:4–46:5

Oil was excluded from certain offerings

LEV 5:11; ; NUM 5:15

Oil used to anoint the sick

JAS 5:14 See also MRK 6:13

God's provision of oil

Oil as a sign of God's blessing

DEU 32:13 See also DEU 7:13, DEU 8:7–8:8, DEU 11:14; HOS 2:8

Oil withdrawn as a sign of judgment

DEU 28:40 See also DEU 28:51; JOL 1:10; MIC 6:15; HAG 1:11

JER 31:12 See also HOS 2:22; JOL 2:18–2:19, JOL 2:24

The firstfruits of the oil harvest

NUM 18:12; ; DEU 18:4; ; 2CH 31:5; ; NEH 10:37

Tithes of oil

DEU 12:17, DEU 14:23; ; NEH 13:12

Oil as a commodity for trade

1KI 5:11 parallel 2CH 2:10; ; EZR 3:7; ; EZK 27:17; ; HOS 12:1

Oil as a sign of prosperity

JOB 29:6 See also DEU 33:24; 2KI 20:13 parallel ISA 39:2; PRO 21:20; ISA 57:9; EZK 16:18–16:19

PSA 45:7 The joy with which God anoints his servants is likened to the oil which anoints the bridegroom in preparation for his wedding. See also PSA 104:15; ECC 9:8; ISA 61:3; MAT 6:17 Oil draws attention away from the fact that a person is fasting; HEB 1:9

Oil as a sign of honour

PSA 23:5 Oil was used to anoint the heads of honoured guests at a meal. See also PSA 141:5; LUK 7:46

title: 4490: ointment
content:
A liquid blend of aromatic substances, usually spices, used as a cosmetic and also as a medicinal balm. Lotions were applied to the body to refresh, to improve appearance and to give a pleasing fragrance. Balm, especially from Gilead, was used to soothe pain and to heal wounds.

Preparation of ointment

JOB 41:31 One way of making ointment was to crush solid ingredients and boil them together in olive oil.

Preparation of the holy anointing oil

EXO 35:28 See also EXO 25:6 parallel EXO 35:8; EXO 30:23–30:25, EXO 30:33 The holy anointing oil was to be prepared only by those authorised to do so.

Ointments made from blended spices

2CH 16:14; ; SNG 3:6; ; LUK 23:56

Containers for ointment

ISA 3:20; MAT 26:7 parallel MRK 14:3; LUK 7:37

Uses of ointment

As a cosmetic lotion

RUT 3:3 See also EST 2:12; SNG 1:12; EZK 16:9PSA 104:15 See also 2SA 12:20, 2SA 14:2; ECC 9:8; DAN 10:3; MAT 6:17; EXO 30:32

To anoint the heads of guests

PSA 23:5 See also PSA 133:2 This may reflect the custom of anointing a guest with perfumed ointment which melted and ran down the face and clothes to produce a pleasing fragrance; LUK 7:46

To anoint the dead

MAT 26:12 parallel MRK 14:8 See also JHN 12:7; GEN 50:2–50:3, GEN 50:26; MRK 16:1 parallel LUK 24:1; JHN 19:39–19:40

Expensive ointments

MAT 26:8–26:9 parallel MRK 14:4–14:5 (Spike)nard was an expensive import from India. See also PRO 21:17; SNG 4:13–4:14; AMO 6:6 The use of expensive ointments was a mark of luxury; JHN 12:5

Ointment used in healing

Oil used to soothe and heal

ISA 1:6 See also PSA 55:21; LUK 10:34

Medicinal balm

JER 8:22 Gilead was famous for its healing balsam. See also 2CH 28:15; JER 46:11, JER 51:8

title: 4492: olive
content:
An evergreen tree native to the Near East. Also the fruit of this tree, for which it is cultivated. The fruit can be eaten or pressed to produce oil for food or other uses.

Olive oil

Olives pressed to produce oil

MIC 6:15 See also EXO 29:40; LEV 24:2; NUM 28:5

Olive oil in food use

EZK 16:13 See also NUM 18:11–18:13; DEU 8:6–8:9; 1CH 27:28; 2CH 11:5–11:11; JOB 29:4–29:6

Olive oil used in the tabernacle

LEV 24:2 See also EXO 25:1–25:7, EXO 30:22–30:33, EXO 35:4–35:9, EXO 35:28

Offerings of olive oil

EXO 29:38–29:41 See also NUM 18:12–18:13, NUM 28:3–28:8; EZR 7:21–7:23; EZK 16:19

Payment, tribute and trade in olive oil

1KI 5:10–5:11 See also 2CH 2:8–2:10, 2CH 2:15–2:16; HOS 12:1; LUK 16:1–16:8; REV 18:11–18:13

Olive trees

Olive trees cultivated for their fruit

HAG 2:19 See also GEN 8:11; DEU 28:38–28:40; 2KI 18:31–18:32; ISA 41:19; AMO 4:9

Wild olive trees

NEH 8:15 See also ROM 11:24

Olive trees in visions

REV 11:3–11:6 See also ZEC 4:1–4:14

Metaphorical references to olive trees

PSA 52:8 See also JDG 9:8–9:9; JOB 15:33; PSA 128:3; ISA 17:4–17:6, ISA 24:10–24:13; JER 11:16–11:17; HOS 14:5–14:6; ROM 11:1–11:36 the grafting of olive trees

Olive groves

EXO 23:10–23:11 See also DEU 6:10–6:11; JOS 24:13; JDG 15:4–15:5; 1SA 8:11–8:14; NEH 5:9–5:11, NEH 9:25; JHN 18:1–18:3

Olive wood

1KI 6:23–6:28 See also 1KI 6:31–6:35

title: 4494: papyrus
content:
A large plant growing in marshland, sometimes to a height of six metres.

Papyrus was used for making boats

EXO 2:3; ; JOB 9:26; ; ISA 18:2

Papyrus indicates an ample water supply

JOB 8:11–8:13; ; ISA 35:7 Papyrus growing in what was once desert depicts the blessing coming on God's redeemed people.

title: 4496: perfume
content:
Substances used to make perfumes

By blending ingredients

EXO 30:23–30:24, EXO 30:34

Substances used as perfumes

SNG 4:13–4:14 See also SNG 1:12–1:14, SNG 3:6, SNG 5:13; MRK 14:3; JHN 12:3

GEN 37:25; ; EZK 27:19; ; REV 18:13

Its tempting allure

PRO 7:17 See also ISA 57:9; EZK 23:40–23:41

Its pleasing fragrance

PRO 27:9; ; SNG 1:3, SNG 4:10

Fragrance used metaphorically

2CO 2:14–2:16 See also EPH 5:2; PHP 4:18

As a cosmetic

EST 2:12 See also RUT 3:3

To give fragrance to clothes

PSA 45:8 See also SNG 4:11
