1SA 8:13 perfumers in the service of the king; ; NEH 3:8 Hananiah

title: 4498: ploughing
content:
Cutting up the furrows in soil and turning it up before work can be done on the land.

Ploughing as a way of life

1KI 19:19–19:21; LUK 17:7 See also 1SA 8:12, 1SA 13:19–13:21; JOB 1:14; PRO 20:4; ISA 28:24; EZK 36:9; AMO 9:13

Rules concerning ploughing

EXO 23:10–23:11, EXO 34:21; ; DEU 22:10

Ploughing used figuratively

JOB 4:8; LUK 9:62 See also JDG 14:18; PSA 129:3, PSA 141:7; ISA 2:4; JER 4:3; HOS 10:11–10:12; AMO 6:12; 1CO 9:10

title: 4500: poison
content:
A substance obtained from plants or animals that can cause illness, pain, disability or death. It is often used symbolically to refer to distress or bitterness.

Kinds of poison

Poison from plants, water or food

EXO 15:23; ; DEU 32:32; ; 2KI 2:19–2:22, 2KI 4:39–4:40; ; HOS 10:4

Poison from animals or reptiles

NUM 21:6; ; DEU 32:24, DEU 32:33

Poison used symbolically

To refer to distress

In these incidences God himself is portrayed as the one who brings such bitter experiences: JOB 6:4; ; JER 8:14, JER 9:15, JER 23:15

To refer to evil and its effects

JOB 20:12–20:16 See also DEU 29:18, DEU 32:32–32:33; PSA 58:4; PRO 23:32; ISA 14:29; JER 8:17; AMO 6:12; ACT 14:2; ROM 3:13; PSA 140:1–140:3; JAS 3:8; REV 9:5

title: 4502: reed
content:
A plant that grows in water

JOB 8:11 See also GEN 41:1–41:4, GEN 41:17–41:21; EXO 2:1–2:6; 1KI 14:15; JOB 40:21; ISA 19:6, ISA 35:7

For fuel

JOB 41:20

As measuring rods

REV 11:1

The metaphorical use of reeds

ISA 9:14–9:15 See also ISA 19:14–19:15; EZK 29:6–29:7

title: 4504: roots
content:
The source and foundation of a tree or plant, anchoring it to the soil, usually found beneath the surface of the ground. Scripture contains literal, metaphorical and figurative references to roots.

Roots of trees and plants

JOB 30:4 See also JOB 14:7–14:9; MAT 13:3–13:6 parallel MRK 4:2–4:6; MRK 11:20–11:21

Jesus Christ as the ``Root of David''

title: 4506: seed
content:
The means of propagating life from one generation to another. Figuratively, the term illustrates principles of spiritual life and growth, especially in later parts of the OT and throughout the NT.

How seed was used

Seed for eating

The absence of seed was a curse

Regulations in the law concerning seed

DEU 22:9 parallel LEV 19:19 See also LEV 11:37–11:38, LEV 27:30

To illustrate birth into eternal life

To illustrate the growth of the kingdom of God

MAT 13:31–13:32 parallels MRK 4:30–4:32, LUK 13:18–13:19MRK 4:26–4:29 See also MAT 13:24–13:30, MAT 13:36–13:43

To illustrate God's word and people's response to it

See also MAT 13:1–13:23 parallels MRK 4:1–4:20, LUK 8:4–8:15 the parable of the sower

To illustrate faith

MAT 17:20

To illustrate the principle that death comes before life

JHN 12:23–12:25 See also 1CO 15:36

title: 4508: sickle
content:
A cutting tool with a short handle used in harvesting grain. Early blades were made of wood lined with flint teeth; these were later replaced with curved iron blades. The cutting down of grain at harvest with a sickle is used as a symbol of God's judgment.

Sickles used to harvest grain

MRK 4:29 See also DEU 16:9, DEU 23:25 Sickles were not to be used in another person's field; 1SA 13:19–13:21; JER 50:16

Cutting down grain as a symbol of judgment

JOL 3:13; REV 14:14–14:19 See also ISA 17:5; JER 51:33; MIC 4:12; MAT 13:30

title: 4510: sowing and reaping
content:
OT references are mainly agricultural with regulatory laws covering crop production and festivals of thanksgiving. God's blessing was reflected in the harvest. The NT refers more to the spiritual sowing and reaping that takes place in the kingdom of God.

Regulations concerning the sowing and reaping of crops

Laws of purity related to planting

LEV 19:23–19:25; ; DEU 22:9

Reapers are to leave gleanings for the poor

LEV 19:9–19:10, LEV 23:22; ; DEU 24:19; ; RUT 2:2–2:3, RUT 2:15–2:16

Harvest celebrations

EXO 23:16 See also LEV 23:9–23:11, LEV 23:39–23:41; DEU 16:9–16:10, DEU 16:13–16:15

The Sabbath-rest for the land and its cultivators

EXO 23:10–23:11, EXO 34:21; ; LEV 25:3–25:5, LEV 25:11

Reaping a good harvest is a blessing from God

It is a consequence of obedience

GEN 26:12; ; LEV 25:18–25:22, LEV 26:3–26:5, LEV 26:10

It is a promise to the remnant of God's people

AMO 9:13–9:14 See also ISA 30:23, ISA 62:8–62:9, ISA 65:21–65:22; JER 31:5; ZEC 8:11–8:12

God's judgment on Israel is reflected in reaping a poor harvest

A poor yield of crops

LEV 26:15–26:16 See also NEH 9:36–9:37; JOB 31:7–31:8; JER 5:17; MIC 6:15

PSA 126:5–126:6; GAL 6:9

See also JHN 4:35–4:38; ROM 1:13; 1CO 3:5–3:9

title: 4522: threshing
content:
The process of beating out or separating grain from harvested stalks.

LEV 26:5 See also JDG 6:11; 1CH 21:20; 1CO 9:9; DEU 25:4; 1TI 5:18 Paul is adapting a humane OT law to the support of Christian workers.

2SA 24:22 parallel 1CH 21:23 A threshing-sledge was a board studded underneath with sharp stones which was dragged by oxen over the wheat ears in order to separate the kernels. See also JOB 41:30; ISA 28:27–28:28, ISA 41:15

content:
The place where winnowing and threshing took place at harvest time.

content:
One of two trees situated in the centre of the Garden of Eden. It is also mentioned in Revelation, where it symbolises life and salvation.

content:
Large, woody plants with trunk, branches, leaves and roots, noted for their long lives. Scripture refers to many different varieties of tree.

title: 4530: unleavened bread
content:
Bread baked without yeast, often made because of insufficient time to prepare bread in the conventional manner. It was also eaten at the Passover Feast and was presented with sacrifices and offerings.

EXO 12:39 See also GEN 19:1–19:3; EXO 12:8–12:11; JDG 6:17–6:19; 1SA 28:21–28:25

Unleavened bread eaten to commemorate the Passover

EXO 12:1–12:20, EXO 13:3–13:7, EXO 23:15, EXO 34:18; ; LEV 23:5–23:6 parallel NUM 28:16–28:17; ; NUM 9:9–9:11; ; DEU 16:1–16:4, DEU 16:8; ; EZK 45:21

content:
A plant on which grapes grow, cultivated primarily for making wine. Jesus Christ described himself as the true vine, the source of life and growth for all believers.

1KI 4:25 See also 2KI 18:31 parallel ISA 36:16; MIC 4:4; ZEC 3:10

title: 4538: vineyard
content:
A cultivated field or garden, sometimes surrounded by a wall, in which vines and other trees were grown. Israel is sometimes compared to a vineyard. Some of Jesus Christ's parables concern vineyards.

Vineyards were sometimes surrounded by walls

MAT 21:33 parallel MRK 12:1 See also NUM 22:24

Vineyards were associated with a settled, peaceful life

ISA 65:20–65:22 See also GEN 9:20; DEU 6:10–6:11; 2KI 18:31–18:32 parallel ISA 36:16–36:17; 2KI 19:29 parallel ISA 37:30; 2CH 26:10; NEH 9:25; PSA 107:37–107:38; SNG 7:12; JER 31:3–31:5, JER 32:15, JER 35:6–35:10; EZK 28:26; AMO 9:14

title: 4540: weeds
content:
Uncultivated plants or shrubs, growing wild, often among cultivated crops or plants. Jesus Christ used the image of weeds in some of his parables to illustrate sin and worldliness.

The presence of weeds indicates desolation and neglect

ZEP 2:9 See also PRO 24:30–24:34; ISA 5:3–5:6, ISA 7:18–7:25, ISA 32:11–32:15, ISA 34:8–34:14, ISA 55:13; HOS 9:5–9:6

A cereal widely cultivated for use as food. One of the main agricultural crops in biblical times, it is referred to throughout the Scriptures and features several times in Jesus Christ's teaching.

title: 4544: wine
content:
Drink made from fermented grape juice, often drunk instead of water in biblical times. Scripture recognises the richness of wine and its value in bringing joy to people; however, it condemns the excessive drinking of wine, and recommends total abstinence in some situations.

title: 4546: winepress
content:
The vat in which grapes were trodden to produce juice for making wine. Scripture sometimes uses the image of treading grapes at the winepress to depict scenes of divine wrath and punishment.

Winepresses were widely used

DEU 15:12–15:14 See also NUM 18:25–18:27, NUM 18:30; JDG 6:11; 2KI 6:24–6:27

Treading grapes at winepresses

Literal references

NEH 13:15 See also JOB 24:11; ISA 16:10; JER 48:33

Metaphorical references

LAM 1:15 See also ISA 63:1–63:6; JOL 3:13; REV 14:17–14:20, REV 19:11–19:16

The association of winepresses with harvest

REV 14:18–14:19 See also DEU 16:13–16:15

Winepresses in vineyards

ISA 5:1–5:2 See also MAT 21:33–21:34 parallel MRK 12:1

Winepresses in place-names

JDG 7:25 See also ZEC 14:10

title: 4548: wineskin
content:
A container or sack, made from animal skin to hold wine. Jesus Christ used the image of old and new wineskins to explain the differences between himself and John the Baptist.

Wineskins used to store or carry wine

1SA 10:2–10:4 See also 1SA 1:24; PSA 119:83; JER 13:12–13:14; HAB 2:15–2:16

Old and new wineskins

JOB 32:17–32:20 New wine was stored in new wineskins, which expanded with the wine as it fermented. See also JOS 9:3–9:13

2SA 16:1–16:2 See also 1SA 16:14–16:20, 1SA 25:14–25:20

The parable of old and new wineskins

MAT 9:14–9:17 parallels MRK 2:18–2:22, LUK 5:33–5:39

title: 4550: winnowing
content:
The exposing of grain to the air by either tossing or fanning so that the loose outer part is blown away. It is also used figuratively to refer to God's judgment.

Winnowing took place at the threshing-floor

See also RUT 3:2

Winnowing used figuratively

JER 15:7; MAT 3:12 parallel LUK 3:17 See also PRO 20:8, PRO 20:26; ISA 41:16; JER 4:11, JER 51:2

title: 4552: wood
content:
Trunks and branches of trees used as raw material for various objects and as fuel for burning. Acacia wood was used extensively in the tabernacle and olive wood in the temple.

Acacia wood

Cypress wood

EZK 27:3–27:6

Items made from wood

EZK 24:3–24:5 See also PRO 26:20–26:21; ISA 44:13–44:20; EZK 15:1–15:8

DEU 19:5 See also NUM 15:32–15:36; DEU 29:10–29:11; JOS 9:3–9:27; 2KI 6:1–6:7

ZEC 12:6 See also JOB 41:27; JER 5:12–5:14

Symbolic use of wood

EZK 37:15–37:28 See also EXO 15:23–15:26

title: 4554: yeast
content:
A raising agent commonly used in bread. Bread that was required quickly was baked without yeast, as at the first Passover, commemorated annually in the Feast of Unleavened Bread. Although some non-meat offerings contained yeast, it could not be burned on the altar.

Unleavened bread was made without yeast

EXO 12:39 See also GEN 19:3; EXO 12:8–12:11, EXO 12:34; JDG 6:17–6:19; 1SA 28:24–28:25

The consumption of yeast was prohibited during the Feast of Unleavened Bread

EXO 12:14–12:15 See also EXO 12:17–12:20, EXO 13:3, EXO 13:6–13:7, EXO 23:15, EXO 23:18, EXO 34:18; LEV 23:6; NUM 28:17; DEU 16:1–16:4; EZK 45:21

Sacrifices burned on the altar were not to contain yeast

LEV 2:11 See also EXO 29:1–29:3, EXO 29:22–29:24, EXO 34:25; LEV 2:4–2:5, LEV 6:16–6:17, LEV 7:12–7:13, LEV 8:2–8:3, LEV 8:26, LEV 10:12; NUM 6:14–6:15, NUM 6:19

Some offerings contained yeast

LEV 23:17

MAT 13:33 parallel LUK 13:20–13:21

Metaphorical references to yeast as something evil

1CO 5:6–5:8 See also MAT 16:5–16:12 parallel MRK 8:14–8:21; LUK 12:1–12:3; GAL 5:2–5:12

title: 4603: animals
content: A category of living creatures that dwell mainly on the land, distinguished from fishes, birds and insects. Scripture stresses that all animals are created by God and are subordinate to human beings.

title: 4604: animals, nature of
content:
As a class within the created order, their variety reflects God's generous giving. He makes human beings their stewards, though they themselves come into the category of creatures.

Animals in their profusion

GEN 1:24–1:25 See also GEN 7:1–7:3, GEN 7:13–7:16, GEN 8:15–8:19; PSA 50:9–50:11, PSA 148:7–148:10; ISA 43:20; EZK 36:11

Animals as illustrated by their variety

ISA 11:6–11:8 See also EXO 23:12 Ox, donkey; EXO 25:3–25:5 Goat, sea cow; LEV 11:4–11:7 Rabbit, pig; LEV 11:29–11:30 Weasel, rat; NUM 19:2 Heifer; DEU 14:4–14:5 Deer, gazelle, antelope; 1SA 17:34–17:35 Sheep, bear, lion; JOB 40:15–40:24 possibly the hippopotamus; JOB 41:1–41:34 possibly the crocodile; PSA 58:4 Snake; PSA 105:30 Frog; SNG 2:15 Fox; ISA 13:21–13:22 Jackal, wild goat, hyena; ISA 51:8 Worm

Human beings within the animal creation

As part of creation

GEN 2:7; ECC 3:18–3:21 See also PSA 49:12

Significantly different from the rest of creation

GEN 1:26–1:27

Human beings as stewards of the animal kingdom

PSA 8:6–8:8 See also GEN 1:26–1:28, GEN 2:19–2:20, GEN 9:1–9:2; HEB 2:6–2:8; JAS 3:7–3:8

Animals as the objects of divine, and therefore human, care

GEN 9:8–9:10 See also GEN 8:1; EXO 9:4–9:6; 2KI 3:17; JOB 5:23, JOB 38:39–38:41, JOB 39:1–39:30; PSA 104:20–104:21; PRO 12:10, PRO 27:23; JOL 2:22; JON 4:11

Animals and Noah's ark

GEN 7:13–7:23

content:
Central to the OT sacrificial system, they were governed by strict laws within the Mosaic code.

Animals as defined by the Law of Moses

Animals for food

LEV 20:25–20:26 See also EXO 22:31; LEV 22:8; DEU 12:15–12:16, DEU 12:21, DEU 14:3–14:8; EZK 4:14, EZK 44:31; ACT 10:9–10:14, ACT 11:6, ACT 15:19–15:20

MAL 1:8 See also LEV 22:24–22:25; DEU 15:21; MAL 1:13–1:14

Involved in judgment

ZEP 1:3 See also GEN 6:7, GEN 7:20–7:23 the floodEXO 9:22–9:25, EXO 13:15; PSA 135:8 JER 9:10, JER 12:4, JER 50:3; EZK 25:12–25:13; JOL 1:19–1:20; HAB 2:17; ZEC 14:15

As agents of judgment

LEV 26:21–26:22 See also EZK 33:27, EZK 39:4, EZK 39:17–39:18; DAN 4:31–4:32; HOS 2:12

title: 4609: beast, the
content:
In apocalyptic literature, symbol of the ultimate adversary of God in the great final spiritual battle.

Beasts represent rulers and nations opposed to God

DAN 7:3–7:7, DAN 7:11–7:12, DAN 7:17, DAN 7:19–7:23

The beast represents the antichrist in the final spiritual battle

The emergence of the beast

REV 11:7, REV 13:1, REV 13:11, REV 17:3

The power and authority of the beast

REV 13:2–13:7, REV 13:12–13:15

Those who will worship the beast, and those who will not

REV 13:8, REV 13:16–13:18, REV 14:9–14:11, REV 16:2, REV 20:4

The defeat of the beast and the fulfilment of God's purposes

REV 16:10–16:11, REV 17:7–17:14, REV 17:16–17:17, REV 19:19–19:20

resource.label: 4121: Satan, as the enemy of God

title: 4612: birds
content:
Part of the created world, used for food. In OT times they were also used in sacrifices. In Scripture, birds are used in many metaphors.

Part of creation

GEN 1:21 See also GEN 1:20–1:30, GEN 2:20; 1CO 15:39; JAS 3:7

Cared for by God

LUK 12:24 parallel MAT 6:26 See also JOB 38:41; PSA 50:11, PSA 147:9

title: 4615: bull
content:
Bulls were particularly prized as sacrifices on account of their considerable value. The sacrifice of a bull was especially associated with the sin offering.

Bulls offered as sacrifices

EXO 29:36; 2SA 6:12–6:13; ACT 14:13 Also used in pagan sacrifices. See also NUM 23:1–23:4; JOB 42:7–42:8; PSA 66:15; HEB 9:13

Instructions for the offering of a bull

LEV 4:13–4:21 See also LEV 1:3–1:9

Bulls sacrificed on significant occasions

NUM 28:16–28:20 The Passover. See also EXO 24:4–24:5 the covenant confirmed; EXO 29:10–29:14; LEV 9:2, LEV 16:11–16:14 the Day of Atonement; NUM 8:5–8:12, NUM 29:12–29:13; 1SA 1:24; 1CH 15:25–15:26, 1CH 29:21–29:22; 2CH 29:20–29:24; EZR 6:9–6:10, EZR 8:35; EZK 43:19–43:27, EZK 45:18–45:24

Godly sacrifices in contrast to pagan ones

1KI 18:22–18:24 See also JDG 6:25–6:28

Even the sacrifice of bulls invalid without the spiritual conditions being fulfilled

PSA 69:30–69:31; ISA 1:11 See also PSA 50:9, PSA 50:13; ISA 66:3; HOS 12:11

Provisional nature of the OT sin offering of a bull

HEB 10:4

The bull as a symbol of strength

DEU 33:17 See also PSA 22:12, PSA 68:30

Laws concerning bulls

EXO 21:28–21:36

1KI 7:25–7:29 parallel 2CH 4:3–4:4; ; 2KI 16:17; ; JER 52:20

Bulls in the setting of judgment

ISA 34:6–34:7; ; JER 50:27

PSA 106:20

title: 4618: calf
content:
A young animal prized as the source of tender meat, suitable for celebrations. However, it also had associations with both the OT sacrificial system and idolatry.

The association of the calf with idolatry

EXO 32:2–32:8; 1KI 12:28–12:29 See also EXO 32:19–32:24; DEU 9:16–9:21; 2KI 10:28–10:29, 2KI 17:16; 2CH 11:15; NEH 9:16–9:18; PSA 106:19–106:20; HOS 8:5–8:6, HOS 10:5, HOS 13:2; ACT 7:41

The sacrifice of calves

MIC 6:6 See also LEV 9:1–9:4, LEV 9:8–9:11; 2SA 6:12–6:13; 1KI 1:9–1:10; HEB 9:11–9:12, HEB 9:19–9:20

The flesh of the calf as a prized food

PRO 15:17; LUK 15:22–15:23 See also GEN 18:6–18:8; 1SA 14:31–14:32, 1SA 28:24–28:25

The metaphorical use of calves

JER 31:18; MAL 4:2 See also PSA 29:6, PSA 68:30; JER 34:18, JER 46:20–46:21

title: 4621: colt
content:
A young, untrained horse or donkey, associated in Scripture with humility or peace.

A colt is a young donkey

JOB 11:12 See also GEN 49:11

The use of a colt by the coming king

ZEC 9:9 See also MAT 21:2–21:7 parallels MRK 11:2–11:7, LUK 19:30–19:35; JHN 12:15

title: 4624: cow
content:
Cows, in OT times, often symbolised wealth and material well-being.

Cows as evidence of prosperity

JOB 21:10 Part of Job's complaint about the prosperity of the wicked. See also GEN 12:16, GEN 24:35, GEN 32:5, GEN 32:13–32:15, GEN 41:1–41:4, GEN 41:17–41:21, GEN 41:25–41:27; 2SA 12:2; 2CH 32:27–32:28; PSA 50:10 evidence of God's wealth; HAB 3:17–3:18 the believer's joy despite the absence of prosperity

In the history of Israel

1SA 6:7–6:14

Cows in God's new earth

ISA 11:7 See also ISA 30:23, ISA 32:20

Cows as a source of nourishment and fuel

2SA 17:29; ; EZK 4:15

Laws concerning cows

EXO 22:30; ; LEV 22:28

Cows in metaphors

Of stupidity

JOB 18:3

Of godless women

AMO 4:1

title: 4627: creatures
content:
Generally, living beings within God's creation, especially the exotic animals seen in visions, which often have a deep symbolic significance.

Creatures representing angelic beings who worship God

EZK 1:5–1:6; REV 4:6–4:8 The strange creatures of Ezekiel and Revelation seem to stand for the fulness and variety of God's creation. See also EZK 1:7–1:25, EZK 3:12–3:13, EZK 10:15–10:22 The creatures are here identified as cherubim; REV 4:9–4:11, REV 5:6–5:14, REV 6:1–6:3, REV 6:5–6:6, REV 7:11, REV 14:3, REV 15:7, REV 19:4

Creatures representing kings or kingdoms

DAN 7:17, DAN 8:20–8:21 The “beasts” here are unnatural, and represent the hostility of human empires to the rule of God. The horns represent individual kings within an imperial dynasty.

Creatures representing Satan

The beast here represents Satan, with the horns representing royal authority at a time of Roman persecution of the church: REV 12:3–12:4, REV 12:7–12:9, REV 13:1; DAN 7:2–7:8, DAN 7:19–7:26, DAN 8:3–8:12; ; REV 11:7, REV 13:2–13:8, REV 13:11–13:18, REV 17:3, REV 17:7–17:13

content:
Although used by shepherds and occasionally kept as pets, in Bible times most dogs were semi-wild scavengers.

The animal

1SA 17:43, 1SA 24:14; ; 2SA 9:8, 2SA 16:9; ; 2KI 8:13; ; ISA 56:10–56:11; ; PHP 3:2; ; REV 22:15

content:
A beast of burden, used to carry both goods and people.

1SA 25:20; MAT 21:7 See also EXO 4:20NUM 22:22; 2PE 2:16 JOS 15:18; JDG 19:28; 2SA 17:23, 2SA 19:26; 1KI 13:23–13:29; ZEC 9:9; MAT 21:5; LUK 10:34; JHN 12:14

Figurative use of the donkey

GEN 16:12 See also GEN 49:14; 2KI 6:25; ISA 1:3; JER 22:19

Samson and the donkey's jaw-bone

JDG 15:15–15:16

content:
A common species of bird. In Scripture the dove symbolises the Spirit of God, and also certain kinds of human behaviour.

A kind of bird

title: 4639: dung and manure
content:
Human dung is considered unclean

A common animal used both for food and within the sacrificial system.

title: 4654: horn
content:
The hard, pointed growth on the heads of certain animals; a projection resembling such a growth. The horns of animals were regarded as symbols of strength. In apocalyptic writings, horns may denote various powers and enemies of God. Altars were made with horn-like corner projections.

Horns of animals

See also GEN 22:13LEV 11:16; DEU 14:15 PSA 22:21, PSA 69:31

As musical instruments

EXO 19:13; ; JOS 6:4; ; 1CH 15:28; ; 2CH 15:14; ; PSA 81:3, PSA 98:6; ; DAN 3:5, DAN 3:7, DAN 3:10, DAN 3:15; ; HOS 5:8

content:
The animal most commonly associated with war and battle, the horse also appears figuratively in poetic and prophetic literature. Its domesticated usage was mainly for personal travel and the haulage of heavy loads.

A wide variety of insects are mentioned in Scripture. Some are useful in the provision of food, while others are viciously destructive. Their behaviour is used figuratively in the poetic and prophetic literature.

content:
A common source of clothing and meat, this animal was given heightened significance by its place in the Jewish sacrificial system and was used figuratively of Jesus Christ.

content:
A feeding trough in a stable or stall, filled with fodder for donkeys, oxen, etc. The term is also used to refer to the stall in which animals were kept. The newborn Jesus was laid in a manger because there was no room at an inn.

A feeding place for livestock

Recognised by the animals of the household

Provision for farm animals

LUK 2:7 See also LUK 2:12, LUK 2:16

content:
Sheep were a major feature of ancient Israel's rural economy. Although they were of importance as a source of food and wool, they also symbolise helplessness and the need for guidance.

title: 4696: yoke
content:
The wooden collar which joins two animals, usually oxen, to enable them to pull together. Used metaphorically the term may denote the partnership of those who share a yoke or, more usually, the subjection of those under a yoke. As his covenant partners God's people are yoked to him and he will break the oppressive yoke of their enemies.

A disease ruinous to crops. In the OT blight was seen as evidence of God's judgment.

title: 4804: breath
content:
The imagery of breath is often used to convey spiritual essence and power, unseen except in its effects, including aspects of the person and work of the Holy Spirit. The main Hebrew and Greek words translated as “breath” in the NIV are also translated as “spirit” and “wind”.

The breath of life

Life imparted by God

GEN 2:7; ACT 17:25 See also GEN 1:30, GEN 6:17, GEN 7:15, GEN 7:22; JOB 27:3, JOB 33:4; PSA 104:30; ISA 42:5, ISA 57:16; JER 38:16; REV 11:11

Life recalled by his sovereign will

ECC 12:7 See also 1KI 17:17; JOB 34:14–34:15; PSA 104:29; ISA 40:7

The breath of God

An emblem of the Holy Spirit

JHN 20:21–20:22 See also EZK 37:5, EZK 37:14

God's creative breath

PSA 33:6 See also GEN 1:2; JOB 26:13, JOB 33:4

God's enlightening breath

JOB 32:8 See also JHN 14:26, JHN 16:13–16:15; 2TI 3:16–3:17; 2PE 1:20–1:21

God's regenerating breath

EZK 37:4–37:14 See also JHN 3:5–3:8; 1TH 1:4–1:5; TIT 3:5

God's empowering breath

ACT 2:1–2:4 See also ISA 11:2, ISA 61:1; JOL 2:28–2:29; JHN 20:21–20:22; ACT 1:8

God's destroying breath

2TH 2:8 See also EXO 15:10; 2SA 22:16 parallel PSA 18:15; JOB 4:9, JOB 15:30; ISA 11:4, ISA 30:28, ISA 30:33, ISA 40:24, ISA 59:19; EZK 21:31

A natural feature of the sky, often associated with God's judgment, presence and glory.

JOB 37:15–37:16 See also JOB 22:14, JOB 26:9, JOB 36:29, JOB 38:9, JOB 38:37; ECC 11:4

content:
A wide range of colours are mentioned in Scripture, some being used symbolically.

Yellow

REV 9:17 See also LEV 13:30

Green

Red

2KI 3:22; MAT 16:2 See also GEN 25:30; EXO 36:19; REV 12:3

Purple

MRK 15:17 NUM 4:13; ; PRO 31:22; ; DAN 5:7; ; ACT 16:14

Scarlet

Black

SNG 5:11 See also REV 6:12

The whole spectrum

title: 4808: crimson
content:
A deep red colour used interchangeably with scarlet and sometimes with purple. It has associations with blood and richness.

The richness of crimson used for ornamentation

EXO 25:3–25:4, EXO 28:6 It was used extensively in the construction of the tabernacle; 2CH 2:7 See also 2CH 2:14, 2CH 3:14

Crimson associated with splendour and prosperity

2SA 1:24; REV 18:16 See also PRO 31:21; JER 4:30; NAM 2:3; MAT 27:28 parallel MRK 15:17; REV 18:12

Crimson associated with blood and sin

ISA 1:18 See also LEV 14:3–14:6, LEV 14:49–14:53; NUM 19:6; ISA 63:1–63:3; HEB 9:19; REV 17:3–17:4

Everyday use of the colour crimson

GEN 38:28; ; JOS 2:17–2:18; ; SNG 4:3

title: 4809: darkness
content: The absence of light. Often used figuratively as a symbol of sin, chaos or the absence of God.

resource.label: 4811: darkness, as a symbol of sin

title: 4810: darkness, natural
content:
Scripture stresses that God is able to be present and known, even in the darkest aspects of the world.

Darkness as a symbol of the terrifying majesty of God

EXO 20:18–20:21; PSA 97:2 See also DEU 4:11–4:12, DEU 5:22–5:24; HEB 12:18–12:21

God is the source of natural darkness

ISA 45:7 See also GEN 1:2–1:5, GEN 1:16–1:18; JOB 26:10; PSA 74:16, PSA 104:19–104:20

God's eye penetrates the darkness

JOB 34:21–34:22; PSA 139:11–139:12 See also DAN 2:22; AMO 5:8–5:9; 2CO 4:6

Miracles involving natural darkness

MAT 27:45 parallels MRK 15:33, LUK 23:44–23:45 See also EXO 10:21–10:23, EXO 14:19–14:20; JOS 24:6–24:7; PSA 105:26–105:28; ISA 50:3; ACT 2:19–2:20; JOL 2:30–2:32; ACT 13:9–13:12

Natural darkness used poetically

2SA 22:10–22:12 parallel PSA 18:9–18:11; ; JOB 3:1–3:10

title: 4811: darkness, as a symbol of sin
content:
Darkness is often used in Scripture as a symbol of sin and its effects. It is often contrasted with light, as a symbol of forgiveness and the presence of God.

Darkness as a symbol of sin

JHN 3:19–3:20 See also JOB 24:16–24:17; PRO 2:12–2:15, PRO 4:19; ISA 5:20, ISA 29:15; MAT 6:23; LUK 22:53 Jesus Christ arrested; ROM 1:21ROM 13:12; 2CO 6:14; EPH 5:8–5:12 1JN 1:5–1:6

Darkness as a symbol of the effects of sin

Darkness symbolising ignorance of the truth

2CO 4:4 See also PSA 82:5; ISA 8:20; 2CO 3:14–3:15; EPH 4:18

Darkness symbolising the inability to find the right way

JHN 12:35 See also JOB 12:24–12:25; ISA 59:9–59:10; MAT 5:14; 1JN 2:9, 1JN 2:11

Darkness symbolises times of trial

JOB 3:3–3:6, JOB 19:8, JOB 23:16–23:17, JOB 30:26; ; PSA 107:10, PSA 143:3; ; ECC 11:8

Darkness symbolises death and the grave

JOB 10:20–10:22 See also PSA 49:19, PSA 88:10–88:12

God's provision for the darkness of sin

God himself is a remedy for darkness

2SA 22:29 See also PSA 18:28, PSA 30:5, PSA 91:4–91:7; ISA 42:16, ISA 50:10; MIC 7:8

God will deliver from darkness

ISA 9:2 See also MAT 4:16; PSA 107:13–107:14; ISA 29:18, ISA 49:8–49:9; LUK 1:78–1:79; ACT 26:17–26:18; COL 1:13; 1TH 5:4–5:5; 1PE 2:9

JHN 8:12 See also JHN 1:4–1:5, JHN 12:46; 1CO 4:5; 2CO 4:6; GEN 1:3; EPH 5:8; 2PE 1:19; 1JN 2:8

God's people rescue others from darkness

ISA 42:6–42:7 See also ISA 60:1–60:5

content:
Darkness is used figuratively of God's present and final judgment upon individuals, unfaithful Israel and the heathen nations.

Darkness as a symbol of judgment upon ungodly individuals

MAT 25:30 See also 1SA 2:9–2:10; JOB 5:13–5:14, JOB 12:25, JOB 15:22–15:23, JOB 15:30, JOB 18:5–18:6, JOB 18:18, JOB 20:24–20:26, JOB 22:10–22:11 Eliphaz reasons on the assumption that Job's suffering proves his wickedness; PSA 35:5–35:6; ISA 8:22; JER 23:11–23:12; MAT 22:13; 2PE 2:17; JUD 1:13; REV 16:10–16:11

ISA 47:5–47:7; ; EZK 30:18, EZK 32:7–32:8; ; NAM 1:8

title: 4815: drought
content: A severe long-term absence of water or drink of any kind, leading to disaster for the people affected in this way. Scripture provides examples of such droughts, while also indicating the possibility and dangers of long-term spiritual thirst arising through disobedience or lack of faith.

title: 4816: drought, physical
content:
Drought arises through the absence of rain, leading to severe hardship for those who are affected by it.

Drought may be a sign of God's displeasure

DEU 11:17 See also DEU 28:22–28:24; 2SA 1:21; 1KI 17:1; PSA 107:33–107:34; ISA 5:6, ISA 5:13; JER 2:2–2:3, JER 14:1–14:6, JER 50:38; EZK 22:23–22:24; AMO 4:7; HAG 1:11; JAS 5:17

The response of believers to drought

It tests their faith

1KI 17:7; HAB 3:17–3:18 See also DEU 8:15; ISA 41:17, ISA 48:20–48:21; JER 2:5–2:6; HOS 13:5

It gives opportunity to help others

ACT 11:28–11:29 See also GEN 41:56–41:57; 1KI 17:9, 1KI 18:1–18:4

Rain is a sign of God's general favour or of his forgiveness

His general favour

ACT 14:17 See also GEN 27:28; JOB 5:10, JOB 38:25–38:28; PSA 65:9–65:10, PSA 147:8; JER 5:24, JER 14:22; MAT 5:45

His response to repentance

JOL 2:12, JOL 2:23 See also MAL 3:10

His response to obedience

DEU 11:13–11:14 See also LEV 26:3–26:4

His response to prayer

JAS 5:18 See also 1KI 8:35–8:36, 1KI 18:36–18:46; ZEC 10:1

content:
Scripture indicates that believers, both as individuals and communities, may go through periods in which they fail to experience the refreshing and life-giving presence of God. One cause of such spiritual thirst is unbelief. Scripture also indicates ways in which this thirst may be relieved.

Spiritual drought in unbelievers

PSA 68:6 See also ISA 29:7–29:8

The remedy for spiritual drought is to be found in Jesus Christ

JHN 7:37–7:39 See also ISA 45:8, ISA 55:1; JHN 4:13–4:14; TIT 3:5–3:7; REV 22:17

The experience of spiritual drought in believers

PSA 63:1 See also PSA 143:6; EZK 19:10–19:14

The cause of spiritual drought in believers

Unconfessed sin

PSA 32:3–32:4 See also PSA 66:18; ISA 1:28–1:30

Spiritual depression

PSA 42:1–42:2

Self-reliance

JER 2:13 See also JER 17:5–17:6

God's judgment

AMO 8:11

The remedy for spiritual drought in the lives of believers

Through confession

1JN 1:8–1:9 See also PSA 32:5, PSA 51:12; 1JN 3:19–3:22

Through Scripture

PSA 1:2–1:3 See also DEU 32:1–32:2; ISA 55:10–55:11

Through Jesus Christ

JHN 6:35 See also JHN 7:37–7:39; JAS 5:7–5:8

Through the Holy Spirit

ISA 44:3 See also ISA 32:13–32:15; ROM 15:13

Finally in heaven

REV 7:16–7:17

content:
A lack of moisture, often due to lack of rain or a period of drought, leading to vegetation drying up. Dryness is often used as a symbol of hopelessness or of the absence of God's blessing.

Evaporation and recession of floodwaters

GEN 8:13–8:14 See also GEN 8:1–8:11

God's drying up of rivers to allow his people to cross

JOS 4:23 See also EXO 14:10–14:31; JOS 2:10, JOS 3:1–3:17, JOS 5:1; PSA 106:7–106:11; ISA 11:15, ISA 51:10

JOL 1:12 See also ISA 24:7, ISA 33:9; JER 23:10; JOL 1:10, JOL 1:17; ZEC 9:5, ZEC 10:11

content:
The geographical direction often identified by the position of the rising sun. Many of Israel's invaders came from the east, often giving the term a negative association in OT writings.

East as a point of the compass

East and west used to demonstrate vast extent

GEN 28:14; ISA 43:5 See also 1CH 12:15; JOB 23:8; PSA 75:6, PSA 103:12; ZEC 8:7; MAT 8:11, MAT 24:27; LUK 13:29

East identified by the rising sun

NUM 2:3 See also JOS 1:15, JOS 19:12; PSA 50:1, PSA 113:3; ISA 41:25, ISA 45:6

The east wind

JOB 15:2 The east wind blows straight out of the desert; PSA 48:7 See also GEN 41:6; EXO 10:13; ISA 27:8; JER 4:11, JER 18:17; EZK 17:10, EZK 27:26; HOS 13:15; JON 4:8

Military threat from the east

JDG 6:33 See also JDG 10:8; 1CH 5:10; ISA 9:12, ISA 46:11

title: 4822: famine
content: A severe long-term absence of food, leading to disaster for the people affected in this way. Scripture provides examples of such famines, while also indicating the possibility and dangers of spiritual famine arising through disobedience or lack of faith.

title: 4823: famine, physical
content:
A serious shortage of food caused by drought, war or natural disasters. Famine is seen as a sign of God's judgment on Israel.

Examples of famines

In the days of Abraham

GEN 12:10 See also GEN 26:1

In the days of Joseph

GEN 41:53–41:57; ; ACT 7:11

In the days of Ruth

RUT 1:1

In the days of David

2SA 21:1

In the days of Elijah

1KI 18:1–18:5; ; LUK 4:25

In the days of Elisha

2KI 4:38, 2KI 6:25

In the days of the prophets

ISA 51:19; ; JER 44:13; ; EZK 7:15; ; JOL 1:10–1:12

In the days of Claudius

ACT 11:28

In the last days

MAT 24:7 parallels MRK 13:8, LUK 21:11

Natural causes of famine

See also DEU 28:22 crop disease1KI 18:1–18:2; JER 14:1–14:62KI 6:24–6:25, 2KI 25:1–25:3 JOL 1:4 locusts

The disobedience of God's people

DEU 28:15–28:18 See also DEU 28:22–28:24, DEU 28:38–28:40, DEU 28:42, DEU 28:51, DEU 28:53, DEU 32:24; JDG 6:1–6:5; 2SA 21:1, 2SA 24:13 parallel 1CH 21:11–21:12; ISA 51:19–51:20; JER 5:17, JER 11:22, JER 14:11–14:18, JER 18:21, JER 29:17–29:18, JER 32:24; EZK 5:16–5:17, EZK 6:11–6:12, EZK 14:13

GEN 41:28–41:30; ; ISA 14:29–14:30; ; REV 6:8, REV 18:8

LAM 4:9 See also GEN 47:13, GEN 47:20; DEU 28:53; 2KI 7:3–7:4; NEH 5:3; JER 52:6; LUK 15:14–15:16, LUK 23:28–23:29

2KI 7:1–7:9, 2KI 7:16; ; JER 32:36–32:37

Praying

Trusting in the Lord

title: 4824: famine, spiritual
content:
A state of spiritual hunger arising from a failure to experience the presence and joy of God. Scripture describes such experiences, indicates one ultimate cause to be unbelief and points to ways in which this famine can be relieved.

The experience of spiritual famine

God's word is withheld

AMO 8:11–8:12 See also 1SA 3:1; PSA 74:9; LAM 2:9; EZK 7:26; LUK 23:8–23:9

God's Spirit is withheld

PSA 51:11 See also PSA 107:33–107:34

Causes of spiritual famine

Sin

ISA 59:2

Unbelief

ACT 13:46 See also ACT 28:25–28:28

Worldliness

ISA 55:2

False beliefs

ISA 8:19–8:22

Self-satisfaction

REV 3:17 It is possible to be well-fed physically but famished spiritually.

Testing God

See also PSA 78:18–78:19, PSA 78:29–78:31

God testing his servants

JOB 23:3, JOB 23:8–23:10

Consequences of spiritual famine

title: 4833: light
content: The brightness that enables sight in the darkness. Scripture often uses light as a symbol of the saving presence of God in a fallen world, with darkness being used as a symbol of sin or the absence of God.

JOB 30:22; PRO 1:27 See also JOB 9:17; PSA 55:8; PRO 10:25; ISA 25:4, ISA 32:2, ISA 54:11; JER 25:32; EZK 38:9; MAT 7:24–7:27; JAS 1:6

God controls storms

JON 1:4; NAM 1:3; ZEC 10:1 See also PSA 83:15, PSA 107:29; JER 11:16, JER 23:19, JER 30:23

Jesus Christ stills the storm

LUK 8:24 parallels MAT 8:26, MRK 4:39

title: 4852: thunder
content:
Thunder as an aspect of violent weather

JOB 28:26 See also JOB 36:29, JOB 36:33; PSA 93:4; JER 10:13, JER 51:16

Thunder as a sign of the presence of God

EXO 19:16 See also EXO 20:18; 1SA 12:17–12:18; JOB 26:14, JOB 37:4–37:5, JOB 40:9; PSA 18:13, PSA 29:3, PSA 68:33, PSA 81:7; JHN 12:29; REV 4:5, REV 6:1, REV 8:5, REV 10:3–10:4, REV 11:19, REV 14:2, REV 19:6

Thunder as an expression of the judgment of God

1SA 2:10 See also EXO 9:23, EXO 9:28–9:29, EXO 9:33–9:34; 1SA 7:10; 2SA 22:14; PSA 77:17–77:18, PSA 104:7; ISA 29:6, ISA 30:30, ISA 33:3; JER 25:30; JOL 2:11, JOL 3:16; AMO 1:2; REV 16:18

The thunder of horses' hoofs

JDG 5:22 See also REV 9:9

The Sons of Thunder

MRK 3:17

title: 4853: weather
content: Patterns of climatic conditions, such as wind and rain, which were of major importance to the rural economies of the Near East. Scripture stresses the sovereignty of God over the weather and sees it as a possible means of judgment or reward.

title: 4854: weather, God's sovereignty over
content:
The weather is under the control of God, who alone is able to direct it.

The weather is under God's control

JOB 28:25–28:27; MAT 8:26–8:27 parallels MRK 4:39–4:41, LUK 8:24–8:25 See also JOB 5:10, JOB 26:8–26:9, JOB 26:13, JOB 37:1–37:18; PSA 135:7, PSA 147:8, PSA 147:16–147:18, PSA 148:8; JER 10:13

God alone understands the mysteries of the weather

PRO 3:19–3:20; JHN 3:8 See also JOB 36:26–36:33, JOB 38:22–38:30, JOB 38:34–38:35, JOB 38:37; ECC 11:3–11:5

Israel's economy was dependent on the weather

LEV 26:4 See also DEU 11:10–11:15, DEU 28:12; JER 5:24; HOS 6:3

The weather demonstrates God's goodness and mercy

GEN 8:22; MAT 5:45; ACT 14:17 See also GEN 9:14–9:16; DEU 33:13–33:14; PSA 68:7–68:9; ISA 30:23, ISA 49:10; JOL 2:23; ZEC 10:1

The weather as a demonstration of God's direct intervention

JON 1:4 See also EXO 14:21; NUM 11:31; PSA 78:26; JON 4:8

The weather as an answer to prayer

JAS 5:17–5:18 See also 1SA 12:17–12:18; 1KI 8:35–8:36 parallel 2CH 6:26–6:27; 1KI 18:41–18:44

The weather can cause suffering

GEN 31:40; ACT 28:2 See also EZR 10:9; JOB 1:16, JOB 1:18–1:19; MAT 20:12; JHN 18:18; ACT 27:15

title: 4855: weather, as God's judgment
content:
The weather can be seen as an expression of God's judgment and grace, as well as providing insights concerning human nature.

The weather as an expression of God's judgment

Judgment in battle

JOS 10:11 See also 2SA 22:10–22:15; ISA 30:30; EZK 38:22

Judgment in general

1KI 17:1; HAG 1:10–1:11 See also GEN 7:11–7:12; EXO 9:22–9:26; LEV 26:18–26:19; DEU 11:17, DEU 28:22–28:24; JER 3:3, JER 14:1–14:6; EZK 22:24; AMO 4:7; ZEC 14:17–14:19

The weather as a source of spiritual insight

About the shortness of life

JAS 4:14 See also JOB 7:9, JOB 24:19, JOB 30:15; HOS 13:3; JAS 1:11

About empty profession

HOS 6:4 See also JOB 6:15–6:17; PRO 25:14; 2PE 2:17

About God's judgment

JER 23:19 See also JER 4:11; HOS 8:7

About God's salvation

ISA 55:10–55:11 See also ISA 44:22, ISA 45:8

About righteous rule

2SA 23:3–23:4 See also PSA 72:6; PRO 16:15; ISA 25:4–25:5

About wholesome teaching

DEU 32:2 See also JOB 29:23

About the troubles of life

MAT 7:24–7:27

About the signs of the times

MAT 16:2–16:3 See also LUK 12:54–12:56

title: 4857: west
content:
The geographical direction often identified by the position of the setting sun.

West as a point of the compass

EXO 10:19; LUK 12:54 See also GEN 13:14; EXO 27:12; NUM 34:6; DEU 3:27; JOS 5:1; 2SA 13:34; 1KI 4:24, 1KI 7:25; JOB 18:20; ISA 24:14, ISA 49:12, ISA 59:19; HOS 11:10; REV 21:13

West and east used to demonstrate vast extent

PSA 103:12; MAT 24:27 See also JOB 23:8; PSA 75:6; ISA 43:5; ZEC 8:7, ZEC 14:4; MAT 8:11

West identified by the setting sun

DEU 11:30; PSA 113:3 See also PSA 50:1; ISA 45:6; MAL 1:11

title: 4858: whirlwind
content:
A violent wind, often associated with the presence and activity of God.

The presence of God in whirlwinds

In replying to Job

JOB 38:1

In the forces of nature

PSA 77:18

In taking Elijah up to heaven

2KI 2:11 See also 2KI 2:1; NAM 1:3

Whirlwinds as symbols of disaster or divine judgment

ISA 40:24; JER 23:19 See also PRO 1:27; ISA 5:28, ISA 21:1, ISA 66:15; JER 4:13; HOS 4:19, HOS 8:7; ZEC 7:14

title: 4859: white
content:
A symbol of purity and regeneration reflecting godliness, and also a symbol of victory.

White used symbolically

Of purity and holiness

MAT 17:1–17:2 parallel MRK 9:2–9:3 See also DAN 7:9; MAT 28:2–28:3 parallel MRK 16:5; JHN 20:12; ACT 1:10; REV 1:12–1:14, REV 4:4, REV 6:1–6:2, REV 19:11–19:14, REV 20:11

Of forgiveness and regeneration

ISA 1:18 See also PSA 51:7; REV 3:4–3:5, REV 3:18, REV 6:11, REV 7:9, REV 7:13–7:14

Of superficial righteousness

MAT 23:27 See also EZK 13:10–13:15, EZK 22:28; ACT 23:3

Of joy

ECC 9:7–9:8

White used descriptively

EST 1:6; LAM 4:7 See also GEN 30:37, GEN 49:12; EXO 16:31 the manna; LEV 11:18LEV 13:3–13:4, LEV 13:38–13:39; 2KI 5:27 JDG 5:10–5:11; EST 8:15; JOB 6:6, JOB 41:32; JOL 1:7ZEC 1:8, ZEC 6:1–6:6 MAT 5:36; REV 2:17

title: 4860: wind
content:
In Hebrew the same word can denote “breath”, “wind” or “spirit”. Scripture describes the powerful effect of wind, and uses the image figuratively to speak of God's power and presence in the world.

The origins of the wind

EXO 14:21–14:22; PSA 78:26; AMO 4:13 See also GEN 8:1 A wind caused the flood waters to recede; EXO 10:13–10:14 An east wind brought the plague of locusts on Egypt; EXO 10:19; NUM 11:31 A wind brought quail to feed the Israelites; JOB 28:25; PSA 107:25, PSA 135:7, PSA 148:7–148:8; ISA 11:15; JER 10:13; EZK 13:13; JON 1:4, JON 4:8

The effects of the wind

1KI 19:11; EZK 27:26; ACT 27:13–27:15 See also GEN 41:5–41:6; JOB 1:18–1:19, JOB 37:9, JOB 37:17–37:18; DAN 7:2; MAT 14:24; LUK 12:55; JHN 6:16–6:18; JAS 3:4

Wind used figuratively

As an image of the Holy Spirit

JHN 3:8 The Greek word for “Spirit” is the same as that for “wind”, allowing Jesus Christ to play on the double meaning of the word.

As an image of spiritual instability

JAS 1:6

As an image of destruction

PSA 103:15–103:16; MAT 7:24–7:27 See also JOB 21:17–21:18; PSA 1:4, PSA 48:7; ISA 17:13, ISA 32:2; JER 4:11–4:12, JER 22:22; EZK 19:12; DAN 2:34–2:35; HOS 8:7; AMO 1:14; HAB 1:9; EPH 4:14; JUD 1:12; REV 6:12–6:13

As an image of something insubstantial

ECC 1:14 See also JOB 6:26; PRO 11:29, PRO 27:15–27:16; ISA 26:18; JER 5:13; HOS 12:1

title: 4900: Time
content:
• 4903: time
  • 4906: abolition

  • 4966: present, the

  • 4975: week
